cli/cmd/command_factory.go:443:func (f *CommandFactory) registerSubcommands(rootCmd *cobra.Command) {

package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"os"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/cmd"
	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/git"
)

var version = "dev"
var commit = "unknown"
var date = "unknown"

func main() {
	// Set version info for cobra
	cmd.Version = version
	cmd.Commit = commit
	cmd.Date = date

	// Get working directory once at startup, then resolve to the git
	// repository root so that ddx always operates from the project root
	// regardless of which subdirectory the user invokes it from.
	cwd, err := os.Getwd()
	if err != nil {
		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Error getting working directory: %v\n", err)
		os.Exit(1)
	}
	workingDir := git.FindProjectRoot(cwd)

	if err := cmd.Execute(workingDir); err != nil {
		// Check if it's an ExitError with a specific exit code
		if exitErr, ok := err.(*cmd.ExitError); ok {
			// Print error message only if it's not empty
			if exitErr.Message != "" {
				fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Error: %v\n", exitErr.Message)
			}
			os.Exit(exitErr.Code)
		}
		// For other errors, print them
		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Error: %v\n", err)
		os.Exit(1)
	}
}

// newInitCommand creates a fresh init command
func (f *CommandFactory) newInitCommand() *cobra.Command {
	cmd := &cobra.Command{
		Use:   "init",
		Short: "Initialize DDx in current project",
		Long: `Initialize DDx in the current project.

This command:
• Creates a .ddx/config.yaml configuration file
• Installs the default DDx library plugin
• Commits the config file to git

Examples:
  ddx init                  # Initialize DDx in current project
  ddx init --force          # Reinitialize existing project
  ddx init --no-git         # Skip git operations`,
		Args: cobra.NoArgs,
		RunE: f.runInit,
	}

	cmd.Flags().BoolP("force", "f", false, "Force initialization even if DDx already exists")
	cmd.Flags().Bool("no-git", false, "Skip git operations")
	cmd.Flags().Bool("silent", false, "Suppress all output except errors")
	cmd.Flags().Bool("skip-claude-injection", false, "Skip injecting meta-prompts into CLAUDE.md")
	cmd.Flags().String("repository", "", "Library repository URL (default: https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx-library)")
	cmd.Flags().String("branch", "", "Library repository branch (default: main)")

	return cmd
}

• Configs - Tool configurations

Examples:
  ddx list              # List all resources

		RunE: f.runList,

	cmd.Flags().BoolP("detailed", "d", false, "Show detailed information")
	cmd.Flags().StringP("filter", "f", "", "Filter resources by name")
	cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "Output results as JSON")
	cmd.Flags().Bool("tree", false, "Display resources in tree format")

	return cmd
}

data.text: I’ve got the shape now: the persona APIs are real runtime code, but the deadcode tool is almost certainly not seeing them through the cobra argument dispatch. Before I pick a fix, I’m checking how the repo has handled similar reachability backfills so I can match the existing pattern instead of inventing one.

data.output:
# Bead Authoring Template

> A bead's description + AC must be sufficient context for a competent
> sub-agent to execute it without hand-curation.
> — Reliability principle **P7** (`docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md`)

This document is the canonical template + rubric for creating beads in
this repository. It exists because the 2026-05-03 bead-quality audit
(`.ddx/executions/20260503T155638-bead57f0cb9e/bead-quality-audit-2026-05-03.md`)
found that only **1 of 20** representative open beads scored 8/8 on
the sufficient-sub-agent-prompt rubric. The most common failure modes
were: no specific test names in AC (80 % miss), no `go test` +
lefthook gate in AC (80 % miss), and no root cause with file:line in
description (65 % miss).

---

## 1. The 8-criterion rubric

Every bead must satisfy:

| # | Criterion | Verifier |
|---|-----------|----------|
| (a) | **Title**: one-line scope clarity, imperative, names subsystem + change | "Pagination off-by-one in bead list endpoint" not "Pagination bug" |
| (b) | **Description**: problem + ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE + proposed fix + non-scope | Root cause cites `cli/path/file.go:42-50` |
| (c) | **AC**: numbered, verifiable, includes specific test names | At least one AC names `TestFoo` or a `go test -run` filter |
| (d) | **AC** includes "wired-in" assertion when introducing new code paths | New function → AC asserts a caller invokes it (production or test) |
| (e) | **AC** includes `go test` command + lefthook gate | Both `cd cli && go test ./...` and `lefthook run pre-commit` named |
| (f) | **Labels**: phase + area + kind + cross-refs | `phase:N`, `area:*`, `kind:*`, plus adr/spec/prevention if applicable |
| (g) | **Parent + Deps**: explicit | Parent set; deps either listed or "no deps" stated |
| (h) | Description reads as **sufficient sub-agent prompt** | A competent agent given only the bead body can pick a file and run tests without asking |

Score 8/8 = execution-ready. Less = retrofit before dispatch.

---

## 2. Template fields

```text
TITLE
  <area>: <imperative one-line scope>
  e.g. "agent: unify ExecuteLoopSpec across cobra/HTTP/server layers"

TYPE
  task | bug | epic | chore

LABELS
  phase:<N>, area:<subsystem>, kind:<category>
  + cross-refs: prevention, adr:<id>, spec:<id>, story:<n>, refactor

PARENT
  <ddx-id> of governing epic / story / spec
  (omit only if this is a root)

DEPS
  - <ddx-id-1>: <one-line why this dep matters>
  - <ddx-id-2>: <one-line why this dep matters>
  (or explicit "No deps.")

SPEC-ID (when applicable)
  --set spec-id=FEAT-NNN  | SD-NNN | TD-NNN | ADR-NNN

DESCRIPTION
  PROBLEM
    <one-paragraph statement of what is broken or missing>

  ROOT CAUSE
    <function/file:line where the defect lives>
    <what the code does today vs what it should do>
    For new features: ROOT CAUSE -> "no existing implementation"
    is OK; replace with CURRENT STATE describing the gap.

  PROPOSED FIX
    <bulleted concrete changes; cite file:line for each>
    - cli/internal/foo/bar.go:120-145  rename Baz -> Quux
    - cli/internal/foo/bar_test.go      add TestQuux_RoundTrip

  NON-SCOPE
    - <thing readers might assume is included but isn't>
    - <thing covered by sister bead ddx-NNNN>

  INTERSECTIONS (optional)
    - <ddx-id>: <how this bead relates to that one>

ACCEPTANCE
  1. <verifiable assertion 1 — names a Test, file path, command, or
     observable artifact>
  2. <verifiable assertion 2>
  3. ...
  N-1. cd cli && go test ./<paths>/... green.
  N. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
```

---

## 3. Examples

### 3.1 Example of a strong bead (audit score 8/8)

`ddx-1e516bc9` — *"fizeau Execute: explicit Harness + empty Model + non-empty Profile/MinPower must route within harness's eligible models"*

Why it scored 8/8:

- **Title (a)** names subsystem (`fizeau Execute`), the precondition (`explicit Harness + empty Model`), and the expected behavior (`route within harness's eligible models`).
- **Description (b)** opens with **OBSERVED** (one paragraph, file:line) → **SCOPE — UPSTREAM (fizeau) ONLY** → **INVESTIGATION FIRST** (with explicit close-as-no-op outcome) → **UPSTREAM CHANGE** (file:line into `service_execute.go:193-224`, `models.yaml:31-60`, `registry.go`) → explicit **NOT IN SCOPE** list naming five excluded paths.
- **AC (c)** names two concrete tests:
  `TestExecute_ExplicitHarnessEmptyModelWithProfile_RoutesWithinHarness` and
  `TestExecute_ExplicitHarnessEmptyModelNoProfile_FailsClearly`.
- **Wired-in (d)**: AC #6 asserts the resolved model surfaces in
  `.ddx/workers/<id>/spec.json`, `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/result.json`, and the
  workers UI — three distinct call-graph endpoints.
- **Test command + lefthook (e)**: AC #9 — `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... green; lefthook pre-commit passes.`
- **Labels (f)**: `phase:2, area:agent, kind:fix, upstream-fizeau, observed-failure`.
- **Parent + Deps (g)**: parent `ddx-e34994e2`; dep `ddx-cfedee8e` with explanation that the ladder must supply MinPower for fizeau to act on.
- **Sufficient (h)**: a sub-agent given only the bead body knows which file in fizeau to read, what the gap looks like, what the negative-test outcome should be, and how to verify the end-to-end fix without asking.

### 3.2 Example of a weak bead (audit score 1/8) — annotated

`ddx-0793ac75` — *"federation: federated GraphQL resolvers + routing-metadata fields"*

Verbatim description:
```
GraphQL resolvers that aggregate across spokes (federatedWorkers,
federatedRuns, federatedDocuments, etc.). Add routing-metadata fields
exposing which spoke owns each result. Write-routing contract: hub
forwards mutations to owning node, never broadcasts (Story 15
dependency).
```

Verbatim AC:
```
1. federated* resolvers in schema.
2. Routing-metadata visible per result.
3. Mutations route to owner; tested.
4. Tests cover happy + degraded paths.
```

Failures, criterion by criterion:

- **(b) FAIL** — no problem statement (what's broken today?), no root cause, no file:line, no non-scope. The reader cannot tell where the schema lives, where resolvers go, or what "routing-metadata" should look like as a Go type.
- **(c) FAIL** — "tested" and "Tests cover happy + degraded paths" name no `Test*` function. Sub-agent has no way to know what to write or where.
- **(d) PARTIAL** — "tested" implies the resolvers are wired, but no production-call-graph assertion (e.g., "schema.graphql Mutation block exposes field X").
- **(e) FAIL** — no `go test` command, no lefthook gate.
- **(h) FAIL** — a sub-agent reading this cold cannot answer: which schema file? which resolver package? what fields on routing-metadata? what's the wire format? Hand-curation by the operator is the only path forward.

**Retrofit shape** (illustrative — not an actual edit):
```
PROBLEM
  Story 14 needs aggregate-across-spokes resolvers. Today the GraphQL
  schema (cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql) exposes only
  per-node Worker/Run/Document queries. Federation requires
  federated* counterparts that fan out to spokes and stitch results.

ROOT CAUSE
  No federated resolver layer exists. cli/internal/server/graphql_resolver.go
  contains only single-node resolvers. Spoke fan-out is in
  cli/internal/server/federation_spoke.go but nothing in the GraphQL
  layer calls it.

PROPOSED FIX
  - schema.graphql: add Federated{Worker,Run,Document} types with
    `ownerSpoke: String!` field.
  - graphql_resolver.go: add federatedWorkers/federatedRuns/
    federatedDocuments resolvers calling federation_spoke.FanOut.
  - graphql_mutation.go: write-routing helper routes mutations to
    owning node via federation_spoke.RouteToOwner.

NON-SCOPE
  - New non-federated resolvers (out: covered by Story 13).
  - Spoke discovery (out: covered by ddx-fe63969f).

ACCEPTANCE
  1. schema.graphql exposes federatedWorkers/federatedRuns/
     federatedDocuments queries with ownerSpoke metadata.
  2. TestFederatedWorkers_FanOutAggregates verifies fan-out + stitch.
  3. TestFederatedMutation_RoutesToOwner verifies write-routing.
  4. TestFederatedWorkers_DegradedSpoke returns partial results +
     warning.
  5. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... green.
  6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
```

---

## 4. Authoring checklist (paste into your editor before running `ddx bead create`)

```text
[ ] (a) Title: imperative, one line, names subsystem + change
[ ] (b) Description has all four sub-elements:
        [ ] PROBLEM (one paragraph)
        [ ] ROOT CAUSE with file:line citation (or CURRENT STATE for new features)
        [ ] PROPOSED FIX (bulleted, file:line per item)
        [ ] NON-SCOPE
[ ] (c) Each AC line is verifiable; at least one names Test* or `go test -run`
[ ] (d) New code path → AC asserts a production or test caller invokes it
[ ] (e) AC includes:
        [ ] cd cli && go test ./<paths>/... green
        [ ] lefthook run pre-commit passes
[ ] (f) Labels: phase:<N>, area:<subsystem>, kind:<category>, +cross-refs
[ ] (g) Parent set; deps listed or "no deps" stated
[ ] (h) Self-test: re-read the bead from cold. Could a competent
        sub-agent execute it without asking a question? If no, retrofit.
[ ] No /tmp/ paths cited as load-bearing context. Inline the relevant
    excerpt instead — /tmp does not survive between machines.
```

---

## 5. Anti-patterns surfaced by the audit

- **`/tmp/` references as load-bearing context.** 5 of 20 audited beads cite a `/tmp/...` plan file. These are functionally broken links to a sub-agent on a different machine. **Inline the excerpt.**
- **"See parent" without inline summary.** Refactor children of `ddx-5cb6e6cd` (C4/C7/C11/C13) systematically defer detail to the parent epic. Each child fails the stand-alone test. **Inline the parent's relevant 5-10 lines.**
- **Vague test phrases.** "Tests cover X." "All execute-bead tests still pass." Both omit the actual `Test*` name or `-run` filter the executor needs.
- **Story-doc copy-paste.** One-paragraph beads (`ddx-0793ac75`, `ddx-0e5c3005`, `ddx-1d52a30c`, `ddx-0ca4ffda`) are story excerpts that were never expanded for execution. Story documents are *upstream* of beads, not the bead itself.
- **Implicit lefthook.** Lefthook runs in pre-commit hooks anyway, but if AC doesn't name it, executors omit it from their own verification before committing.

---

## 6. When to skip a criterion


---
# Reliability Principles

See this document for the 7 reliability principles applied to ddx try / ddx work execution.

These principles describe how the execution machinery should behave when a layer rejects a candidate, encounters transient failure, or needs to surface degraded state to operators.

## P1: Fail-Open At Every Machinery Layer

When a layer's specific check rejects a candidate, the layer skips itself and emits a structured event. It does not wedge the pipeline.

The auto-routing fix (`workers.go:803`, commit `3b4f5d58`) is the canonical example: preflight is now advisory when no operator pin exists.

## P2: Single Responsibility Per Layer

Each layer rejects only on conditions it owns. Routing pre-flight does NOT decide provider availability (`fizeau` owns); cooldown does NOT decide eligibility (`picker` owns); etc.

Cross-layer concerns are the smell.

## P3: Observable Degradation

Every fail-open emits a structured event surfaced in the workers panel. Operators see "preflight skipped (no operator pin)" instead of silent acceptance OR endless retry loop.

## P4: Bounded Blast Radius

A failure on bead X must not affect bead Y. The stay-alive fix at commit `41cb762e` established this for preflight rejections (per-bead continue, not loop exit). Extend it to all layers.

## P5: Operator-Visible State

Worker reports current state (`idle`, `claiming`, `executing`, `reviewing`, `blocked-on-X`) at all times. No "8 hours running, 0 attempts" mystery state.

ADR-022 rev 5 `§Probe + freshness state model` defines the worker side; the UI workers panel surfaces it.

## P6: Auto-Retry Only For Transient Classes

Cooldown fires ONLY when the model genuinely couldn't make progress (clean no-changes with rationale). Disrupted, preflight-rejected, network-error, claim-race -> no cooldown, return to ready.

Existing code: `shouldSuppressNoProgress` at `execute_bead_loop.go:1545` already respects `Disrupted` (commit `47d8054e`).

## P7: Bead = Prompt

A bead's description + AC must be sufficient context for a competent sub-agent to execute it without hand-curation. Investigation done, file:line citations included, concrete test names specified, explicit non-scope marked.

If a sub-agent succeeds where the bead's auto-prompt failed, the BEAD failed (not the executor). Bead-authoring template enforces this; bead-quality audit (forthcoming bead) retrofits existing beads.

.ddx/attachments/ddx-ee2cd434/events.jsonl:12:{"actor":"ddx","body":"no_changes_rationale — bead ddx-ee2cd434 (SD-024 Stage 2)

(a) WHAT IS DONE
----------------
The previous attempt (commit 5ae02964) migrated the high-value `r.Run(RunOptions{...})`
and `RunViaService(..., RunOptions{...})` test sites onto the SD-024 successor API
(`r.RunWithConfig` + `RunWithConfigViaService` + `AgentRunRuntime`) across:

  - cli/internal/agent/agent_test.go    (~22 sites: TestRunWith*, TestSessionLogging,
                                         TestRunPromptFile, TestRunStdinMode,
                                         TestRunModel*, TestRunNonZeroExit,
                                         TestRunUnknownHarness, TestRunEmptyPrompt,
                                         TestIntegration_CodexEcho, TestRunOpencode*,
                                         and the *Permissions* pair)
  - cli/internal/agent/runner_test.go   (lmstudio / openrouter dispatch regressions)
  - cli/internal/agent/script_test.go   (runScript helper now builds rcfg via
                                         TestRunConfigOpts and dispatches via
                                         RunWithConfigViaService)

Those 26 sites are the bulk of the "test sites that exercise the high-level agent
run path" called out in the bead description.

(b) WHAT IS BLOCKING
--------------------
The bead AC reads:

    rg 'RunOptions\{|CompareOptions\{|QuorumOptions\{' cli/ returns 0 matches outside
    the type definitions about to be retired.

This is a *global* gate. It cannot be satisfied by test-only changes — it requires
retiring every remaining `RunOptions{...}` construction in both production and test
code. The remaining sites fall into two classes:

CLASS A — tests that call functions whose signature *takes* RunOptions, so the
RunOptions construction is not a "test site" the test could rewrite without
changing the function under test:

  - cli/internal/agent/agent_test.go        BuildArgs(h, RunOptions{...}, model)  — 14 sites
                                            BuildArgs is the function under test.
  - cli/internal/agent/prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go
        line 78  resolvePrompt(RunOptions{PromptFile})              — exercises r.resolvePrompt
        line 85  defaultResolvePromptForCompare(RunOptions{...})    — exercises pkg-level helper
        line 112 RunViaServiceWith(ctx, svc, dir, RunOptions{...})  — exercises stub-injection helper
  - cli/internal/agent/session_index_test.go
        line 195 SessionIndexEntryFromResult(root, RunOptions{...}, ...)
        line 278 RunViaServiceWith(ctx, svc, dir, RunOptions{...})
  - cli/internal/agent/models_test.go
        line 45  BenchmarkArmsToCompare(arms, RunOptions{...})
  - cli/internal/agent/virtual_test.go
        lines 164/174/214 runVirtualFn(runner, RunOptions{...})
  - cli/internal/agent/claude_stream_test.go
        lines 206/212/218/224/291/324  internal opts: RunOptions{...} fixtures

Migrating any of these test sites requires first changing the *signature* of the
production function under test (BuildArgs, resolvePrompt, defaultResolvePromptForCompare,
RunViaServiceWith, runVirtualFn, BenchmarkArmsToCompare, SessionIndexEntryFromResult,
plus the internal claude_stream helpers). That is production-code refactor work,
explicitly outside the scope the bead description names.

CLASS B — production callers still constructing RunOptions{...}:

  - cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go              :1462
  - cli/internal/server/server.go     :1913, :3786
  - cli/internal/exec/store.go        :201
  - cli/internal/agent/runner.go      :248, :281, :300, :321, :976
  - cli/internal/agent/service_run.go :82  (RunWithConfigViaService bridge — by design)
  - cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go :61, :261, :414, :685
  - cli/internal/agent/grade.go       :169
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go :706
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go :531

Until these are migrated (and in many cases until the function signatures they
flow through are migrated), the AC's grep gate cannot return zero matches.

The fresh-eyes review on the prior attempt itself concluded:

    Path forward: simplest fix is splitting follow-up beads for
    (a) prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go,
    (b) remaining *_test.go RunOptions sites,
    (c) production-code migration,
    then closing this bead with a narrower AC.

That assessment is correct. The bead title ("5-10 sites") and description ("migrate
test sites … in agent_test.go and any sibling test using RunOptions") describe a
test-scoped refactor; the AC describes a global retirement. The two are not
compatible inside one bead.

(c) WHAT A FOLLOW-UP ATTEMPT WOULD NEED
---------------------------------------
Either:

  Option 1 (preferred): re-cut this bead with a narrowed AC, e.g.

      "rg 'RunOptions\{' cli/internal/agent/{agent,runner,script}_test.go returns
      0 matches in tests that exercise r.Run / RunViaService."

      That AC matches the test-only scope, and the prior commit already satisfies it.

  Option 2: expand bead scope to cover the production migration as well. That is
      a multi-file Stage-2 refactor touching cmd/, internal/server/, internal/exec/,
      and most of internal/agent/. It also requires migrating the signatures of
      BuildArgs, resolvePrompt, defaultResolvePromptForCompare, RunViaServiceWith,
      runVirtualFn, BenchmarkArmsToCompare, and SessionIndexEntryFromResult so the
      test sites in Class A can shed RunOptions. This belongs in its own bead (or
      epic), not in a "5-10 sites" test-migration bead.

No new code is committed in this attempt because:

  1. The work the bead description actually scopes is already done (5ae02964).
  2. Doing test-only edits that don't move the AC gate would be exploratory churn.
  3. Doing the full production migration here would silently expand the bead scope
     past what the description and title authorize.

  - cli/internal/agent/script_test.go   (runScript helper now builds rcfg via

                                         RunWithConfigViaService)

Those 26 sites are the bulk of the "test sites that exercise the high-level agent
run path" called out in the bead description.

(b) WHAT IS BLOCKING
--------------------
The bead AC reads:

    rg 'RunOptions\{|CompareOptions\{|QuorumOptions\{' cli/ returns 0 matches outside
    the type definitions about to be retired.

This is a *global* gate. It cannot be satisfied by test-only changes — it requires
retiring every remaining `RunOptions{...}` construction in both production and test
code. The remaining sites fall into two classes:

CLASS A — tests that call functions whose signature *takes* RunOptions, so the
RunOptions construction is not a "test site" the test could rewrite without
changing the function under test:

  - cli/internal/agent/agent_test.go        BuildArgs(h, RunOptions{...}, model)  — 14 sites
                                            BuildArgs is the function under test.
  - cli/internal/agent/prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go
        line 78  resolvePrompt(RunOptions{PromptFile})              — exercises r.resolvePrompt
        line 85  defaultResolvePromptForCompare(RunOptions{...})    — exercises pkg-level helper
        line 112 RunViaServiceWith(ctx, svc, dir, RunOptions{...})  — exercises stub-injection helper
  - cli/internal/agent/session_index_test.go
        line 195 SessionIndexEntryFromResult(root, RunOptions{...}, ...)
        line 278 RunViaServiceWith(ctx, svc, dir, RunOptions{...})
  - cli/internal/agent/models_test.go
        line 45  BenchmarkArmsToCompare(arms, RunOptions{...})
  - cli/internal/agent/virtual_test.go
        lines 164/174/214 runVirtualFn(runner, RunOptions{...})
  - cli/internal/agent/claude_stream_test.go
        lines 206/212/218/224/291/324  internal opts: RunOptions{...} fixtures

Migrating any of these test sites requires first changing the *signature* of the
production function under test (BuildArgs, resolvePrompt, defaultResolvePromptForCompare,
RunViaServiceWith, runVirtualFn, BenchmarkArmsToCompare, SessionIndexEntryFromResult,
plus the internal claude_stream helpers). That is production-code refactor work,
explicitly outside the scope the bead description names.

CLASS B — production callers still constructing RunOptions{...}:

  - cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go              :1462
  - cli/internal/server/server.go     :1913, :3786
  - cli/internal/exec/store.go        :201
  - cli/internal/agent/runner.go      :248, :281, :300, :321, :976
  - cli/internal/agent/service_run.go :82  (RunWithConfigViaService bridge — by design)
  - cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go :61, :261, :414, :685
  - cli/internal/agent/grade.go       :169
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go :706
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go :531

Until these are migrated (and in many cases until the function signatures they
flow through are migrated), the AC's grep gate cannot return zero matches.

The fresh-eyes review on the prior attempt itself concluded:

    Path forward: simplest fix is splitting follow-up beads for
    (a) prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go,
    (b) remaining *_test.go RunOptions sites,
    (c) production-code migration,
    then closing this bead with a narrower AC.

That assessment is correct. The bead title ("5-10 sites") and description ("migrate
test sites … in agent_test.go and any sibling test using RunOptions") describe a
test-scoped refactor; the AC describes a global retirement. The two are not
compatible inside one bead.

(c) WHAT A FOLLOW-UP ATTEMPT WOULD NEED
---------------------------------------
Either:

  Option 1 (preferred): re-cut this bead with a narrowed AC, e.g.

      "rg 'RunOptions\{' cli/internal/agent/{agent,runner,script}_test.go returns
      0 matches in tests that exercise r.Run / RunViaService."

      That AC matches the test-only scope, and the prior commit already satisfies it.

  Option 2: expand bead scope to cover the production migration as well. That is
      a multi-file Stage-2 refactor touching cmd/, internal/server/, internal/exec/,
      and most of internal/agent/. It also requires migrating the signatures of
      BuildArgs, resolvePrompt, defaultResolvePromptForCompare, RunViaServiceWith,
      runVirtualFn, BenchmarkArmsToCompare, and SessionIndexEntryFromResult so the
      test sites in Class A can shed RunOptions. This belongs in its own bead (or
      epic), not in a "5-10 sites" test-migration bead.

No new code is committed in this attempt because:

  1. The work the bead description actually scopes is already done (5ae02964).
  2. Doing test-only edits that don't move the AC gate would be exploratory churn.
  3. Doing the full production migration here would silently expand the bead scope
     past what the description and title authorize.

This bead closes as 'investigation complete; see child beads for fixes'. ddx-b808df39 references this bead's findings in its implementation notes.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-18T04:31:48Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1833233","closing_commit_sha":"1d0b3e373a618623062909694332536c74f2630a","created_at":"2026-04-18T03:01:36.338860593Z","description":"## Context

ddx-b808df39 (P0: ddx agent stop/kill command for hung workers) treats hung-worker remediation as an operational need, but does NOT investigate the UNDERLYING cause of the hangs. The feature is a useful escape hatch, but shipping it without understanding WHY workers hang means we'll keep needing the escape hatch.

Opus fresh-eyes review (2026-04-18) flagged: 'Hung workers for 33h–142h are an operational defect independent of root cause' — the feature bead is justified, but a SIBLING root-cause investigation is missing from the portfolio.

## Observed

The referenced 33h and 142h hang durations in ddx-b808df39 imply multiple incidents where an execute-bead worker process remained in-progress past any reasonable upper bound for a single bead. Needs to investigate:

1. Is the worker process alive but stuck in a tool call (bash subprocess that never returns)?
2. Is it alive but mid-LLM-call with no timeout (provider hang + no client-side deadline)?
3. Is it dead but the bead's claimed_at never cleared (tracker-side leak)?
4. Is the worktree locked or fsync'd in a way that prevents cleanup?

## Fix direction

This bead is an INVESTIGATION, not a fix. Deliverable is a short report that:

1. Reproduces or observes at least one hang incident and captures:
   - ps / pgrep state of the worker process tree
   - stack traces via 'go tool pprof' against the worker (if alive)
   - Timeline from .ddx/executions/<attempt>/embedded/*.jsonl (last event type + timestamp)
   - tracker claimed_at and retry_after state

2. Classifies the hang into one of the four categories above (or a new category).

3. Files concrete follow-up beads for each identified root cause:
   - Tool subprocess timeout if bash hangs
   - LLM client deadline if provider hangs
   - Claim expiry / heartbeat if tracker-side leak
   - File-lock / worktree cleanup if fs-side

4. Recommends whether ddx-b808df39's stop/kill mechanism is sufficient as mitigation or needs additional primitives.

## Relation to ddx-b808df39

ddx-b808df39 ships the OPERATOR TOOL to stop a hung worker. This bead finds the BUG that causes hangs. Both are needed; ship ddx-b808df39 first if the operator-tool is more urgent, then this investigation.

## Non-goals

- Fixing the root cause in this bead. Scope is investigation + follow-up bead filing.
- Preventing all possible hangs (impossible). Scope is the actually-observed hang modes.","events_attachment":"ddx-0a651925/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-18T04:50:48.095091931Z","id":"ddx-0a651925","issue_type":"task","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:investigation","area:agent","area:workers"],"notes":"Investigation complete (attempt 20260418T043148-1346e8a3). Report at .ddx/executions/20260418T043148-1346e8a3/incident_report.md.

Root-cause summary:
- RC1 (PRIMARY): Runner.Run and Runner.RunAgent discard caller ctx (runner.go:239, agent_runner.go:163). WorkerManager.Stop cannot interrupt a running agent.
- RC2: idle-reset timer instead of wall-clock deadline for non-claude paths (executor.go:154, agent_runner.go:264).
- RC3: compaction-stuck breaker fires too late (~2h observed).
- RC4: provider HTTP calls likely lack per-request deadlines (confirm against live hung worker).
- BashTool subprocess hang ruled out (120s default timeout). Tracker-side / fs-lock hypotheses are downstream of RC1.

Follow-up beads filed (parent=ddx-0a651925):
- …-526efaf1 P0 [RC1] propagate caller context
- …-b203c889 P0 [RC2] wall-clock deadline
- …-3e3913a0 P1 [RC4] per-request HTTP deadlines
- …-b0562380 P2 [RC3] bound compaction-stuck breaker by wall-clock
- …-34d039b7 P1 autonomous worker watchdog (complements ddx-b808df39)

ddx-b808df39's operator stop/kill is a correct mitigation but NOT sufficient as sole fix — until RC1 lands, operators will keep needing it. Ship ddx-b808df39 first, then RC1 → RC2 → RC4 → RC3 → watchdog.

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:101:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As a developer running `go test`, my test runs do not leave behind registered projects in my real `~/.local/share/ddx/server-state.json`. When a machine has already been polluted, I can prune it with one command. The server sweep is aggressive enough that obvious test-dir pollution does not survive a restart.

**Acceptance criteria:**

1. **Fix A — test isolation enforced by CI.**
   - Every existing test that instantiates `ServerState` or boots a server helper is migrated to an isolated state dir. No test writes to `~/.local/share/ddx/server-state.json`.
   - A lint test (Go or shell) parses `cli/internal/server/**/*_test.go` (and any other directories that wire up `ServerState`) and fails if it finds a construction that does not pass an explicit isolated state path or override `XDG_DATA_HOME` first. Test cases added for both the allowed-pattern and the disallowed-pattern.

2. **Fix B — sweep recognizes test-dir pollution.**
   - `ServerState.migrate` drops entries whose `Path` matches any of:
     - prefix `/tmp/`, `/private/tmp/`, `/var/folders/`
     - regex `/Test[A-Z][A-Za-z0-9_]*\d+/` anywhere in the path
   - Missing paths matching these patterns are dropped on first sweep (no 24h tombstone).
   - Reachable paths matching these patterns are still dropped (they are never real projects — the test dir may exist transiently but is never the user's registered project).
   - Unit test seeds a fixture state with one real project, one `/tmp/TestFoo123/001` missing, one `/tmp/TestBar456/002` still on disk, and one `/private/tmp/TestMac789/001`; asserts only the real project survives.

3. **Fix C — pruning CLI.**
   - `ddx server state prune [--dry-run] [--state <path>]` applies the Fix B rules to an on-disk state file.
   - Writes `server-state.json.bak-<YYYYMMDDTHHMMSS>` before overwriting.
   - Prints summary: `Pruned X of Y entries (Z kept)`. Dry-run prints the summary without writing.
   - Integration test uses a fixture state containing 10 real + 100 fake entries, runs the command, asserts 10 remain and a backup file with exactly 110 entries was written.

4. **Perf amplifier — regression test.**
   - A test asserts `GetBeadSnapshots` does **not** call `os.Stat` on any project already tombstoned or pattern-filtered. (Implementation detail: sweep on startup + skip tombstoned in the hot loop.)
   - If Fix A+B are in place, a fresh `go test ./...` run followed by a server restart produces a state file with ≤ 20 projects on a typical dev machine. Asserted via a CI job that runs the tests, starts the server briefly, inspects the state file, and fails if test-dir patterns are present.

5. **Backward compatibility.**
   - The prune CLI is safe to run on an existing polluted state file. The sweep upgrade is safe on restart — no data loss for real projects.
   - On upgrade, a note is logged: "Pruned X phantom test-dir projects from state file."

6. **Cross-reference.**
   - Bead notes link to `ddx-9ce6842a` (beads perf) and `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions feed) as the two surfaces whose perf problems are amplified by this bug. Those beads' fixture sizing assumptions were correct for a clean state file; the operator experience will still feel slow on a polluted machine until Fix C is run.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"a101e215a0b34590951f51340d2f25c07ff1c5cc","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:44:13.806406362Z","description":"## Observed

`~/.local/share/ddx/server-state.json` on a developer machine contains **7,343 registered projects**. Composition:

- **6,936 named "001"** — all are Go test temp dirs like `/tmp/TestAgentCapabilitiesCommandJSON1451829170/001`, `/tmp/TestAgentCheckSuccess3666443068/001`.
- **344 named "002"** — same pattern in tests that use `002/`.
- **~60 real projects** (ddx, helix, axon, plus per-branch worktrees).
- Only 135 of the 6,936 are marked unreachable; the rest are "alive" because their `/tmp` dir still exists or because `LastSeen` is recent.
- State file size: 1.8 MB.

## Root cause

1. Go tests that exercise the server (or anything that wires up a `ServerState`) call `RegisterProject` with a temp-dir path. The state file they write to is **not isolated** — it defaults to the shared XDG location (`serverAddrDir()` at `cli/internal/server/server.go:67`) unless the test explicitly overrides `XDG_DATA_HOME` or constructs state via the `tc.StateDir` injection path.
2. There is no test-side deregistration on teardown. Temp dirs get cleaned up by `t.TempDir()`'s own finalizer, but the project entry in the production state file stays.
3. The reachability sweep (`ServerState.migrate` at `cli/internal/server/state.go:85-148`) tombstones missing-path entries and drops them 24h later. With thousands of entries being added per test run, the rate of addition outpaces the 24h GC.

## Perf amplifier (cross-cutting)

Both `GetBeadSnapshots` (`state_graphql.go:49-105`) and `GetAgentSessionsGraphQL` (`state_graphql.go:476-489`) iterate every registered project per request. With N=7343 that's ~7k `os.Stat` + `store.ReadAll` attempts per bead-list or sessions query, mostly against dead paths. This is part of why `ddx-9ce6842a` (beads perf) and `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions feed) feel slow in practice even before their own N·M bugs are considered.

## Proposed direction

Three independently-landable fixes:

### Fix A — Test isolation (highest leverage)

- Audit every test that calls `ServerState` / `RegisterProject` / boots a server. Each one must point at an isolated state dir (`t.TempDir()` + `XDG_DATA_HOME` override, or direct `tc.StateDir` injection).
- Add a linting test: parse `cli/internal/server/**/*_test.go` for constructions of `ServerState` or HTTP server test helpers and assert each uses an isolated state dir. Fail CI otherwise.
- This is the real fix. The rest is damage mitigation.

### Fix B — Smarter sweep (defense in depth)

- Sweep: any path under `/tmp`, `/private/tmp`, `/var/folders/*/T/` (macOS test temp root), or matching `/tmp/Test*` should be treated as a **candidate test-dir project** and dropped immediately on first sweep that finds it missing — no 24h tombstone.
- Sweep should also drop entries where `Path` contains any `Test[A-Z]\w+\d+` segment (the Go testing convention), regardless of whether the dir currently exists. These are never real projects.
- Unit test: seed a state with mixed real + fake-test-dir entries, run sweep, assert only the real ones remain.

### Fix C — Manual cleanup CLI

- `ddx server state prune` — read state file, apply Fix B's rules, write backup to `~/.local/share/ddx/server-state.json.bak-<timestamp>`, write cleaned state, print summary (X dropped, Y kept).
- Useful for recovery on machines that have already accumulated pollution — like the reporter's. Keep even after Fix A lands.
- Dry-run flag prints the summary without writing.

## Out of scope

- Moving state to SQLite.
- Per-user project management UI. (Follow-up if the projects list becomes a real operator surface.)
- Renaming "001" away from a confusing default. The name field is just `filepath.Base(path)`; fixing Fix A makes this question moot.","events_attachment":"ddx-15f7ee0b/events.jsonl","execute-loop-last-detail":"escalation exhausted: unsupported model "minimax/minimax-m2.7" for harness "gemini"; supported models: gemini-2.5-pro, gemini-2.5-flash, gemini-2.5-flash-lite","execute-loop-last-status":"execution_failed","execute-loop-no-changes-count":1,"feature":"FEAT-020","foo":"bar","id":"ddx-15f7ee0b","issue_type":"bug","labels":["feat-020","feat-008","testing","hygiene","perf-amplifier"],"notes":"REVIEW:BLOCK

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:145:{"acceptance":"- [ ] Option A, B, or C selected and documented in an ADR or design note under the ddx repo
- [ ] Implementation lands with migration path for existing .ddx/beads.jsonl files (no lost bead history)
- [ ] Concurrent writes from 2+ workers on the same machine produce a deterministic, semantically correct tracker state
- [ ] Concurrent writes from 2+ machines (via ddx server or shared remote) produce a deterministic, semantically correct tracker state
- [ ] Claim races are resolved by exactly one winner; losers see a structured 'already_claimed' error
- [ ] Crash recovery: a claimed bead whose worker died returns to unclaimed after the heartbeat timeout (existing mechanism continues to work)
- [ ] Existing beads.jsonl format continues to work as a read-only snapshot for offline tooling (jq queries, etc.)
- [ ] Unit + integration tests covering the concurrency scenarios above
- [ ] Migration test: apply the upgrade to axon's current beads.jsonl (748+ beads on sindri side, 30+ new on eitri side) and verify no semantic loss","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:20:46.447948869Z","description":"Fourth child of ddx-b7ee0d6f. .ddx/beads.jsonl is a git-tracked mutable ledger. Multiple writers on different machines or in parallel worker processes produce divergent files that require manual textual merge. git merge can sometimes append-merge JSONL cleanly if each side touches disjoint lines, but when both sides touch the same bead (claim / close / add-event), the merge is semantically broken even when git reports it as resolved.

## Evidence

On axon, eitri and sindri both wrote to beads.jsonl independently for 14 days. Local has 134 commits touching the tracker; origin has 336. When I tried to rebase my planning commit onto origin, git reported conflicts in .ddx/beads.jsonl — and these conflicts would have been 'resolved' by git's textual merge logic even when the semantic outcome is wrong (e.g. two machines claiming the same bead with different claimed-machine values).

The existing execute-loop help text says:

    By default execute-loop submits to the running ddx server as a background
    worker and returns immediately. Use --local to run inline in the current
    process.

So a ddx server EXISTS and is supposed to coordinate. But .ddx/beads.jsonl is still the ledger, which means the server is not the source of truth — it's just a submission surface. The git-tracked file is authoritative, and that's what's racing.

## Options (pick one during implementation)

**A. Server-authoritative tracker.** When the ddx server is running, it owns beads.jsonl. All reads and writes go through the server's API. The git-tracked file becomes a periodic snapshot for backup/offline use, not the source of truth. Workers on multiple machines talk to a shared server.

**B. CRDT append-only log.** Replace the current representation with an append-only log keyed by (machine_id, bead_id, sequence, timestamp). Each writer appends to its own partition; readers compute the effective state by merging partitions with a conflict-resolution rule (last-writer-wins per (bead_id, field), or better). git textual merge of the partitioned log is always safe because writers never share lines.

**C. Per-bead lock files with atomic rename.** Each bead mutation acquires a lock file (beads/<id>.lock), writes beads/<id>.json atomically via rename, releases the lock. The JSONL aggregate is regenerated from per-bead files. Git tracks per-bead files, not the aggregate. Safe across local parallelism but not across machines without network coordination.

**Recommendation**: A is the cleanest if the ddx server is already deployed as authoritative infrastructure. B is the best fit for the current decentralized model. C is a stopgap that solves intra-machine parallelism but not multi-machine drift.

## Scope note

Do NOT conflate this bead with the fetch/push/rebase triad (ddx-bd42f640, ddx-83ec1430, ddx-6aa50e57). Those three fix the git-level divergence; this bead fixes the tracker-level divergence. Both are needed — the triad without tracker safety would still produce semantically-conflicting beads.jsonl merges even when the underlying git history is linear.

## Test cases

- Two workers on the same machine claim different beads concurrently: both claims persist correctly
- Two workers on different machines claim different beads concurrently: both claims persist correctly (requires network coordination)
- Two workers attempt to claim the same bead: one wins, the other sees 'already claimed' and picks a different one
- Recovery from crash between claim and CloseWithEvidence: the bead returns to unclaimed after a heartbeat timeout
- Tracker state is deterministic from any valid ordering of concurrent writes (CRDT option)","id":"ddx-2791dc4f","issue_type":"bug","labels":["bug","execute-loop","tracker","concurrency"],"parent":"ddx-b7ee0d6f","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-820c201","status":"closed","title":"execute-loop: .ddx/beads.jsonl concurrent-write safety","updated_at":"2026-04-14T14:45:05.880884507Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:399:{"archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-15T06:24:35Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3766264","closing_commit_sha":"604f17d53ce26b5776472f9fa77a82a2eb1b9cba","created_at":"2026-04-15T01:35:08.058474818Z","description":"The ddx-bead skill guides bead creation but underspecifies what makes a bead *execution-ready* for automated execute-bead runs. Observed during axon ADR-018 execute-loop driving: beads that technically met the current skill's requirements (title, AC, labels, description pointing at spec-id) still produced bad execute-bead outcomes because the description lacked the context the agent needed to ship correct code in one pass.

## Gaps in the current skill

1. **Description quality is underspecified.** The skill gives AC quality rules but the description field just says 'per SD-004 Section 2' is OK. In practice, agents need:
   - Exact file paths to touch (e.g., 'crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs')
   - Cross-bead context when dependencies are listed (e.g., 'A1 landed the credential_revocations table in crates/axon-storage/src/auth_schema.rs — query it via SELECT 1')
   - Inline spec excerpts, NOT just doc-id references. If the spec is ADR-018 §4's failure table, paste the table into the description. Don't say 'see ADR-018 §4'.
   - Files NOT to touch (scope negation)

2. **spec-id and the doc graph are unreliable for execution context.** The current skill implies spec-id linkage is sufficient. It is not — spec-id drives docgraph.BuildGraphWithConfig() which only resolves HELIX-shaped doc references. Generic beads on non-HELIX projects have no equivalent. AND even when resolution works, the agent only gets a file path, not the relevant section. The bead description is the only place that carries content the agent can rely on.

3. **No guidance on cross-bead context.** When bead B depends on bead A, B's description should describe what A landed (file paths, type names, key APIs) — not assume the agent will reopen A's history. Automated execute-bead does not have that context; each bead is read cold.

4. **No guidance on verification commands in AC.** AC items like 'unit tests green' are too vague. Should be 'cargo test -p axon-server green' or 'bun test passes in sdk/typescript'. Named commands.

5. **No guidance on bead size.** A bead should be one unit of testable work, sized so a single agent pass can complete it. Too small is overhead; too large defeats review. Heuristic: if the AC lists more than ~12 checkboxes, consider splitting.

6. **No guidance on incorporating review findings on reopen.** When a bead is reopened after a REQUEST_CHANGES review, the author should either (a) fold the review findings into the description for the next attempt to see, or (b) rely on the tool to thread review events into the prompt (a separate ddx bead — see ddx-a317208c). The skill is silent on this.

7. **No template for description.** A recommended description template (Scope / Files to touch / Cross-bead context / Verification / Quality bar) would make good beads cheaper to author.

## Scope

Update skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md to add:

- A 'What makes a bead execution-ready' section that codifies the points above
- A 'Description template' with recommended sections: Scope, Files to touch, Files NOT to touch, Cross-bead context, Verification commands, Spec excerpts inline
- A 'Bead sizing' section with the ~12-AC heuristic
- A 'Review-driven reopen' section that tells authors to update the description in place when a bead is reopened after a review rejection
- A 'Common anti-patterns' section enumerating the mistakes observed during the axon execute-loop:
  - Listing doc IDs without inlining the relevant text
  - Not naming file paths in the description
  - AC items that are not verifiable by a named command
  - Depending on unmerged work without explaining what it landed

Keep the existing required-fields and label-conventions sections — they are correct.

## Context

Observed during axon ADR-018 execute-loop:
- Bead A1 was well-sized but initially had scope creep in its description (audit_retention_policies, which belonged to F1). Agent added the scope-creep table faithfully.
- Bead A2's initial description said 'UserIdentity { provider, subject, user_id }' — a typo carried over from an earlier ADR revision. Agent implemented 'subject' faithfully even though ADR-018 §3 said 'external_id'. The description was the contract the agent followed.
- Bead B1 is being re-authored right now with a richer description (inline ADR-018 §4 failure table, exact file paths, cross-bead context about A1 schema and A2 types) — this is the experiment to validate the authoring changes proposed in this bead.

## Acceptance

- [ ] skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md gains 'What makes a bead execution-ready' section
- [ ] Description template added with the 6 recommended sections
- [ ] Bead sizing heuristic documented
- [ ] Review-driven reopen guidance added
- [ ] Common anti-patterns enumerated
- [ ] Skill still lints against any existing skill-format CI check","events_attachment":"ddx-78613f54/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-15T06:25:35.769449241Z","id":"ddx-78613f54","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","area:bead","area:skill","kind:documentation","gap:bead-authoring"],"owner":"ddx","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-4ef43076","status":"closed","title":"skill: improve ddx-bead SKILL.md with authoring discipline for execution-ready beads","updated_at":"2026-04-15T06:25:42.623027765Z"}

Pull in ddx-agent v0.3.11. Should be a simple passthrough update — the new surface is **omlx provider support**, which is an additional backend behind the existing OpenAI-compatible `Provider` interface. No ddx-side code changes expected beyond the dependency bump; it just becomes configurable downstream.

## v0.3.11 highlights (for release notes / changelog context)

### omlx provider
Local MLX-based inference server with an OpenAI-compatible API plus an extension endpoint at `/v1/models/status` that returns per-model `max_context_window` and `max_tokens`. Served at port 1235 by convention. Tool calling is auto-translated server-side, so from ddx's perspective it's just another openai-compat endpoint — no protocol changes in ddx required.

### Flavor field + probe-based detection
New `ProviderConfig.Flavor` config field (`lmstudio`, `omlx`, `openrouter`, `ollama`) lets operators declare the server type explicitly. When absent, the CLI probes `/v1/models/status` and `/api/v0/models` to autodetect. Replaces fragile port-based heuristics.

### LookupModelLimits
Queries the provider's live API for context and output token limits. Three-tier cascade in the CLI: explicit config (`context_window` / `max_tokens`) → live API probe → catalog context_window (new) → compaction package default. Fixes a case where LM Studio servers omit `context_length` from `/v1/models`, causing compaction to fire at 131k instead of the model's actual ~262k.

### Catalog context_window fallback
`ModelEntry.ContextWindow` YAML field now parsed from models.yaml; `Catalog.ContextWindowForModel(id)` exposes it. Case-insensitive lookup to handle live servers presenting mixed case (e.g. `Qwen3.5-27B-4bit`).

### Spec updates
FEAT-003, SD-005, SD-006 now document the limit-discovery flow, flavor field, and CLI context-window resolution order (AR-2026-04-17).

## Files likely touched in ddx

- ddx-agent vendored binary / go.mod dep bump
- Potentially docs/CHANGELOG noting the new omlx passthrough
- No routing or harness logic changes expected — omlx is just another `openai-compat` `type` in config.yaml","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-18T00:39:29.879035281Z","id":"ddx-a0f7a4bb","issue_type":"chore","labels":["kind:chore","area:vendoring","area:agent"],"notes":"2026-04-18: superseded scope. v0.3.12 just released; it builds on v0.3.11 and adds three new APIs directly relevant to the DDx autorouting effort (ddx-2d974641):

- AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE env-var — unblocks ddx-6a5dfe35 wire-evidence collection (request/response dump at openai-go transport boundary, with Bearer redaction)
- Provider.DetectedFlavor() — cached accessor for the probe-resolved flavor; used by DDx routing for capability gating (ddx-4817edfd)
- Provider.SupportsTools() / SupportsStream() / SupportsStructuredOutput() — flavor-keyed protocol capability flags; consume these in ddx-4817edfd instead of re-deriving

Vendor bump should target v0.3.12 directly. omlx remains a simple openai-compat passthrough on the DDx side — just another 'type: openai-compat' + 'flavor: omlx' in config.yaml — but DDx can now read capability flags at routing time rather than guessing.","owner":"erik","priority":1,"status":"closed","title":"chore: bring in ddx-agent v0.3.11 (omlx provider support)","updated_at":"2026-04-18T01:51:14.662676501Z"}

README or docs/ entry describes how to regenerate fixtures via scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh when omlx server behavior changes.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-18T04:14:07Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1833233","closing_commit_sha":"ad14ee84ff0faa5b8754f5274ea795fb1511cde7","created_at":"2026-04-18T03:01:36.207143767Z","description":"## Context

ddx-agent v0.3.14 shipped the SSE comment-frame filter that unblocks omlx. Manual dogfooding (2026-04-18) confirmed a single bead executing via vidar-omlx for 6+ minutes with 17,000+ streaming events before the fix caused the regression this bead is meant to prevent: we have no automated assertion that the omlx path actually stays healthy.

Both fresh-eyes reviews (codex + opus, 2026-04-18) flagged: 'missing bead for v0.3.14 consumer-side smoke test in DDx'. This is that bead.

## Fix

Add an end-to-end smoke test that runs a real ddx work invocation against a recorded-or-live omlx endpoint and asserts the attempt closes (or at least produces a closing-status result: success / already_satisfied / a genuine model-side error) without surfacing 'unexpected end of JSON input' or silent stream termination.

Two implementation options:

1. **Record + replay via virtual harness** (preferred for CI isolation). Capture a full AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1 trace of a successful vidar-omlx run, store it as a fixture, replay via a test double that emulates omlx's SSE quirks (keep-alive comment frames, reasoning warmup silence, reasoning_content deltas). Test asserts ddx-agent SDK consumes the full stream, ddx work's execute-bead orchestrator records closing status, and bead state mutates correctly.

2. **Live smoke, opt-in** (developer-machine only). A test file guarded by build tag  that makes a real request to vidar-omlx. Useful for catching provider-side regressions (e.g., omlx server ships a breaking change). Skip in CI.

Do BOTH: option 1 for CI signal, option 2 for manual release verification.

## What this prevents

- Regression: a future ddx-agent bump reintroduces the SSE-comment-frame bug or similar streaming defect. CI fixture test catches it before the bump merges.
- Regression: DDx-side change breaks the omlx dispatch path (e.g., adds a header that trips omlx's parser). CI catches it.
- Silent-success: ddx work completes but actually landed a bogus result_rev because of a subtle SSE-frame-dropping bug. Fixture replay asserts the expected final outcome.

## Coverage target

Minimum: one happy-path fixture (successful bead close) AND one error-path fixture (model returns malformed tool-call; execute-bead records task_failed cleanly without corrupting the bead tracker).

## Files likely touched

- cli/internal/agent/omlx_e2e_test.go (new) — fixture replay + assertions
- testdata/omlx-wire/ (new) — captured AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE JSONL fixtures
- scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh (new) — helper to regenerate fixtures against a live endpoint

## Depends on

`bun run test:e2e` from `cli/internal/server/frontend` exits 1: 42 of 196 tests fail, 149 pass, 5 skipped. The failures are not regressions — they are accumulated drift between the test suite and the current UI. `playwright.config.ts` runs only `bun run preview` (port 4173, static SvelteKit output, no Go backend), and the suite outgrew that scope.

Bead ddx-955b9c41 (closed 2026-04-21) flagged the split between `playwright.config.ts` (4173) and `playwright.demo.config.ts` (18080) as "likely intentional but undocumented". This epic resolves it.

## Failure breakdown

**(A) TC-008 HTTP API — 6 tests.** `e2e/app.spec.ts:368-409`. Direct `request.get('/api/...')` to `/api/health`, `/api/documents`, `/api/beads`, `/api/beads/status`, `/api/personas`, `/api/docs/graph`. Static preview returns 404; the Go server returns 200 for all six (verified with curl against the running ddx-server on https://127.0.0.1:7743 during the session that filed this bead).

**(B) TC-001 Dashboard — 8 tests.** `e2e/app.spec.ts:9-57`. All visit `/` and expect `h1` "Dashboard". Per Stage 3.8 (commit 3bf655cf), `src/routes/+page.svelte` does `goto(`/nodes/${nodeStore.value.id}`, { replaceState: true })` whenever a node is selected — `/` is no longer a Dashboard route. Tests were not migrated.

**(C) Pages requiring backend data — ~28 tests.** Spread across `e2e/app.spec.ts` (TC-002, TC-003, TC-005, TC-007), `e2e/beads-smoke.spec.ts`, `e2e/executions.spec.ts`, `e2e/navigation.spec.ts` (TC-006, TC-007), `e2e/screenshots.spec.ts`, `e2e/demo-recording.spec.ts`, `e2e/workers.spec.ts`, `src/routes/layout.e2e.ts`. All fail because the static preview can't serve real document/bead/session data.

## In-scope files

Configuration:
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/playwright.config.ts` — switch `webServer` to spawn the Go ddx server (built binary or `go run`) on a deterministic port with a fixture working dir, instead of `bun run preview`. Set `use.baseURL` to that port. Keep `bun run preview` available as a separate project ("static-only") for any tests that genuinely don't need a backend.
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/playwright.demo.config.ts` — **leave alone**. Used for demo recording; do not modify.

Fixture:
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/fixtures/<name>/` — new directory containing a seeded `.ddx/beads.jsonl` (handful of beads in varied states: open, closed, ready, blocked), a tiny `docs/` library (a few documents the API can index), and any minimum config the server needs. Self-contained — must NOT depend on the developer's `~/.config/ddx` or the parent repo's `.ddx/`.

Tests (categorize and migrate):
- `e2e/app.spec.ts` (TC-001, TC-002, TC-003, TC-005, TC-007, TC-008) — rewrite navigation paths to use `/nodes/<fixture-node-id>/projects/<fixture-project-id>/...`, matching the pattern in `e2e/navigation.spec.ts:177`. Drop assumptions about `/` resolving to a Dashboard route.
- `e2e/beads-smoke.spec.ts`, `e2e/executions.spec.ts`, `e2e/navigation.spec.ts`, `e2e/screenshots.spec.ts`, `e2e/demo-recording.spec.ts`, `e2e/workers.spec.ts`, `src/routes/layout.e2e.ts` — verify each passes against the new harness; update navigation paths and any data-shape assumptions where needed.

CI / runbook:
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/README.md` (or whatever is canonical) — document that `bun run test:e2e` now requires the ddx Go binary built first, and how the fixture is structured.

## Out-of-scope (file separate beads if needed)

- Server source changes in `cli/internal/server/` — the API is correct; this bead is about test wiring.
- Frontend route additions or component changes — only test files and fixtures change.
- `playwright.demo.config.ts` and `demo-recording.spec.ts` semantics — leave untouched (recording is its own concern).
- Visual regression baselines in `e2e/screenshots.spec.ts` — re-baselining if pixel diffs appear is a follow-up bead, not this one.

## Why an epic, not a single task

Per beads.md, anything that touches more than ~3 files or crosses subsystems should decompose. This crosses test-infra (playwright config + fixture seeding) and four distinct test files at minimum. Codex should decompose during refinement: at minimum, "(1) harness + fixture", "(2) migrate TC-001/TC-008", "(3) migrate page-data tests", and "(4) update runbook".

## Rollback / cleanup

If a child bead fails partway:
- Do not leave the fixture dir half-populated under `e2e/fixtures/`. Either commit a complete fixture or revert.
- Do not commit a `playwright.config.ts` that runs but skips tests (no `.skip` to make CI green).
- The currently-passing 149 tests must continue to pass at every checkpoint.

The tool already knows which tiers exist (model-catalog.yaml: cheap/standard/smart) and which harnesses are configured. Given a live probe of each provider's health at attempt time, the loop can auto-escalate on its own.

## Principle

Discovery over configuration. The user shouldn't write a fallback chain — the tool should figure it out.

## Default policy (no flags)

- Start at **cheap** tier, execute the bead
- If merge succeeds AND post-review (gap #1) APPROVES → close
- If anything fails (execution_failed, no_changes after N retries, post_run_check_failed, REQUEST_CHANGES from review) → escalate one tier and retry
- Chain: cheap → standard → smart → exhausted (preserve, flag for human)
- Review agent runs at max(impl_tier+1, smart). Never review cheap with cheap.

## Resource discovery at attempt time

- Before picking a concrete harness/model for a tier, probe each candidate provider (local inference host, API key validity, network reachability)
- If provider returns 502 / 500 / timeout / auth-rejected within a short budget, mark it unhealthy with a cooldown and try the next provider in the tier
- If every provider in a tier is unhealthy, skip the tier and escalate
- Per-provider health state is process-local and visible via a new 'ddx agent providers' sub-command (ddx-09ea1884 Visibility 3)

## Rare overrides

- --min-tier standard (skip cheap — useful for hard beads known to need it)
- --max-tier standard (never reach smart — cost control)
- --harness <name> / --model <name> (debug override; disables escalation)

## Surface

Default invocation becomes:

  ddx agent execute-loop --once

Everything else is implicit. Review, escalation, and resource discovery are on-by-default.

## Events

Each attempt appends an execute-bead event: { harness, model, tier, provider, probe_result, attempt_status, review_status }. The event stream shows exactly why a tier/provider was picked or skipped.

## Context

Observed during axon ADR-018 loop:
- bragi was down (502) — I manually probed with curl and switched to vidar-coder. Tool should do this.
- qwen3-coder-next merged but opus review rejected — I manually re-invoked with sonnet. Tool should escalate automatically.
- Resulting CLI invocation in my notes was too complex (—-fallback-chain a/b,c/d,e/f --post-review g/h). User feedback: too many knobs. Reach for a default that just works.

## Acceptance criteria replaced with discovery-driven ones (see accept field)","events_attachment":"ddx-dc231144/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-dc231144","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","area:agent","area:exec","kind:feature","gap:review-loop"],"notes":"<operator-feedback 2026-04-15>
Additional observation: --local is too prominent as a flag. The default IS server-worker mode, but I (an experienced DDx operator) still reached for --local reflexively because I wanted a synchronous handle to the subprocess. The root cause: I didn't know how to tail a server-side worker from the CLI.

Possible fixes (minor, could be part of this bead's scope):
- 'ddx agent execute-loop --once --follow' that submits to server and tails the worker log to stdout until completion, then returns. Gives the synchronous-feel without forking a subprocess.
- 'ddx server workers wait <worker-id>' that blocks until the named worker is in a terminal state.
- Document in execute-loop --help that --local is for debugging only; the primary surface is server-worker.

Not worth a separate bead — just ergonomics for the already-correct default.

      cli/cmd/command_factory.go (production), cli/internal/config/testconfig.go,
        cli/internal/exec/types.go (mostly comments)

  Net surface: ~4,000 LoC of net change across the agent package, the cmd
  package, the server package, the exec package, the config package, plus
  ~10,000 LoC of test code that constructs the legacy types or accepts them
  as helper parameters. Every public-API surface change cascades to its
  callers, including BuildArgs (15+ test call sites) and
  SessionIndexEntryFromResult (consumed by session-index machinery and the
  oversize-prompt ingress test).

## What is blocking

The migration is too large for a single execute-bead worker window even with
the expanded scope. Three concrete reasons:

1. **Per-bead size budget.** SD-024 §Migration discipline (lines 234–256)
   explicitly mandates "<500 LoC of net change, <20 minutes of agent time,
   under $10 of model spend" per bead, with a footnote that "Anything that
   wants to 'do the whole loop migration in one shot' is sized wrong and
   must be split." This bead's expanded scope is ~4,000 production LoC plus
   ~10,000 test LoC — 8x to 28x the SD-mandated budget. The previous attempt
   spent 16.6 minutes and $6.75 producing no_changes; even doubling that
   budget cannot produce a green tree across this surface in one pass.

2. **The migration is not one atomic change but a sequence of seam swaps.**
   Each affected public API (RunViaService → RunWithConfigViaService,
   RunCompareViaService → RunCompareWithConfigViaService, BuildArgs's
   RunOptions parameter, SessionIndexEntryFromResult's RunOptions parameter,
   RunFunc closure type) has its own caller graph. SD-024's own discipline
   (lines 248–256) prescribes per-caller migration beads precisely so each
   step leaves the tree green and lint-clean. Bundling them into one bead
   re-creates the bug class the SD's migration discipline was written to
   avoid: a half-finished refactor with the lint gate firing on the
   intermediate state, where the worker either has to disable hooks (forbidden)
   or revert most of the work to commit anything.

3. **Test-file lint cascade.** The runtime-lint pre-commit gate (lefthook.yml
   pattern 2) flags any staged Go file containing a RunOptions{} / CompareOptions{}
   / QuorumOptions{} composite literal. Migrating BuildArgs's signature
   alone (to take a non-RunOptions parameter) re-stages every one of the
   15+ agent_test.go test files, and the gate fires on each. The same
   cascade fires when SessionIndexEntryFromResult's signature changes
   (re-staging session_index_test.go), when RunFunc's closure type changes
   (re-staging compare/quorum/benchmark tests), and so on for each public-API
   seam swap. Every signature change requires migrating its consumers in
   the *same commit* — but the consumers' test files already contain
   composite literals from sibling test cases that aren't in this bead's
   AC. Those literals stay valid (BuildArgs still works) but the gate fires
   because the file is now staged. Untangling this requires touching every
   composite-literal site in every staged test file — hence the ~10,000 LoC
   of test work above.

## What a follow-up attempt would need

Per SD-024 §Stage 2 (lines 281–286) and §Migration discipline (lines 222–256),
this bead should be split along the seam-swap boundaries the SD itself
prescribes. Concretely, the recommended split is:

a) **B22d-grade.go** (~10 LoC): switch the single `r.Run(RunOptions{...})`
   call in cli/internal/agent/grade.go to
   `r.runInternal(RunArgs{...})` via the existing dispatch seam from
   ddx-dd640c18. Satisfies AC items 1, 4, 5 (RunOptions/CompareOptions/
   QuorumOptions removed from grade.go — items 4 and 5 are vacuous as the
   types aren't present today). Build and tests green; runtime-lint green
   because the staged file contains no legacy literals after the swap.
   Independently buildable, lint-clean, AC-checkable.

b) **B22d-cmd-RunViaService** (~30 LoC): switch
   cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1462's RunViaService call to
   RunWithConfigViaService, threading a ResolvedConfig through the call.
   Independently buildable, lint-clean.

c) **B22d-cmd-RunBenchmarkViaService** (~30 LoC): same migration for
   :1243 RunBenchmarkViaService → a new RunBenchmarkWithConfigViaService
   that takes ResolvedConfig.

d) **B22d-execute_bead-dispatch** (~80 LoC + 2-3 test files):
   migrate execute_bead.go dispatchAgentRun (line 647 RunOptions
   composite, line 938 RunViaServiceWith) and execute_bead_review.go
   dispatchReviewRun (line 531 RunOptions composite, line 704
   RunViaServiceWith) onto a new internal seam that takes
   ResolvedConfig + AgentRunRuntime. Update the corresponding
   execute_bead_*_test.go files in this bead (they already import the
   functions and stage as siblings).

e) **B22d-BuildArgs-signature** (~150 LoC, mostly tests):
   change BuildArgs's parameter from RunOptions to a new internal
   buildArgsInput struct (or a slice of explicit args). Update
   agent_test.go's 15+ call sites in the same commit. Adjacent runner.go
   call sites stay on RunOptions internally — runner.go is not in the AC
   removal list.

f) **B22d-SessionIndexEntryFromResult-signature** (~80 LoC):
   change SessionIndexEntryFromResult's RunOptions parameter to take
   the small subset of fields it actually uses (Harness, Model, Provider,
   Correlation, PromptSource, etc.) as discrete arguments or a small
   internal struct. Update session_index_test.go and the four
   service-run callers in the same commit.

g) **B22d-RunFunc-and-compare-internals** (~250 LoC):
   change RunFunc's signature, runCompareArmWith, defaultResolvePromptForCompare,
   the public RunCompareWith/RunQuorumWith/RunBenchmarkWith signatures
   and BenchmarkArmsToCompare's return shape. Update prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go,
   models_test.go, claude_stream_test.go in the same commit. After this
   step, compare_adapter.go has no RunOptions/CompareOptions/QuorumOptions
   remaining (AC items 2, 3 satisfied).

h) **B22d-service_run-public-API** (~150 LoC):
   delete RunViaService, RunViaServiceWith, runFixtureHarnessViaRunner and
   inline their bodies into the new RunWithConfigViaService internal helper.
   At this point all callers have already moved (steps b, c, d) so the
   deletion is mechanical. service_run.go has no RunOptions remaining
   (AC item 2 satisfied).

After all eight steps, the legacy types are unreachable from any caller,
runner.go's internal RunOptions usage can be retired in B22 final, and
the type definitions in types.go:36/81/171 can be deleted as planned.

The grade.go change in isolation (step a) is genuinely a 10-LoC win and
satisfies 3 of the 7 AC items (1, 4, 5) — but the bead's stated rule
"Do not commit partial, exploratory, or red code hoping the reviewer will
accept it" forbids landing it without also satisfying items 2, 3, 6, 7.
Per attempt-1's findings, the only path that satisfies *all* AC items in
*one commit* is the ~14,000 LoC change above, which both exceeds the
SD-024 size budget and is unworkable in a single execute-bead worker
window.

The right shape for this work is the eight-bead split above. Each step
is independently <500 LoC, satisfies SD-024 §Migration discipline, and
ends with a green tree, a passing build, and no runtime-lint violations
on its staged files.

.ddx/attachments/ddx-ade5ebb3/events.jsonl:3:{"actor":"ddx","body":"Bead ddx-ade5ebb3 — agent: retire RunOptions + CompareOptions + QuorumOptions + legacy Run

STATUS: not attempted; scope-vs-budget mismatch between bead description
and actual codebase state. Request re-scoping into sub-beads (see below)
before another execution attempt.

================================================================
What is done
================================================================

Stage 2 dispatch-site migrations (beads c0ff5d3e, 4c31e465, 21f9e321,
ee2cd434) landed RunWithConfig / RunCompareWithConfigViaService /
RunQuorumWithConfigViaService alongside the legacy Run /
RunCompareViaService / RunQuorumViaService entry points. The CLI
dispatch sites (`ddx agent run`, `ddx agent compare`, `ddx agent
quorum`) now construct rcfg + AgentRunRuntime / CompareRuntime /
QuorumRuntime and dispatch through the new `*WithConfig*` paths.
A subset of agent test files were migrated by bead ee2cd434.

Stage 1 cleanup (bead edbfa046) removed ExecuteBeadLoopOptions and
the legacy `ExecuteBeadWorker.Run(opts)` method, renamed
`RunWithConfig` → `Run`. That cleanup touched 18 files and ~123
net-changed lines.

================================================================
Why this bead cannot land in one pass as currently scoped
================================================================

The bead description in-scopes only two files:

  - cli/internal/agent/types.go (delete RunOptions / CompareOptions /
    QuorumOptions structs)
  - cli/internal/agent/runner.go (delete legacy Run, rename
    RunWithConfig → Run)

The bead acceptance criteria, however, require `cd cli && go build
./...` and `cd cli && go test ./...` to pass. After the structs and
the legacy method are deleted, the tree does not compile. A grep
over the post-Stage-2 tree shows the deleted symbols are still
referenced in:

  RunOptions: 137 occurrences across 42 files
  CompareOptions: ~15 occurrences across compare_adapter.go,
    models.go, agent_compare_config_test.go, plus 4 testdata files
  QuorumOptions: ~5 occurrences across compare_adapter.go,
    agent_quorum_config_test.go, plus testdata

Stage 2's dispatch-site migrations only retired *construction* of
these structs at the four CLI entry points and a portion of
agent-package tests. The structs are still load-bearing for:

  - Internal helpers in cli/internal/agent: BuildArgs, resolveHarness,
    resolvePrompt, resolveModel, resolveTimeout, resolveWallClock,
    runVirtualFn, RunAgent, runScriptFn, runClaudeWithFallbackFn,
    SessionIndexEntryFromResult, BenchmarkArmsToCompare, and the
    entire compare_adapter.go internal pipeline (CompareOptions /
    QuorumOptions are used as the working struct shape inside
    RunCompare / RunQuorum, not just at the top of the public API).
  - The `runner.Run(RunOptions{...})` self-call inside
    runner.go::TestProviderConnectivity (line 976).
  - The `r.Run(RunOptions{...})` call inside grade.go::169.
  - The `agent.RunViaService` exported function and its internal
    callers (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1462, cli/internal/server/server.go
    at lines 1913 and 3786, cli/internal/exec/store.go:201).
  - The `Run(opts agent.RunOptions)` interface contract on
    cli/internal/exec/types.go:11 plus its three implementers
    (cmd/exec.go, internal/exec/store.go, exec_acceptance_test.go,
    workers_stop_propagation_test.go).
  - cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead_test.go's two test runners
    (fakeAgentRunner, modelPassthroughCapture).
  - cli/internal/agent/prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go's
    resolvePrompt / defaultResolvePromptForCompare /
    RunViaServiceWith calls (the bead 21 commit explicitly noted
    these were left out of scope).
  - cli/tools/lint/evidencelint/* — the testdata uses a *separate*
    stub `agent.RunOptions` in testdata/src/agent/agent.go that is
    independent of the production type, so testdata compiles fine,
    but analyzer.go's package-level doc comments and
    runOptionsTypeNames list reference the production type's name.

================================================================
Estimated true cost
================================================================

Stage 1's analogous cleanup (ExecuteBeadLoopOptions) was 18 files /
~123 net lines. Stage 2's RunOptions/CompareOptions/QuorumOptions
cleanup is ~3-4× larger by raw reference count, and ~2× larger
structurally because (a) RunOptions is used as the central internal
data carrier inside compare_adapter.go's full Run / RunCompare /
RunQuorum pipelines (not just at the dispatch entry), and (b)
external callers in three different packages (cmd/, internal/exec/,
internal/server/) each need their own rcfg + AgentRunRuntime
construction wired in, which means the `agent.RunViaService` /
`agent.RunCompareViaService` / `agent.RunQuorumViaService`
back-compat shims must stay or be replaced.

Conservative estimate: ~40 files / ~1500–2500 net lines / 3-5×
the per-bead size budget defined in SD-024 §"Per-bead size budget"
(target <500 LoC, <20 minutes, <$10 spend).

A no-change here is preferable to a half-finished commit that
leaves the tree red or smuggles a partial migration past review.

================================================================
What a follow-up attempt would need
================================================================

Recommend splitting into a fresh stage-2-cleanup sub-stage with
beads sized to the SD-024 budget. Suggested decomposition:

  1. Migrate cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go internals to
     drive RunCompare / RunQuorum through AgentRunRuntime + rcfg
     end-to-end; retire CompareOptions and QuorumOptions struct
     usage *inside* the agent package.

  2. Migrate cli/internal/agent helpers (BuildArgs, resolveHarness,
     resolvePrompt, resolveModel, resolveTimeout, resolveWallClock,
     runVirtual, RunAgent, runScript, runClaudeWithFallback,
     SessionIndexEntryFromResult, BenchmarkArmsToCompare) so they
     take rcfg + AgentRunRuntime (or per-field args) instead of
     RunOptions. Includes runner.go::TestProviderConnectivity and
     grade.go's `r.Run` self-call.

  3. Migrate cli/internal/agent/service_run.go (RunViaService et
     al.) to take rcfg + AgentRunRuntime and update all three
     callers: cmd/agent_cmd.go:1462, server.go:1913+3786,
     internal/exec/store.go:201.

  4. Migrate cli/internal/exec (types.go interface +
     store.go construction + store_test.go mock) and
     cmd/exec.go's serviceExecAgentRunner +
     exec_acceptance_test.go mock onto the rcfg-based interface.

  5. Migrate the remaining agent-package test sites that bead
     ee2cd434 left out of scope (prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go,
     execute_bead_runtime_test.go, models_test.go,
     session_index_test.go, virtual_test.go, claude_stream_test.go,
     and the BuildArgs tests in agent_test.go that still construct
     RunOptions literals as inputs to the unit under test).

  6. Migrate cli/cmd test sites (agent_execute_bead_test.go's two
     fake runners, agent_run_config_test.go remnants).

  7. Migrate cli/internal/server (server.go RunOptions
     construction + workers_stop_propagation_test.go mock).

  8. THEN run this cleanup bead as the no-op final-deletion
     bead it was designed to be: delete RunOptions /
     CompareOptions / QuorumOptions structs from types.go,
     delete the legacy Run method on Runner, rename
     RunWithConfig → Run. At that point the AC's `go build`
     and `go test` will pass on the existing tree.

After step 7, the tree should grep-clean for the three struct
names except in cli/tools/lint/evidencelint/testdata/src/agent/
(self-contained stub package, leave alone) and in analyzer.go's
package doc comment + runOptionsTypeNames list (which can be
updated cosmetically in step 8 alongside the deletion).

================================================================
Verification
================================================================

  cd cli && go build ./...   # green at base rev (b199cf3c)
  rg 'type (RunOptions|CompareOptions|QuorumOptions) ' cli/
    # 3 matches in cli/internal/agent/types.go, all current

No code changes were made in this worktree.
