Updated\n    ddx-b808df39 notes to cross-reference this investigation so the\n    operator stop/kill tool tracks the underlying fixes.\n\ndiff --git a/.ddx/executions/20260418T043148-1346e8a3/incident_report.md b/.ddx/executions/20260418T043148-1346e8a3/incident_report.md\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 00000000..5e8547cf\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/.ddx/executions/20260418T043148-1346e8a3/incident_report.md\n@@ -0,0 +1,193 @@\n+# Investigation: root cause of 33–142h hung execute-bead workers\n+\n+**Bead:** ddx-0a651925 (investigates; sibling to ddx-b808df39)\n+**Attempt:** 20260418T043148-1346e8a3\n+**Scope:** Source-code + execution-bundle analysis. No live hung worker was available\n+in this isolated worktree to capture live `ps`/`pprof`, so the incident evidence is\n+drawn from `.ddx/executions/*` bundles and the worker/runner source path.\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 1. Observed evidence (from `.ddx/executions/`)\n+\n+| Metric                                               | Count / value                                        |\n+| ---------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------- |\n+| Total attempt bundles                                | 328                                                  |\n+| Bundles with `result.json`                           | 291 (89%)                                            |\n+| Bundles with `manifest.json` but **no** `result.json` | 37 (11%) — executions whose worker never finalized  |\n+\n+Top five longest *finalized* attempts:\n+\n+| `duration_ms` | Wall time    | `detail`                                                                    | Bundle                          |\n+| ------------- | ------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------- |\n+| 7,720,908     | **2h 8m**    | `agent: compaction stuck: 50 consecutive failed compaction attempts`        | 20260416T230316-efbf91c5        |\n+| 7,200,098     | **2h 0m**    | `timeout after 2h0m0s` (claude streaming wall-clock fired)                  | 20260415T015231-c2ffa9ba        |\n+| 6,764,927     | **1h 53m**   | success (agent harness, qwen)                                               | 20260416T230315-f9f46822        |\n+| 5,649,987     | **1h 34m**   | compaction stuck                                                            | 20260416T230313-e193942b        |\n+| 4,284,347     | **1h 11m**   | compaction stuck                                                            | 20260415T103422-bb3a45fa        |\n+\n+**Key observation:** the 33h / 142h hang durations cited in ddx-b808df39 are\n+~15× and ~65× longer than the longest *finalized* attempt on record. This means\n+the hung workers never reached the point of writing `result.json` — they were\n+blocked inside `runner.Run` (or deeper) for days. Every code path that\n+*should* have terminated them (idle timeout, compaction-stuck, stall detector,\n+cancel propagation) failed to fire.\n+\n+The 37 manifest-only bundles are candidates for hung or externally-killed\n+workers; e.g. `20260416T215953-6b515c05` has a manifest from 2026-04-16T21:59Z\n+with no result. Those fit the profile of \"workers running 33h–142h with no bead\n+assigned\" reported on agent projects.\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 2. Root-cause classification (source code evidence)\n+\n+The investigation checked the four hypotheses from the bead description.\n+\n+### RC1 (PRIMARY) — Caller context is discarded inside the Runner\n+\n+`cli/internal/agent/runner.go:239` (`Runner.Run`):\n+```go\n+ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())\n+```\n+\n+`cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:163` (`Runner.RunAgent`):\n+```go\n+ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())\n+```\n+\n+`RunOptions` (types.go) takes no `context.Context` at all. `agent.ExecuteBead`\n+likewise does not accept ctx, and `server.WorkerManager.runWorker` passes its\n+own ctx into `worker.Run(ctx, …)` but that ctx **never reaches the agent\n+invocation**.\n+\n+**Impact:** `WorkerManager.Stop(id)` (workers.go:648) calls `handle.cancel()`\n+which cancels only the worker goroutine's ctx. The running agent provider call\n+/ subprocess is detached. The only way to interrupt a stuck agent today is a\n+SIGKILL from outside the process. This is the structural defect that enables\n+33–142h hangs — once the agent is stuck, nothing inside DDx can stop it.\n+\n+### RC2 — Idle-reset timer, not wall-clock deadline\n+\n+`cli/internal/agent/executor.go:35-40` creates an *idle timeout* context value:\n+```go\n+func withExecutionTimeout(ctx context.Context, timeout time.Duration) context.Context {\n+    return context.WithValue(ctx, executionTimeoutKey{}, timeout)\n+}\n+```\n+\n+`executor.go:154-178` consumes it and **resets the timer on every stdout/stderr\n+byte**. `agent_runner.go:264-285` implements the same pattern against agentlib\n+events.\n+\n+The default timeout is 2h (`types.go:363 DefaultTimeoutMS = 7200000`). The\n+comment claims \"2 hours — long enough for any task; supervisor/agent decides\n+termination\" but the actual behaviour is **\"2 hours of silence required to fire\"**.\n+A provider that emits a heartbeat, a ping, a retry line, or any byte resets the\n+timer indefinitely.\n+\n+The `runClaudeStreaming` path (`claude_stream.go:373-384`) is the only path that\n+uses a wall-clock deadline — and it is the only path we see actually hitting\n+the timeout (bundle 20260415T015231 exactly 2h 0m 0.098s).\n+\n+**Impact:** for `harness=agent` (qwen, minimax, openrouter, etc.), no wall-clock\n+bound exists. 33–142h hangs are consistent with a provider that dribbles events\n+slowly enough to defeat the idle reset.\n+\n+### RC3 — Compaction-stuck breaker fires late\n+\n+`cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:218-241` increments a counter on each no-op\n+compaction event and trips at `stuckThreshold = maxConsecutiveCompactionFailures`\n+(50 by default). On observed `efbf91c5` it took **2h 8m** to trip. If one\n+iteration takes 3 minutes to produce a no-op compaction event, 50 events =\n+~150 min — matches the observed tail.\n+\n+**Impact:** on its own, the breaker is too slow to be a useful hang-guard. It\n+should be replaced or complemented by a wall-clock-bounded variant.\n+\n+### RC4 — Provider HTTP requests likely lack a per-request deadline\n+\n+`RunAgent` passes the internal `context.Background()`-derived ctx into\n+`agentlib.Run(ctx, req)`. The agent library's providers (`openai.Client`,\n+anthropic client) do not appear to install a per-request deadline themselves\n+(grepping the v0.3.14 module cache shows short timeouts on discovery/probe\n+only; the main call path has no WithTimeout). A stalled TCP socket after\n+headers are received but before the response body arrives can hang a streaming\n+read indefinitely.\n+\n+**Impact:** consistent with \"alive but mid-LLM-call with no client-side\n+deadline\" — hypothesis (2) in the bead description. Cannot be fully confirmed\n+from source alone; needs runtime `go tool pprof http://localhost:<port>/debug/pprof/goroutine`\n+against a live hung worker.\n+\n+### RC-skipped — BashTool subprocess hang (hypothesis 1)\n+\n+Not the root cause. `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent@v0.3.14/tool/bash.go:15`\n+installs a 120s default wall-clock timeout (`context.WithTimeout(ctx, timeout)`)\n+and calls `exec.CommandContext`. Per-call bash hangs self-terminate at 2min.\n+\n+### RC-skipped — Tracker-side claim leak (hypothesis 3)\n+\n+Tracker state is incidental to the hang: the bead's own description notes the\n+hung workers exist as processes (`ps aux | grep claude`). The worker process is\n+alive; the claim is alive because the worker is alive. Fixing the claim alone\n+does not kill the worker. This hypothesis is downstream, not upstream.\n+\n+### RC-skipped — Worktree file-lock (hypothesis 4)\n+\n+No evidence in bundles. Execute-bead worktrees are plain git worktrees at\n+`/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/*`; no process-held file locks would survive across 33–142h\n+without the owning process. If the process is alive, the lock is alive — same\n+as RC3.\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 3. Relationship to ddx-b808df39\n+\n+ddx-b808df39 proposes an operator `ddx agent workers stop <id>` command that\n+sends **SIGTERM at the OS level**, then SIGKILL after a grace period. That\n+design **does** work around RC1 (process-level signal bypasses the broken ctx\n+plumbing). So ddx-b808df39 is a *correct mitigation* — but only a mitigation.\n+\n+Without fixing RC1 (and at least RC2), operators will keep needing to invoke\n+`workers stop` manually. The autonomous remediations every other system relies\n+on (cancel ctx, idle timeout, circuit breaker) are each broken today in ways\n+that require explicit code changes, not just a new operator tool.\n+\n+**Recommendation:** Ship ddx-b808df39 first (quick operational relief), then\n+resolve the child beads filed below in order RC1 → RC2 → RC4 → RC3.\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 4. Follow-up beads filed\n+\n+Each addresses one root cause with a deterministic acceptance test. Priority\n+and ID are shown; full details available via `ddx bead show <id>`.\n+\n+The IDs below carry an `.execute-bead-wt-…` prefix because `ddx bead create`\n+was invoked from inside the isolated execute-bead worktree — this is a\n+separate DDx bug worth noting but orthogonal to the hang investigation. They\n+are parented to `ddx-0a651925` and resolvable by `ddx bead list --parent ddx-0a651925`.\n+\n+| RC  | Priority | Bead ID (suffix)                                                           | Title                                                              |\n+| --- | -------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------ |\n+| RC1 | P0       | `…-526efaf1` | propagate caller context through Runner.Run and RunAgent           |\n+| RC2 | P0       | `…-b203c889` | add wall-clock deadline alongside idle timeout for agent harness   |\n+| RC3 | P2       | `…-b0562380` | bound compaction-stuck breaker by wall-clock time                  |\n+| RC4 | P1       | `…-3e3913a0` | audit and enforce per-request HTTP deadlines on agent provider calls |\n+| —   | P1       | `…-34d039b7` | autonomous worker watchdog with process-level reaper (complements ddx-b808df39) |\n+\n+**Suggested execution order:** RC1 → RC2 → RC4 → RC3 → watchdog. RC1 is a\n+prerequisite for RC4 being meaningful.\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 5. What this investigation could NOT do\n+\n+- Capture live `ps`/`pprof` from a hung worker — none was accessible from the\n+  isolated execute-bead worktree. RC4 in particular needs a live `go tool pprof`\n+  goroutine dump to confirm that the stuck goroutine is in `net/http` streaming\n+  read, not some other path.\n+- Confirm whether the agent library's providers install any timeout that was\n+  missed in grep. The listed RC4 bead should start with a provider-path audit\n+  rather than an immediate fix.","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"7c4457b5-7817-4bd4-9850-94282c2cfdb7","uuid":"b9b08566-e074-49f2-a31f-e547a318b057","timestamp":"2026-04-18T04:51:23.303Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"commit 13cd213e157cd211c48f51796bd62a2fb8b1fcae\nAuthor: Test User <test@ddx.test>\nDate:   Sat Apr 18 00:49:37 2026 -0400\n\n    docs(agent): investigate root cause of 33–142h hung workers [ddx-0a651925]\n    \n    Root-cause investigation report for hung execute-bead workers observed on\n    agent projects (33h–142h). Identified four root causes from source-code and\n    execution-bundle analysis:\n    \n      RC1 (PRIMARY): Runner.Run and Runner.RunAgent discard caller context\n                     (runner.go:239, agent_runner.go:163). WorkerManager.Stop\n                     cannot actually interrupt a running agent.\n      RC2: idle-reset timer masquerades as wall-clock deadline for non-claude\n           paths (executor.go:154, agent_runner.go:264).\n      RC3: compaction-stuck breaker fires far too late (~2h observed).\n      RC4: provider HTTP calls likely lack per-request deadlines.\n    \n    Rules out BashTool subprocess hang (120s default wall-clock timeout)\n    and tracker/fs-lock hypotheses (downstream of RC1).\n    \n    Filed five child beads (P0/P1/P2) parented to ddx-0a651925. Updated\n    ddx-b808df39 notes to cross-reference this investigation so the\n    operator stop/kill tool tracks the underlying fixes.\n\ndiff --git a/.ddx/executions/20260418T043148-1346e8a3/incident_report.md b/.ddx/executions/20260418T043148-1346e8a3/incident_report.md\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 00000000..5e8547cf\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/.ddx/executions/20260418T043148-1346e8a3/incident_report.md\n@@ -0,0 +1,193 @@\n+# Investigation: root cause of 33–142h hung execute-bead workers\n+\n+**Bead:** ddx-0a651925 (investigates; sibling to ddx-b808df39)\n+**Attempt:** 20260418T043148-1346e8a3\n+**Scope:** Source-code + execution-bundle analysis. No live hung worker was available\n+in this isolated worktree to capture live `ps`/`pprof`, so the incident evidence is\n+drawn from `.ddx/executions/*` bundles and the worker/runner source path.\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 1. Observed evidence (from `.ddx/executions/`)\n+\n+| Metric                                               | Count / value                                        |\n+| ---------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------- |\n+| Total attempt bundles                                | 328                                                  |\n+| Bundles with `result.json`                           | 291 (89%)                                            |\n+| Bundles with `manifest.json` but **no** `result.json` | 37 (11%) — executions whose worker never finalized  |\n+\n+Top five longest *finalized* attempts:\n+\n+| `duration_ms` | Wall time    | `detail`                                                                    | Bundle                          |\n+| ------------- | ------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------- |\n+| 7,720,908     | **2h 8m**    | `agent: compaction stuck: 50 consecutive failed compaction attempts`        | 20260416T230316-efbf91c5        |\n+| 7,200,098     | **2h 0m**    | `timeout after 2h0m0s` (claude streaming wall-clock fired)                  | 20260415T015231-c2ffa9ba        |\n+| 6,764,927     | **1h 53m**   | success (agent harness, qwen)                                               | 20260416T230315-f9f46822        |\n+| 5,649,987     | **1h 34m**   | compaction stuck                                                            | 20260416T230313-e193942b        |\n+| 4,284,347     | **1h 11m**   | compaction stuck                                                            | 20260415T103422-bb3a45fa        |\n+\n+**Key observation:** the 33h / 142h hang durations cited in ddx-b808df39 are\n+~15× and ~65× longer than the longest *finalized* attempt on record. This means\n+the hung workers never reached the point of writing `result.json` — they were\n+blocked inside `runner.Run` (or deeper) for days. Every code path that\n+*should* have terminated them (idle timeout, compaction-stuck, stall detector,\n+cancel propagation) failed to fire.\n+\n+The 37 manifest-only bundles are candidates for hung or externally-killed\n+workers; e.g. `20260416T215953-6b515c05` has a manifest from 2026-04-16T21:59Z\n+with no result. Those fit the profile of \"workers running 33h–142h with no bead\n+assigned\" reported on agent projects.\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 2. Root-cause classification (source code evidence)\n+\n+The investigation checked the four hypotheses from the bead description.\n+\n+### RC1 (PRIMARY) — Caller context is discarded inside the Runner\n+\n+`cli/internal/agent/runner.go:239` (`Runner.Run`):\n+```go\n+ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())\n+```\n+\n+`cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:163` (`Runner.RunAgent`):\n+```go\n+ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())\n+```\n+\n+`RunOptions` (types.go) takes no `context.Context` at all. `agent.ExecuteBead`\n+likewise does not accept ctx, and `server.WorkerManager.runWorker` passes its\n+own ctx into `worker.Run(ctx, …)` but that ctx **never reaches the agent\n+invocation**.\n+\n+**Impact:** `WorkerManager.Stop(id)` (workers.go:648) calls `handle.cancel()`\n+which cancels only the worker goroutine's ctx. The running agent provider call\n+/ subprocess is detached. The only way to interrupt a stuck agent today is a\n+SIGKILL from outside the process. This is the structural defect that enables\n+33–142h hangs — once the agent is stuck, nothing inside DDx can stop it.\n+\n+### RC2 — Idle-reset timer, not wall-clock deadline\n+\n+`cli/internal/agent/executor.go:35-40` creates an *idle timeout* context value:\n+```go\n+func withExecutionTimeout(ctx context.Context, timeout time.Duration) context.Context {\n+    return context.WithValue(ctx, executionTimeoutKey{}, timeout)\n+}\n+```\n+\n+`executor.go:154-178` consumes it and **resets the timer on every stdout/stderr\n+byte**. `agent_runner.go:264-285` implements the same pattern against agentlib\n+events.\n+\n+The default timeout is 2h (`types.go:363 DefaultTimeoutMS = 7200000`). The\n+comment claims \"2 hours — long enough for any task; supervisor/agent decides\n+termination\" but the actual behaviour is **\"2 hours of silence required to fire\"**.\n+A provider that emits a heartbeat, a ping, a retry line, or any byte resets the\n+timer indefinitely.\n+\n+The `runClaudeStreaming` path (`claude_stream.go:373-384`) is the only path that\n+uses a wall-clock deadline — and it is the only path we see actually hitting\n+the timeout (bundle 20260415T015231 exactly 2h 0m 0.098s).\n+\n+**Impact:** for `harness=agent` (qwen, minimax, openrouter, etc.), no wall-clock\n+bound exists. 33–142h hangs are consistent with a provider that dribbles events\n+slowly enough to defeat the idle reset.\n+\n+### RC3 — Compaction-stuck breaker fires late\n+\n+`cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:218-241` increments a counter on each no-op\n+compaction event and trips at `stuckThreshold = maxConsecutiveCompactionFailures`\n+(50 by default). On observed `efbf91c5` it took **2h 8m** to trip. If one\n+iteration takes 3 minutes to produce a no-op compaction event, 50 events =\n+~150 min — matches the observed tail.\n+\n+**Impact:** on its own, the breaker is too slow to be a useful hang-guard. It\n+should be replaced or complemented by a wall-clock-bounded variant.\n+\n+### RC4 — Provider HTTP requests likely lack a per-request deadline\n+\n+`RunAgent` passes the internal `context.Background()`-derived ctx into\n+`agentlib.Run(ctx, req)`. The agent library's providers (`openai.Client`,\n+anthropic client) do not appear to install a per-request deadline themselves\n+(grepping the v0.3.14 module cache shows short timeouts on discovery/probe\n+only; the main call path has no WithTimeout). A stalled TCP socket after\n+headers are received but before the response body arrives can hang a streaming\n+read indefinitely.\n+\n+**Impact:** consistent with \"alive but mid-LLM-call with no client-side\n+deadline\" — hypothesis (2) in the bead description. Cannot be fully confirmed\n+from source alone; needs runtime `go tool pprof http://localhost:<port>/debug/pprof/goroutine`\n+against a live hung worker.\n+\n+### RC-skipped — BashTool subprocess hang (hypothesis 1)\n+\n+Not the root cause. `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent@v0.3.14/tool/bash.go:15`\n+installs a 120s default wall-clock timeout (`context.WithTimeout(ctx, timeout)`)\n+and calls `exec.CommandContext`. Per-call bash hangs self-terminate at 2min.\n+\n+### RC-skipped — Tracker-side claim leak (hypothesis 3)\n+\n+Tracker state is incidental to the hang: the bead's own description notes the\n+hung workers exist as processes (`ps aux | grep claude`). The worker process is\n+alive; the claim is alive because the worker is alive. Fixing the claim alone\n+does not kill the worker. This hypothesis is downstream, not upstream.\n+\n+### RC-skipped — Worktree file-lock (hypothesis 4)\n+\n+No evidence in bundles. Execute-bead worktrees are plain git worktrees at\n+`/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/*`; no process-held file locks would survive across 33–142h\n+without the owning process. If the process is alive, the lock is alive — same\n+as RC3.\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 3. Relationship to ddx-b808df39\n+\n+ddx-b808df39 proposes an operator `ddx agent workers stop <id>` command that\n+sends **SIGTERM at the OS level**, then SIGKILL after a grace period. That\n+design **does** work around RC1 (process-level signal bypasses the broken ctx\n+plumbing). So ddx-b808df39 is a *correct mitigation* — but only a mitigation.\n+\n+Without fixing RC1 (and at least RC2), operators will keep needing to invoke\n+`workers stop` manually. The autonomous remediations every other system relies\n+on (cancel ctx, idle timeout, circuit breaker) are each broken today in ways\n+that require explicit code changes, not just a new operator tool.\n+\n+**Recommendation:** Ship ddx-b808df39 first (quick operational relief), then\n+resolve the child beads filed below in order RC1 → RC2 → RC4 → RC3.\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 4. Follow-up beads filed\n+\n+Each addresses one root cause with a deterministic acceptance test. Priority\n+and ID are shown; full details available via `ddx bead show <id>`.\n+\n+The IDs below carry an `.execute-bead-wt-…` prefix because `ddx bead create`\n+was invoked from inside the isolated execute-bead worktree — this is a\n+separate DDx bug worth noting but orthogonal to the hang investigation. They\n+are parented to `ddx-0a651925` and resolvable by `ddx bead list --parent ddx-0a651925`.\n+\n+| RC  | Priority | Bead ID (suffix)                                                           | Title                                                              |\n+| --- | -------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------ |\n+| RC1 | P0       | `…-526efaf1` | propagate caller context through Runner.Run and RunAgent           |\n+| RC2 | P0       | `…-b203c889` | add wall-clock deadline alongside idle timeout for agent harness   |\n+| RC3 | P2       | `…-b0562380` | bound compaction-stuck breaker by wall-clock time                  |\n+| RC4 | P1       | `…-3e3913a0` | audit and enforce per-request HTTP deadlines on agent provider calls |\n+| —   | P1       | `…-34d039b7` | autonomous worker watchdog with process-level reaper (complements ddx-b808df39) |\n+\n+**Suggested execution order:** RC1 → RC2 → RC4 → RC3 → watchdog. RC1 is a\n+prerequisite for RC4 being meaningful.\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 5. What this investigation could NOT do\n+\n+- Capture live `ps`/`pprof` from a hung worker — none was accessible from the\n+  isolated execute-bead worktree. RC4 in particular needs a live `go tool pprof`\n+  goroutine dump to confirm that the stuck goroutine is in `net/http` streaming\n+  read, not some other path.\n+- Confirm whether the agent library's providers install any timeout that was\n+  missed in grep. The listed RC4 bead should start with a provider-path audit\n+  rather than an immediate fix.","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false}}

rationale: Bead ddx-9f4b1a8c (B22d-f) cannot be landed in isolation against base 4267be6c.

What is done
- The signature change itself is straightforward and verified locally:
  SessionIndexEntryFromResult(projectRoot string, harness, model, provider, effort string,
                              correlation map[string]string, result *Result,
                              startedAt, endedAt time.Time) SessionIndexEntry
  - cli/internal/agent/session_index.go: removed the RunOptions field/parameter
    and rewired the body to read from the discrete params (harness/model/provider/
    effort/correlation). After this change, RunOptions no longer appears in
    session_index.go (AC #1).
  - The single existing caller in cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:257
    (the bead's "four service-run callers" overcounts; only one call site exists)
    was updated to:
      SessionIndexEntryFromResult(workDir, opts.Harness, opts.Model,
                                  opts.Provider, opts.Effort, opts.Correlation,
                                  result, start, finishedAt)
  - cli/internal/agent/session_index_test.go (TestSessionIndexPreservesWorker-
    IDCorrelation, line ~195) was updated to call the new signature with
    discrete args. The test passes.
  - `cd cli && go build ./...` passes (AC #2).
  - `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/...` runs clean for everything that
    isn't TestIntegration_CodexEcho. That test fails for an unrelated reason
    (real codex CLI returns "thread not found") on this machine — it is not
    a regression introduced by this change, and -short does not skip it.

What is blocking
- The lefthook pre-commit `runtime-lint` rule (lefthook.yml) runs the
  SD-024 runtimelint analyzer and filters its diagnostics down to the set
  of staged files. Because the bead's in-scope files
  (cli/internal/agent/service_run.go, cli/internal/agent/session_index_test.go)
  contain many out-of-scope RunOptions usages — RunViaService,
  runFixtureHarnessViaRunner, RunWithConfigViaService, RunViaServiceWith,
  the `opts := RunOptions{...}` composite at service_run.go:82, and the
  TestRunViaServiceWithAppendsOneSessionIndexRow composite at
  session_index_test.go:274 — the lint blocks the commit even though none
  of those usages are touched by this bead.
- The bead's stated out-of-scope explicitly excludes "service_run.go public
  API deletions (step h)" (per the parent step-d bead description). Those
  call sites and signatures are what the lint is flagging.
- Running the analyzer over ./internal/agent/... reports many other
  RunOptions references across the package (compare_adapter.go,
  execute_bead.go, execute_bead_review.go, grade.go, runner.go,
  agent_runner.go, agent_runner_service.go, claude_stream.go, models.go,
  etc.) that depend on later B22d steps and the broader Stage-4 retirement.
- Per project policy, --no-verify / hook-skipping is forbidden; per the
  bead instructions, modifying out-of-scope files is forbidden. There is no
  in-scope way to make the lint pass on the staged-file set.

What a follow-up attempt would need
- Land step h (or the broader RunOptions retirement) FIRST so that
  service_run.go and session_index_test.go no longer contain pre-existing
  RunOptions usages, then this bead can run and the lint will pass on the
  staged-file set; OR
- Re-decompose the lint scope: change runtime-lint in lefthook.yml to
  filter diagnostics to only the staged hunk lines (e.g. via a diff-aware
  pass) rather than the whole staged file, so a partial migration step
  isn't blocked by file-level peers; OR
- Expand this bead's in-scope to include the full RunOptions removal from
  service_run.go and session_index_test.go (effectively folding step h into
  step f), and re-queue with a model that can carry the broader change.

The diff that was prepared and then reverted is reproducible from the bead
description in 5 minutes; the blocker is project-level lint policy versus
bead scope, not implementation difficulty.

Context: bead **ddx-bbb2d177**, which\n+was filed after v0.3.14 shipped the `sseCommentFilter` fix for bead\n+**agent-f237e07b** (upstream).\n+\n+## File format\n+\n+Each fixture is a JSONL file with two record kinds:\n+\n+- First line: `{\"kind\":\"meta\", ...}` — describes the capture and the assertion\n+  shape the replay test will enforce (expected status, substrings that must or\n+  must not appear in the assembled content / error).\n+- Remaining lines: `{\"kind\":\"frame\",\"body\":\"<raw SSE body>\"}` — one frame per\n+  record, written verbatim (including `\\n\\n` terminators) to the test HTTP\n+  response in arrival order.\n+\n+Frames include both `: keep-alive` SSE comment frames (the exact shape that\n+triggered the v0.3.13 \"unexpected end of JSON input\" regression) and normal\n+`data: {...}` chunks.\n+\n+## When to regenerate\n+\n+Regenerate fixtures only when real omlx server behavior changes in a way the\n+existing captures no longer represent — for example, a new reasoning-phase\n+frame shape, a header shift, or a chunk-boundary change. Do NOT regenerate\n+just to refresh timestamps.\n+\n+## How to regenerate\n+\n+Use `scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh`, which wraps `ddx agent run` with\n+`AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1` (shipped in ddx-agent v0.3.14) so the full\n+stream is captured to a sidecar JSONL, then reshapes it into the fixture\n+format:\n+\n+```bash\n+scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh \\\n+  --endpoint http://vidar:1235/v1 \\\n+  --model    Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit \\\n+  --prompt   \"Reply with the word ready.\" \\\n+  --name     happy-path \\\n+  --status   success \\\n+  --out      cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/happy_path.jsonl\n+```\n+\n+After the script writes the fixture, hand-edit the `meta.expected` block to\n+pin the `content_contains` / `error_contains` / `finish_reason` assertions for\n+the capture you just took — the script cannot infer those from the wire\n+bytes alone.\n+\n+## Why fixtures live here instead of the upstream agent repo\n+\n+The agent repo (`DocumentDrivenDX/agent`) has its own provider-level SSE\n+tests in `provider/openai/openai_test.go`. Those guard the SDK's handling of\n+SSE framing in isolation. The fixtures in *this* directory guard the DDx\n+consumer — `ddx work` → `Runner.RunAgent` → `agentlib.Run` — against both\n+SDK regressions (a bad ddx-agent bump) and DDx-side regressions (a header or\n+request-shape change that trips omlx's parser). The two layers cover\n+different failure modes and both matter.\ndiff --git a/cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/error_path.jsonl b/cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/error_path.jsonl\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 00000000..3dd1154f\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/error_path.jsonl\n@@ -0,0 +1,6 @@\n+{\"kind\":\"meta\",\"name\":\"error-path\",\"description\":\"omlx-style server sends role delta, a reasoning chunk, then a malformed JSON data frame. b/scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh\n@@ -0,0 +1,108 @@\n+#!/usr/bin/env bash\n+# capture-omlx-fixture.sh — regenerate a testdata/omlx-wire fixture from a\n+# live omlx endpoint. Wraps `ddx agent run` with AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1\n+# (shipped in ddx-agent v0.3.14 — bead agent-f237e07b) and reshapes the wire\n+# dump into the fixture JSONL format the consumer-side smoke test expects.\n+#\n+# Usage:\n+#   scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh \\\n+#     --endpoint http://vidar:1235/v1 \\\n+#     --model    Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit \\\n+#     --prompt   \"Reply with the word ready.\" \\\n+#     --out      cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/happy_path.jsonl\n+#\n+# Required tools: ddx (v0.6.0-alpha22+), jq.\n+#\n+# What the script does:\n+#   1. Runs an isolated ddx agent invocation against the given omlx endpoint\n+#      with AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE=1 AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1 so the whole\n+#      SSE stream is captured to a sidecar wire file.\n+#   2. Extracts every `dir==\"response\"` body chunk in arrival order and emits\n+#      one {\"kind\":\"frame\",\"body\":...} record per chunk.\n+#   3. Prepends a {\"kind\":\"meta\",...} record with the endpoint, model, prompt,\n+#      and a user-supplied expected outcome so the replay test knows what to\n+#      assert.\n+#\n+# Fixtures are checked in. Regenerate only when the omlx server changes wire\n+# behavior (new frame type, header shift, chunk-size change) in a way the\n+# existing fixtures no longer represent.\n+\n+set -euo pipefail\n+\n+endpoint=\"\"\n+model=\"\"\n+prompt=\"\"\n+out=\"\"\n+name=\"\"\n+status=\"success\"\n+\n+while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do\n+  case \"$1\" in\n+    --endpoint) endpoint=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n+    --model)    model=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n+    --prompt)   prompt=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n+    --out)      out=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n+    --name)     name=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n+    --status)   status=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;  # success | error\n+    -h|--help)\n+      sed -n '2,28p' \"$0\"\n+      exit 0\n+      ;;\n+    *) echo \"unknown arg: $1\" >&2; exit 2 ;;\n+  esac\n+done\n+\n+for v in endpoint model prompt out; do\n+  if [[ -z \"${!v}\" ]]; then\n+    echo \"missing required --$v\" >&2\n+    exit 2\n+  fi\n+done\n+\n+command -v jq >/dev/null || { echo \"jq is required\" >&2; exit 2; }\n+command -v ddx >/dev/null || { echo \"ddx is required\" >&2; exit 2; }\n+\n+wire_file=\"$(mktemp -t omlx-wire.XXXXXX.jsonl)\"\n+trap 'rm -f \"$wire_file\"' EXIT\n+\n+echo \"capturing wire dump from $endpoint → $wire_file\" >&2\n+\n+AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE=1 \\\n+AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1 \\\n+AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_FILE=\"$wire_file\" \\\n+ddx agent run \\\n+  --harness agent \\\n+  --provider vidar-omlx \\\n+  --model \"$model\" \\\n+  --prompt-text \"$prompt\" \\\n+  --timeout 120s \\\n+  >/dev/null || {\n+    echo \"ddx agent run failed — fixture capture incomplete\" >&2\n+    echo \"wire dump (may still be useful): $wire_file\" >&2\n+    exit 1\n+  }\n+\n+[[ -z \"$name\" ]] && name=\"$(basename \"$out\" .jsonl)\"\n+\n+tmp_out=\"$(mktemp -t omlx-fixture.XXXXXX.jsonl)\"\n+trap 'rm -f \"$wire_file\" \"$tmp_out\"' EXIT\n+\n+# Meta record — expected outcome is a hint for the replay test. Authors may\n+# need to hand-edit content_contains / error_contains after inspecting the\n+# captured body.\n+jq -cn \\\n+  --arg name \"$name\" \\\n+  --arg desc \"Captured $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ) from $endpoint model=$model prompt=$prompt\" \\\n+  --arg status \"$status\" \\\n+  '{kind:\"meta\", name:$name, description:$desc,\n+    expected:{status:$status, error_must_not_contain:\"unexpected end of JSON input\"}}' \\\n+  >> \"$tmp_out\"\n+\n+# Frame records — every response chunk in capture order. AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE\n+# with STREAM_FULL=1 emits one {\"dir\":\"response\",\"body\":\"…\"} record per read.\n+jq -c 'select(.dir==\"response\" and (.body|type==\"string\") and (.body|length>0))\n+       | {kind:\"frame\", body:.body}' \\\n+  \"$wire_file\" >> \"$tmp_out\"\n+\n+mv \"$tmp_out\" \"$out\"\n+echo \"wrote $(wc -l < \"$out\") records to $out\" >&2","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"51e05227-4794-4fdf-b297-f2720148e2a0","uuid":"ad85bb2f-b87a-4555-85d6-b2e11af2638d","timestamp":"2026-04-18T04:31:26.286Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"diff --git a/cli/internal/agent/omlx_e2e_test.go b/cli/internal/agent/omlx_e2e_test.go\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 00000000..3438eff3\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/cli/internal/agent/omlx_e2e_test.go\n@@ -0,0 +1,230 @@\n+package agent\n+\n+import (\n+\t\"bufio\"\n+\t\"encoding/json\"\n+\t\"io\"\n+\t\"net/http\"\n+\t\"net/http/httptest\"\n+\t\"os\"\n+\t\"path/filepath\"\n+\t\"strings\"\n+\t\"testing\"\n+\t\"time\"\n+\n+\t\"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent/provider/openai\"\n+\t\"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert\"\n+\t\"github.com/stretchr/testify/require\"\n+)\n+\n+// omlxFixture is the parsed representation of a testdata/omlx-wire/*.jsonl file.\n+// Each fixture captures a recorded vidar-omlx SSE response plus the outcome the\n+// consumer must produce when it replays. Fixtures are authored either by hand\n+// (the checked-in happy/error paths) or regenerated by\n+// scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh against a live omlx endpoint.\n+type omlxFixture struct {\n+\tMeta   omlxFixtureMeta\n+\tFrames []string // raw SSE bodies, written verbatim in order\n+}\n+\n+type omlxFixtureMeta struct {\n+\tName        string              `json:\"name\"`\n+\tDescription string              `json:\"description\"`\n+\tExpected    omlxFixtureExpected `json:\"expected\"`\n+}\n+\n+type omlxFixtureExpected struct {\n+\t// Status is \"success\" or \"error\".\n+\tStatus string `json:\"status\"`\n+\t// ErrorMustNotContain is a substring that the stream error — if any — must\n+\t// not contain. This is the regression guard: \"unexpected end of JSON input\"\n+\t// is the exact signature of the v0.3.13 SSE comment-frame defect (bead\n+\t// agent-f237e07b) that v0.3.14's sseCommentFilter fixed.\n+\tErrorMustNotContain string `json:\"error_must_not_contain\"`\n+\t// ContentContains is a substring the assembled content must contain on the\n+\t// happy path.\n+\tContentContains string `json:\"content_contains\"`\n+\t// FinishReason is the expected finish reason on the happy path.\n+\tFinishReason string `json:\"finish_reason\"`\n+\t// ErrorContains is a substring the surfaced error must contain on the\n+\t// error path — asserts the error is propagated rather than swallowed.\n+\tErrorContains string `json:\"error_contains\"`\n+}\n+\n+// loadOMLXFixture reads a JSONL fixture from testdata/omlx-wire/. The first\n+// line is the {\"kind\":\"meta\", ...} record; remaining {\"kind\":\"frame\",\"body\":...}\n+// lines are the SSE bodies to stream in order.\n+func loadOMLXFixture(t *testing.T, path string) omlxFixture {\n+\tt.Helper()\n+\tf, err := os.Open(path) // #nosec G304 -- test fixtures are checked in.\n+\trequire.NoError(t, err, \"open fixture %s\", path)\n+\tt.Cleanup(func() { _ = f.Close() })\n+\n+\tvar fix omlxFixture\n+\tscanner := bufio.NewScanner(f)\n+\t// Default 64KB scanner buffer is tight for long reasoning lines.\n+\tscanner.Buffer(make([]byte, 64*1024), 1024*1024)\n+\tfirst := true\n+\tfor scanner.Scan() {\n+\t\tline := strings.TrimSpace(scanner.Text())\n+\t\tif line == \"\" {\n+\t\t\tcontinue\n+\t\t}\n+\t\tvar record struct {\n+\t\t\tKind string          `json:\"kind\"`\n+\t\t\tBody string          `json:\"body\"`\n+\t\t\tRaw  json.RawMessage `json:\"-\"`\n+\t\t}\n+\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &record); err != nil {\n+\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"parsing fixture line in %s: %v\", path, err)\n+\t\t}\n+\t\tswitch record.Kind {\n+\t\tcase \"meta\":\n+\t\t\tif !first {\n+\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"fixture %s: meta record must be the first non-empty line\", path)\n+\t\t\t}\n+\t\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &fix.Meta); err != nil {\n+\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"parsing meta in %s: %v\", path, err)\n+\t\t\t}\n+\t\tcase \"frame\":\n+\t\t\tfix.Frames = append(fix.Frames, record.Body)\n+\t\tdefault:\n+\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"fixture %s: unknown record kind %q\", path, record.Kind)\n+\t\t}\n+\t\tfirst = false\n+\t}\n+\trequire.NoError(t, scanner.Err(), \"scanning fixture %s\", path)\n+\trequire.NotEmpty(t, fix.Meta.Name, \"fixture %s: missing meta.name\", path)\n+\trequire.NotEmpty(t, fix.Frames, \"fixture %s: no frame records\", path)\n+\treturn fix\n+}\n+\n+// newOMLXReplayServer stands up an httptest.Server that behaves like a vidar-omlx\n+// /v1/chat/completions endpoint by replaying the fixture's frames verbatim,\n+// with a short inter-frame flush so the consumer's SSE decoder sees per-chunk\n+// arrivals rather than one batched blob.\n+func newOMLXReplayServer(t *testing.T, frames []string) *httptest.Server {\n+\tt.Helper()\n+\tsrv := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n+\t\t// Match omlx's advertised Content-Type exactly — the SSE comment-frame\n+\t\t// middleware in provider/openai activates only when Content-Type starts\n+\t\t// with text/event-stream.\n+\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"text/event-stream; charset=utf-8\")\n+\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Cache-Control\", \"no-cache\")\n+\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Transfer-Encoding\", \"chunked\")\n+\t\tflusher, _ := w.(http.Flusher)\n+\t\tfor _, body := range frames {\n+\t\t\tif _, err := io.WriteString(w, body); err != nil {\n+\t\t\t\treturn\n+\t\t\t}\n+\t\t\tif flusher != nil {\n+\t\t\t\tflusher.Flush()\n+\t\t\t}\n+\t\t\t// Small delay to reproduce per-frame arrival; long enough that the\n+\t\t\t// decoder cannot batch frames, short enough that the test stays\n+\t\t\t// under a second total.\n+\t\t\ttime.Sleep(5 * time.Millisecond)\n+\t\t}\n+\t}))\n+\tt.Cleanup(srv.Close)\n+\treturn srv\n+}\n+\n+// TestOMLXFixtureReplay_ConsumerEndToEnd drives a full ddx work orchestration\n+// path (Runner.RunAgent → agentlib.Run → openai.Provider.ChatStream) against an\n+// httptest double that replays recorded omlx SSE wire traces. This is the\n+// consumer-side smoke test for ddx-agent v0.3.14 — see bead ddx-bbb2d177.\n+//\n+// What this guards against:\n+//\n+//  1. A future ddx-agent bump reintroducing the SSE comment-frame bug (bead\n+//     agent-f237e07b): the happy-path fixture includes `: keep-alive\\n\\n`\n+//     frames between data frames and must NOT surface \"unexpected end of\n+//     JSON input\".\n+//  2. A DDx-side change breaking the omlx dispatch path (e.g., a header or\n+//     request-shape change that trips omlx's parser).\n+//  3. A silent-success regression where the stream is consumed without error\n+//     but the assembled content is empty or malformed — the happy-path assertion\n+//     pins both the final content and finish_reason.\n+//  4. Error-path regressions where a real model-side failure is swallowed by\n+//     the consumer — the error-path fixture emits a malformed data frame and\n+//     asserts the consumer surfaces the error with a specific signature (and\n+//     specifically NOT \"unexpected end of JSON input\", which would mean the\n+//     SSE filter regressed).\n+func TestOMLXFixtureReplay_ConsumerEndToEnd(t *testing.T) {\n+\tfixtureDir := filepath.Join(\"testdata\", \"omlx-wire\")\n+\tcases := []string{\"happy_path.jsonl\", \"error_path.jsonl\"}\n+\n+\tfor _, name := range cases {\n+\t\tname := name\n+\t\tt.Run(name, func(t *testing.T) {\n+\t\t\tfix := loadOMLXFixture(t, filepath.Join(fixtureDir, name))\n+\n+\t\t\tsrv := newOMLXReplayServer(t, fix.Frames)\n+\n+\t\t\tprovider := openai.New(openai.Config{\n+\t\t\t\tBaseURL: srv.URL + \"/v1\",\n+\t\t\t\tAPIKey:  \"test\",\n+\t\t\t\tModel:   \"Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit\",\n+\t\t\t\t// Flavor \"omlx\" pins the capability table to the one used in\n+\t\t\t\t// production for vidar-omlx routing — matches the real\n+\t\t\t\t// dispatch path.\n+\t\t\t\tFlavor: \"omlx\",\n+\t\t\t})\n+\n+\t\t\tr := NewRunner(Config{SessionLogDir: t.TempDir(), TimeoutMS: 10000})\n+\t\t\tr.LookPath = mockLookPath\n+\t\t\tr.AgentProvider = provider\n+\n+\t\t\t// A WorkDir prevents the agent from using the live worktree when\n+\t\t\t// it invokes its built-in read/write/bash tools during the loop.\n+\t\t\tresult, err := r.RunAgent(RunOptions{\n+\t\t\t\tHarness: \"agent\",\n+\t\t\t\tPrompt:  \"smoke test — omlx fixture replay\",\n+\t\t\t\tWorkDir: t.TempDir(),\n+\t\t\t})\n+\t\t\trequire.NoError(t, err, \"RunAgent must not return a plumbing error; failures are reported via result.ExitCode / result.Error\")\n+\n+\t\t\t// The regression signature is always disallowed — on BOTH paths.\n+\t\t\t// v0.3.13 would surface this even for well-formed streams when\n+\t\t\t// keep-alive comment frames were present.\n+\t\t\tif fix.Meta.Expected.ErrorMustNotContain != \"\" {\n+\t\t\t\tassert.NotContains(t, result.Error, fix.Meta.Expected.ErrorMustNotContain,\n+\t\t\t\t\t\"consumer surfaced the v0.3.13 SSE comment-frame regression signature — the sseCommentFilter in ddx-agent v0.3.14+ is missing or broken\")\n+\t\t\t}\n+\n+\t\t\tswitch fix.Meta.Expected.Status {\n+\t\t\tcase \"success\":\n+\t\t\t\tassert.Equal(t, 0, result.ExitCode, \"happy-path fixture must close cleanly; got error=%q\", result.Error)\n+\t\t\t\tassert.Empty(t, result.Error, \"happy-path must produce no error\")\n+\t\t\t\tif want := fix.Meta.Expected.ContentContains; want != \"\" {\n+\t\t\t\t\tassert.Contains(t, result.Output, want,\n+\t\t\t\t\t\t\"happy-path must assemble content from post-keep-alive data frames\")\n+\t\t\t\t}\n+\t\t\tcase \"error\":\n+\t\t\t\tassert.Equal(t, 1, result.ExitCode, \"error-path fixture must report failure via ExitCode=1\")\n+\t\t\t\tassert.NotEmpty(t, result.Error, \"error-path must surface a non-empty error\")\n+\t\t\t\tif want := fix.Meta.Expected.ErrorContains; want != \"\" {\n+\t\t\t\t\tassert.Contains(t, result.Error, want,\n+\t\t\t\t\t\t\"error-path must propagate the underlying parse error\")\n+\t\t\t\t}\n+\t\t\tdefault:\n+\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"fixture %s: unknown expected status %q\", name, fix.Meta.Expected.Status)\n+\t\t\t}\n+\t\t})\n+\t}\n+}\n+\n+// TestOMLXFixtureReplay_FixturesExist is a cheap guardrail that fails fast if\n+// the testdata/omlx-wire directory is emptied or renamed. The full replay test\n+// above would also fail in that case, but a dedicated assertion surfaces the\n+// structural problem without the noise of a streaming-test stack trace.\n+func TestOMLXFixtureReplay_FixturesExist(t *testing.T) {\n+\tfor _, name := range []string{\"happy_path.jsonl\", \"error_path.jsonl\"} {\n+\t\tp := filepath.Join(\"testdata\", \"omlx-wire\", name)\n+\t\tinfo, err := os.Stat(p)\n+\t\trequire.NoError(t, err, \"missing fixture %s\", p)\n+\t\tassert.False(t, info.IsDir(), \"%s must be a file\", p)\n+\t\tassert.NotZero(t, info.Size(), \"%s must not be empty\", p)\n+\t}\n+}\ndiff --git a/cli/internal/agent/omlx_smoke_test.go b/cli/internal/agent/omlx_smoke_test.go\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 00000000..fd43c76e\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/cli/internal/agent/omlx_smoke_test.go\n@@ -0,0 +1,66 @@\n+//go:build omlx_smoke\n+\n+// Opt-in live smoke test for the ddx work → ddx-agent → vidar-omlx consumer\n+// path. Unlike the fixture-replay test in omlx_e2e_test.go (which ships in CI\n+// and uses a recorded test double), this test hits a real omlx endpoint and is\n+// intended for manual release verification against a live server. the consumer-side\n+end-to-end smoke test that guards `ddx work` against regressions on the\n+`ddx-agent → vidar-omlx` dispatch path. Context: bead **ddx-bbb2d177**, which\n+was filed after v0.3.14 shipped the `sseCommentFilter` fix for bead\n+**agent-f237e07b** (upstream).\n+\n+## File format\n+\n+Each fixture is a JSONL file with two record kinds:\n+\n+- First line: `{\"kind\":\"meta\", ...}` — describes the capture and the assertion\n+  shape the replay test will enforce (expected status, substrings that must or\n+  must not appear in the assembled content / error).\n+- Remaining lines: `{\"kind\":\"frame\",\"body\":\"<raw SSE body>\"}` — one frame per\n+  record, written verbatim (including `\\n\\n` terminators) to the test HTTP\n+  response in arrival order.\n+\n+Frames include both `: keep-alive` SSE comment frames (the exact shape that\n+triggered the v0.3.13 \"unexpected end of JSON input\" regression) and normal\n+`data: {...}` chunks.\n+\n+## When to regenerate\n+\n+Regenerate fixtures only when real omlx server behavior changes in a way the\n+existing captures no longer represent — for example, a new reasoning-phase\n+frame shape, a header shift, or a chunk-boundary change. Those guard the SDK's handling of\n+SSE framing in isolation. The fixtures in *this* directory guard the DDx\n+consumer — `ddx work` → `Runner.RunAgent` → `agentlib.Run` — against both\n+SDK regressions (a bad ddx-agent bump) and DDx-side regressions (a header or\n+request-shape change that trips omlx's parser). b/scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh\n@@ -0,0 +1,108 @@\n+#!/usr/bin/env bash\n+# capture-omlx-fixture.sh — regenerate a testdata/omlx-wire fixture from a\n+# live omlx endpoint. Wraps `ddx agent run` with AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1\n+# (shipped in ddx-agent v0.3.14 — bead agent-f237e07b) and reshapes the wire\n+# dump into the fixture JSONL format the consumer-side smoke test expects.\n+#\n+# Usage:\n+#   scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh \\\n+#     --endpoint http://vidar:1235/v1 \\\n+#     --model    Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit \\\n+#     --prompt   \"Reply with the word ready.\" \\\n+#     --out      cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/happy_path.jsonl\n+#\n+# Required tools: ddx (v0.6.0-alpha22+), jq.\n+#\n+# What the script does:\n+#   1. Runs an isolated ddx agent invocation against the given omlx endpoint\n+#      with AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE=1 AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1 so the whole\n+#      SSE stream is captured to a sidecar wire file.\n+#   2. Extracts every `dir==\"response\"` body chunk in arrival order and emits\n+#      one {\"kind\":\"frame\",\"body\":...} record per chunk.\n+#   3. Prepends a {\"kind\":\"meta\",...} record with the endpoint, model, prompt,\n+#      and a user-supplied expected outcome so the replay test knows what to\n+#      assert.\n+#\n+# Fixtures are checked in. Regenerate only when the omlx server changes wire\n+# behavior (new frame type, header shift, chunk-size change) in a way the\n+# existing fixtures no longer represent.\n+\n+set -euo pipefail\n+\n+endpoint=\"\"\n+model=\"\"\n+prompt=\"\"\n+out=\"\"\n+name=\"\"\n+status=\"success\"\n+\n+while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do\n+  case \"$1\" in\n+    --endpoint) endpoint=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n+    --model)    model=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n+    --prompt)   prompt=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n+    --out)      out=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n+    --name)     name=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n+    --status)   status=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;  # success | error\n+    -h|--help)\n+      sed -n '2,28p' \"$0\"\n+      exit 0\n+      ;;\n+    *) echo \"unknown arg: $1\" >&2; exit 2 ;;\n+  esac\n+done\n+\n+for v in endpoint model prompt out; do\n+  if [[ -z \"${!v}\" ]]; then\n+    echo \"missing required --$v\" >&2\n+    exit 2\n+  fi\n+done\n+\n+command -v jq >/dev/null || { echo \"jq is required\" >&2; exit 2; }\n+command -v ddx >/dev/null || { echo \"ddx is required\" >&2; exit 2; }\n+\n+wire_file=\"$(mktemp -t omlx-wire.XXXXXX.jsonl)\"\n+trap 'rm -f \"$wire_file\"' EXIT\n+\n+echo \"capturing wire dump from $endpoint → $wire_file\" >&2\n+\n+AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE=1 \\\n+AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1 \\\n+AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_FILE=\"$wire_file\" \\\n+ddx agent run \\\n+  --harness agent \\\n+  --provider vidar-omlx \\\n+  --model \"$model\" \\\n+  --prompt-text \"$prompt\" \\\n+  --timeout 120s \\\n+  >/dev/null || {\n+    echo \"ddx agent run failed — fixture capture incomplete\" >&2\n+    echo \"wire dump (may still be useful): $wire_file\" >&2\n+    exit 1\n+  }\n+\n+[[ -z \"$name\" ]] && name=\"$(basename \"$out\" .jsonl)\"\n+\n+tmp_out=\"$(mktemp -t omlx-fixture.XXXXXX.jsonl)\"\n+trap 'rm -f \"$wire_file\" \"$tmp_out\"' EXIT\n+\n+# Meta record — expected outcome is a hint for the replay test. Authors may\n+# need to hand-edit content_contains / error_contains after inspecting the\n+# captured body.\n+jq -cn \\\n+  --arg name \"$name\" \\\n+  --arg desc \"Captured $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ) from $endpoint model=$model prompt=$prompt\" \\\n+  --arg status \"$status\" \\\n+  '{kind:\"meta\", name:$name, description:$desc,\n+    expected:{status:$status, error_must_not_contain:\"unexpected end of JSON input\"}}' \\\n+  >> \"$tmp_out\"\n+\n+# Frame records — every response chunk in capture order. AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE\n+# with STREAM_FULL=1 emits one {\"dir\":\"response\",\"body\":\"…\"} record per read.\n+jq -c 'select(.dir==\"response\" and (.body|type==\"string\") and (.body|length>0))\n+       | {kind:\"frame\", body:.body}' \\\n+  \"$wire_file\" >> \"$tmp_out\"\n+\n+mv \"$tmp_out\" \"$out\"\n+echo \"wrote $(wc -l < \"$out\") records to $out\" >&2","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false}}

.ddx/attachments/ddx-6860e1ea/events.jsonl:2:{"actor":"opus-reviewer","body":"Opus detailed review of e55536e — most critical bead in this batch because subscriptions have classic silent-failure modes.

TRANSPORT WIRING: server.go:3478-3485 registers POST{} + GET{} + Websocket{} on the gqlgen handler. Websocket uses gorilla/websocket.Upgrader allowing all origins (dev) with 30s KeepAlivePingInterval. GET /graphql route registered at server.go:428, so WebSocket upgrade handshake actually reaches the gqlgen handler. gorilla/websocket promoted to direct dep in go.mod.

RESOLVER (resolver_sub_worker.go:20):
- ProgressSubscriber interface at L13: SubscribeProgress(workerID) (<-chan agent.ProgressEvent, func()) — WorkerManager satisfies natively (workers.go:623, exact signature, no shim)
- Calls r.Workers.SubscribeProgress(workerID) to get source channel + unsub cleanup
- Goroutine fans events from src to buffered out chan *WorkerEvent (cap 16), maps Phase/Message/BeadID/TS/EventID/WorkerID to GraphQL shape
- DUAL ctx.Done() select arms: one on receive (L33), one on send (L55). On cancel OR source close, goroutine returns; defer unsub(); defer close(out) fire. No leak.
- Returns (out, nil) to gqlgen

INTEGRATION TEST (graphql_subscription_worker_test.go, 231 lines): REAL WebSocket. httptest.NewServer wraps a test handler with exact same gqlgen+Websocket transport config (mirrors server.go). websocket.DefaultDialer.Dial with graphql-transport-ws subprotocol. Full handshake: connection_init -> connection_ack, subscribe with query payload, reads next frames, handles ping/pong keepalive, waits for complete. Verifies all 3 stub events arrive in order with correct phase/logLine/beadID. This is NOT a fake — actually exercises WebSocket transport and graphql-transport-ws protocol over real TCP socket.

EVENT SOURCE: production wiring at server.go:3474 passes s.workers (live *WorkerManager) as Workers field on resolver. WorkerManager.SubscribeProgress (workers.go:619) is real pub/sub via handle.progressSubs mutex-protected append + drainProgress fan-out. Not polling.

Reviewed and approved by user before any implementation beads are filed from the roadmap.","claimed-at":"2026-05-03T02:14:24Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"4025192","closing_commit_sha":"fabf513096b522e921020e323ef1bf4a9cf36afa","created_at":"2026-05-03T01:43:27.404796076Z","description":"Operator-observed gap: when ddx-server restarts, in-flight worker subprocesses lose their connection to the orchestrator. The current model has two execution paths (--local vs server-spawned) that diverge in lifecycle management, configuration plumbing (the dropped-flags class — see ddx-29058e2a), and observability (server can see only what it spawned).

PROPOSED ARCHITECTURAL SHIFT
WORKERS ARE LONG-LIVED PROCESSES THAT PHONE HOME TO THE SERVER VIA API. --local mode collapses to "worker registers with itself" using an in-process implementation of the same API. Same code path, same flags, same lifecycle.

THE ADR EVALUATES AND CHOOSES AMONG

PROPOSAL A — Server orchestrates, workers are long-lived clients
- Worker subprocess starts (either via `ddx work` CLI or via server-spawned)
- Worker registers with server (via /api/workers/register, gets worker-id + heartbeat token)
- Worker polls /api/workers/<id>/next-bead via long-poll or websocket; receives bead spec from server
- Worker reports progress via /api/workers/<id>/event {kind, body}
- Worker reports completion via /api/workers/<id>/result {bead-id, status, evidence}
- If server restarts: worker's heartbeat fails → worker enters "disconnected" state, finishes current bead, reconnects on server return
- If worker dies: server's heartbeat-timeout reclaims the bead

PROPOSAL B — Server is optional sidecar, worker is autonomous
- Worker runs independently; no server required
- If a server is reachable (via .ddx/server.addr), worker emits events to it best-effort
- Server is a passive observer, not an orchestrator
- Restart-safe by construction (worker doesn't depend on server)
- Loses: server-driven label filters, dynamic priority overrides, centralized cancel

PROPOSAL C — Hybrid: workers are autonomous executors but yield orchestration to server when present
- Worker checks for .ddx/server.addr at startup
- If present: registers as managed; receives bead assignments from server's queue
- If absent or unreachable: runs autonomously using local bead store
- Server restart: managed workers detect via heartbeat failure; gracefully complete current bead, then either retry registration OR fall back to autonomous mode (configurable)

UNIFIED API CONTRACT (all proposals)

POST /api/workers/register { project_root, harness, model_pref, label_filter, capabilities[] } → { worker_id, session_token, heartbeat_interval_ms }
POST /api/workers/<id>/heartbeat { state: idle|claiming|executing|reviewing } → { server_command: continue|pause|drain|terminate }
GET  /api/workers/<id>/next-bead → { bead, attempt_id, base_rev } | { wait_for_seconds: N }
POST /api/workers/<id>/event { kind, body, bead_id } → 204
POST /api/workers/<id>/result { bead_id, attempt_id, status, evidence_dir, commit_sha } → 204
POST /api/workers/<id>/disconnect → 204 (graceful)

The existing --local mode collapses to: a worker that registers with itself (no server) using an in-process implementation of the API. Same code path, same flags, same lifecycle.

OUTSTANDING BEADS THIS ADR MUST RECONCILE (overlap analysis required in the ADR output)

ABSORBED (implementation subsumed by the new architecture; ADR explicitly closes these as superseded once roadmap lands):
- ddx-29058e2a (ExecuteLoopSpec unification): becomes the API request/response payload — the spec IS the wire format. Refactor concern dissolves.
- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive): ALREADY FIXED at commit 41cb762e via flat 30s poll-interval default + attempted-map reset. Whether the new architecture preserves these tactical fixes or replaces them via API-driven semantics needs an ADR decision.
- ddx-cfedee8e (escalation: wire ladder): the ladder becomes server-side orchestration logic, not loop-body logic. ADR must specify where ladder lives in the new shape.

CONFLICTS WITH IN-FLIGHT REFACTOR (refactor epic ddx-5cb6e6cd children) — ADR must resolve sequencing:
- ddx-c8f79963 (C5: no_changes adjudication move into try.Attempt): refactors the SAME code that the API transition refactors. Either land C5 first then transition, OR fold C5 into the transition.
- ddx-06eb05d8 (C7: uniform Guard contract + delete attempted/hookFailed maps): same surface; same choice.
- ddx-848069a3 (C8: routing preflight to Drain startup): related to "server-side preflight" decision in API design.
- ddx-9228a484 (C9: StopCondition + cost-cap, absorbs ddx-cfedee8e and ddx-dc157075): heavy overlap. ADR must decide whether StopCondition becomes a server-side concept, a worker-side concept, or both.
- ddx-387a0178 (C13: file/identifier rename + ddx try CLI command): the ADR's new package layout (cli/internal/agent/work/) may conflict with C13's rename plan. Coordinate.
- ddx-1d867ec1 (rename execute_bead_loop.go): same as C13.

PARALLEL-SAFE (no conflict; ADR notes them but doesn't reshape):
- ddx-5b3e57f4 (cooldown: distinguish disrupted from gave-up): the API contract makes disruption naturally observable (server sees worker disconnect → marks Disrupted). But the cooldown logic itself is independent. ADR should call out that the API design enables cleaner Disrupted classification.
- ddx-1e516bc9 (fizeau Execute upstream gap): independent of API design.
- ddx-4c51d33e LAYER 1, ddx-055e8d32 LAYER 2, ddx-5ae050dc LAYER 3 (GraphQL project scoping): all in cli/internal/server/graphql/. Independent of worker API.
- ddx-9d55601f (picker bug): already fixed.
- ddx-a946c744 + ddx-aee651ec + ddx-3e60fd84 + ddx-83440482 (REACH/checks): hook into pre_merge gate; needs to integrate with the new worker contract but doesn't block ADR.
- All Story 6/14/15/16 frontend + backend feature beads: independent.

SPEC UPDATES THIS ADR DRIVES (the ADR roadmap must include these as deliverables)

- New ADR document: docs/helix/03-decide/ADR-XXX-worker-client-server-architecture.md
- FEAT-006 (Agent Service) — major amendment: worker API contract section, --local-collapses-to-API explanation, deprecation of direct execution paths
- FEAT-010 (Executions) — amendment: worker lifecycle now spans across server restarts; "execution" becomes a bead-attempt regardless of which worker performs it
- SD-024 (Resolved Config / CLIOverrides) — possible amendment: if ResolvedConfig becomes part of the API request payload, SD-024 reshapes
- TD-030 (Backend interface + chaos contract) — cross-reference if any
- CONTRACT-003 (Fizeau Service) — no change required (fizeau is below the worker; the API is between worker and ddx-server, not worker and fizeau)

NOT IN SCOPE FOR THIS ADR
- Specific code changes (this TD documents the design; implementation is follow-up beads from the roadmap)
- Multi-server federation (each server owns its workers; cross-server is a separate concern)
- Auth beyond session tokens (the worker-server channel is local; no remote workers in v1)
- Reshaping the bead schema (out of scope; ADR-004 owns that)

OUTPUTS (TD deliverables)
- ADR document at docs/helix/03-decide/ADR-XXX-worker-client-server-architecture.md
- Updated FEAT-006 (or proposed amendments listed)
- Sequence diagrams for: register, claim, execute, restart-recovery, graceful-shutdown
- Compatibility table: which existing tests/behaviors break, which migrate cleanly
- Implementation roadmap: ordered list of beads to file as follow-ups
- Overlap reconciliation table: each overlapping bead (above) classified as ABSORBED / CONFLICT-RESOLVED / PARALLEL-SAFE with explicit rationale

SEQUENCING DIRECTIVE (per user direction, 2026-05-03)
- Should land BEFORE major refactor children that touch the loop body (C5, C7, C9) — otherwise they'd refactor against the OLD model and need re-doing
- Should NOT block routine bug fixes or feature beads
- C5/C7/C9 explicitly FROZEN until the ADR's roadmap establishes the new package layout","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"claude","fallback_chain":[],"requested_harness":"claude"}","created_at":"2026-05-03T02:18:38.009514865Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=claude"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"attempt_id":"20260503T021424-dac100b6","harness":"claude","input_tokens":21,"output_tokens":14799,"total_tokens":14820,"cost_usd":1.0767455,"duration_ms":249728,"exit_code":0}","created_at":"2026-05-03T02:18:38.0358447Z","kind":"cost","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=14820 cost_usd=1.0767"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"escalation_count":0,"fallback_chain":[],"final_tier":"","requested_profile":"","requested_tier":"","resolved_model":"","resolved_provider":"claude","resolved_tier":""}","created_at":"2026-05-03T02:18:43.263235583Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"provider=claude"},{"actor":"erik","body":"APPROVE

Affected functions (per audit):
- escalation.Ladder.Next (cli/internal/agent/escalation/ladder.go) — added by S10 ladder work, no caller
- EvaluateRetryPolicy (cli/internal/agent/retry_policy.go) — added by recent retry work, no caller
- RunWithLockBackoff (cli/internal/agent/triage_dispatch.go or similar) — added by ddx-da11a34a lock contention, no caller
- DispatchPostAttempt — added by ddx-aede917d or sibling, no caller

For each: decide wire vs delete based on whether the BEAD that added it is still relevant (its acceptance criteria still describes desired behavior) OR whether subsequent design changes have made it obsolete.

Recommended decision: **WIRE all four**. The beads that added them are recently-landed and the acceptance criteria are still relevant. The unwired state is a process failure (the implementer wrote and tested the code but didn't replace the old code path). Wiring is the right fix; delete only if the underlying behavior is genuinely obsolete (none of these are).

After wiring, verify with: drain runs and observes the new code paths firing in execution evidence. This bead closes as 'investigation complete; see child beads for fixes'. ddx-b808df39 references this bead's findings in its implementation notes.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-18T04:31:48Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1833233","closing_commit_sha":"1d0b3e373a618623062909694332536c74f2630a","created_at":"2026-04-18T03:01:36.338860593Z","description":"## Context

ddx-b808df39 (P0: ddx agent stop/kill command for hung workers) treats hung-worker remediation as an operational need, but does NOT investigate the UNDERLYING cause of the hangs. The feature is a useful escape hatch, but shipping it without understanding WHY workers hang means we'll keep needing the escape hatch.

Opus fresh-eyes review (2026-04-18) flagged: 'Hung workers for 33h–142h are an operational defect independent of root cause' — the feature bead is justified, but a SIBLING root-cause investigation is missing from the portfolio.

## Observed

The referenced 33h and 142h hang durations in ddx-b808df39 imply multiple incidents where an execute-bead worker process remained in-progress past any reasonable upper bound for a single bead. Needs to investigate:

1. Is the worker process alive but stuck in a tool call (bash subprocess that never returns)?
2. Is it alive but mid-LLM-call with no timeout (provider hang + no client-side deadline)?
3. Is it dead but the bead's claimed_at never cleared (tracker-side leak)?
4. Is the worktree locked or fsync'd in a way that prevents cleanup?

## Fix direction

This bead is an INVESTIGATION, not a fix. Deliverable is a short report that:

1. Reproduces or observes at least one hang incident and captures:
   - ps / pgrep state of the worker process tree
   - stack traces via 'go tool pprof' against the worker (if alive)
   - Timeline from .ddx/executions/<attempt>/embedded/*.jsonl (last event type + timestamp)
   - tracker claimed_at and retry_after state

2. Classifies the hang into one of the four categories above (or a new category).

3. Files concrete follow-up beads for each identified root cause:
   - Tool subprocess timeout if bash hangs
   - LLM client deadline if provider hangs
   - Claim expiry / heartbeat if tracker-side leak
   - File-lock / worktree cleanup if fs-side

4. Recommends whether ddx-b808df39's stop/kill mechanism is sufficient as mitigation or needs additional primitives.

## Relation to ddx-b808df39

ddx-b808df39 ships the OPERATOR TOOL to stop a hung worker. This bead finds the BUG that causes hangs. Both are needed; ship ddx-b808df39 first if the operator-tool is more urgent, then this investigation.

## Non-goals

- Fixing the root cause in this bead. Scope is investigation + follow-up bead filing.
- Preventing all possible hangs (impossible). Scope is the actually-observed hang Integration tests for the JWT auth middleware (ADR-018 §4).\n//!\n//! Builds a minimal axum router with a single protected data-plane route:\n//!   GET /tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\n//!\n//! Tests the four golden paths required by the bead:\n//! 1. No auth header → 401 unauthenticated\n//! 2. Valid JWT with correct grants → 200\n//! 3. Valid JWT with wrong tenant in aud → 403 credential_wrong_tenant\n//! 4. Valid JWT with grants for a different database → 403 database_not_granted\n\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\n\nuse axum::body::Body;\nuse axum::http::StatusCode;\nuse axum::routing::get;\nuse axum::Router;\nuse http::Request;\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\nuse axon_core::auth::{\n    GrantedDatabase, Grants, JwtClaims, Op, TenantId, TenantMember, TenantRole, User, UserId,\n};\nuse axon_server::auth_pipeline::{AuthPipelineState, InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer, jwt_verify_layer};\nuse axon_storage::MemoryStorageAdapter;\n\nconst ISSUER: &str = \"integration-issuer\";\nconst TENANT: &str = \"acme\";\nconst DATABASE: &str = \"orders\";\nconst USER_ID: &str = \"u-integration-01\";\nconst SECRET: &[u8] = b\"integration-test-secret\";\nconst PROTECTED_PATH: &str = \"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\";\n\nfn now_secs() -> u64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_secs()\n}\n\nfn make_state() -> Arc<AuthPipelineState> {\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\n    storage.insert_user(User {\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        display_name: \"Integration User\".to_string(),\n        email: None,\n        created_at_ms: 0,\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\n    });\n    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\n    });\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\n        issuer: Arc::new(JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER.to_string())),\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(\n            Box::new(storage) as Box<dyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync>\n        )),\n    })\n}\n\nfn make_app(state: Arc<AuthPipelineState>) -> Router {\n    Router::new()\n        .route(PROTECTED_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            state,\n            jwt_verify_layer,\n        ))\n}\n\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request<Body>) -> (StatusCode, Value) {\n    let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\"service should not fail\");\n    let status = resp.status();\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\n        .await\n        .unwrap_or_default();\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n    (status, json)\n}\n\nfn bearer(token: &str) -> String {\n    format!(\"Bearer {token}\")\n}\n\nfn valid_token(issuer: &JwtIssuer, grants: Grants) -> String {\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let claims = JwtClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants,\n    };\n    issuer.issue(&claims).expect(\"issue should succeed\")\n}\n\nfn orders_read_grants() -> Grants {\n    Grants {\n        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n            name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n            ops: vec![Op::Read],\n        }],\n    }\n}\n\n// ── Test 1: No auth header → 401 unauthenticated ──────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_no_auth_header_returns_401() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(\n        json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n        \"unauthenticated\"\n    );\n}\n\n// ── Test 2: Valid JWT with read grants → 200 ──────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_valid_jwt_with_read_grant_returns_200() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let token = valid_token(&state.issuer, orders_read_grants());\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", bearer(&token))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, _) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::OK);\n}\n\n// ── Test 3: Wrong tenant in aud → 403 credential_wrong_tenant ─────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_wrong_tenant_returns_403() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let claims = JwtClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: \"different-tenant\".to_string(), // URL says \"acme\"\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants: orders_read_grants(),\n    };\n    let token = state.issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", bearer(&token))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status.as_u16(), 403);\n    assert_eq!(\n        json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n        \"credential_wrong_tenant\"\n    );\n}\n\n// ── Test 4: Grants for different database → 403 database_not_granted ──────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_wrong_database_returns_403() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let grants = Grants {\n        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n            name: \"invoices\".to_string(), // URL says \"orders\"\n            ops: vec![Op::Read],\n        }],\n    };\n    let token = valid_token(&state.issuer, grants);\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", bearer(&token))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status.as_u16(), 403);\n    assert_eq!(\n        json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n        \"database_not_granted\"\n    );\n}\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}

Embedded admin UI — assets from `ui/build` compiled into the binary.\n2\t//!\n3\t//! The `UiAssets` struct embeds every file under `ui/build` at compile time.\n4\t//! The axum handler serves them at `/ui/*path` with correct `Content-Type`\n5\t//! headers, and falls back to `index.html` for any unrecognised path so that\n6\t//! SvelteKit client-side routing works correctly.\n7\t//!\n8\t//! **Build prerequisite**: `ui/build` must exist before `cargo build`.\n9\t//! Run `cd ui && bun run build` (or `npm run build`) first.\n10\t\n11\tuse axum::body::Body;\n12\tuse axum::http::{header, StatusCode, Uri};\n13\tuse axum::response::{IntoResponse, Response};\n14\tuse rust_embed::RustEmbed;\n15\t\n16\t/// All files under `ui/build`, embedded at compile time.\n17\t///\n18\t/// The `#[folder]` path is relative to the crate root (`crates/axon-server/`),\n19\t/// so `../../ui/build` resolves to `ui/build` in the workspace root.\n20\t#[derive(RustEmbed)]\n21\t#[folder = \"../../ui/build\"]\n22\tstruct UiAssets;\n23\t\n24\t/// Axum handler for `GET /ui` and `GET /ui/*path`.\n25\t///\n26\t/// Strips the `/ui` prefix, looks up the file in [`UiAssets`], and responds\n27\t/// with the correct `Content-Type`.  Unknown paths fall back to `index.html`\n28\t/// so SvelteKit's client-side router can handle them.\n29\tpub async fn embedded_ui_handler(uri: Uri) -> Response {\n30\t    let raw = uri.path();\n31\t    // Strip the /ui prefix that axum leaves on the URI when not using nest_service.\n32\t    let asset_path = raw\n33\t        .strip_prefix(\"/ui/\")\n34\t        .or_else(|| raw.strip_prefix(\"/ui\"))\n35\t        .unwrap_or(raw);\n36\t    let asset_path = if asset_path.is_empty() {\n37\t        \"index.html\"\n38\t    } else {\n39\t        asset_path\n40\t    };\n41\t\n42\t    serve_asset(asset_path)\n43\t}\n44\t\n45\tfn serve_asset(path: &str) -> Response {\n46\t    match UiAssets::get(path) {\n47\t        Some(content) => {\n48\t            let mime = mime_guess::from_path(path).first_or_octet_stream();\n49\t            Response::builder()\n50\t                .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, mime.as_ref())\n51\t                .body(Body::from(content.data))\n52\t                .unwrap_or_else(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.into_response())\n53\t        }\n54\t        None => {\n55\t            // SPA fallback: let the client-side router handle unknown paths.\n56\t            match UiAssets::get(\"index.html\") {\n57\t                Some(index) => Response::builder()\n58\t                    .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \"text/html; charset=utf-8\")\n59\t                    .body(Body::from(index.data))\n60\t                    .unwrap_or_else(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.into_response()),\n61\t                None => StatusCode::NOT_FOUND.into_response(),\n62\t            }\n63\t        }\n64\t    }\n65\t}\n66\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2","uuid":"639a3f3a-e27d-4d23-b9e3-a0b9bc507e2b","timestamp":"2026-04-15T01:45:23.541Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-axon-dccbdf73-20260415T013041-1340da9f/crates/axon-server/src/embedded_ui.rs","content":"//! The axum handler serves them at `/ui/*path` with correct `Content-Type`\n//! headers, and falls back to `index.html` for any unrecognised path so that\n//! SvelteKit client-side routing works correctly.\n//!\n//! **Build prerequisite**: `ui/build` must exist before `cargo build`.\n//! Run `cd ui && bun run build` (or `npm run build`) first.\n\nuse axum::body::Body;\nuse axum::http::{header, StatusCode, Uri};\nuse axum::response::{IntoResponse, Response};\nuse rust_embed::RustEmbed;\n\n/// All files under `ui/build`, embedded at compile time.\n///\n/// The `#[folder]` path is relative to the crate root (`crates/axon-server/`),\n/// so `../../ui/build` resolves to `ui/build` in the workspace root.\n#[derive(RustEmbed)]\n#[folder = \"../../ui/build\"]\nstruct UiAssets;\n\n/// Axum handler for `GET /ui` and `GET /ui/*path`.\n///\n/// Strips the `/ui` prefix, looks up the file in [`UiAssets`], and responds\n/// with the correct `Content-Type`.  Unknown paths fall back to `index.html`\n/// so SvelteKit's client-side router can handle them.\npub async fn embedded_ui_handler(uri: Uri) -> Response {\n    let raw = uri.path();\n    // Strip the /ui prefix that axum leaves on the URI when not using nest_service.\n    let asset_path = raw\n        .strip_prefix(\"/ui/\")\n        .or_else(|| raw.strip_prefix(\"/ui\"))\n        .unwrap_or(raw);\n    let asset_path = if asset_path.is_empty() {\n        \"index.html\"\n    } else {\n        asset_path\n    };\n\n    serve_asset(asset_path)\n}\n\nfn serve_asset(path: &str) -> Response {\n    match UiAssets::get(path) {\n        Some(content) => {\n            let mime = mime_guess::from_path(path).first_or_octet_stream();\n            Response::builder()\n                .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, mime.as_ref())\n                .body(Body::from(content.data))\n                .unwrap_or_else(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.into_response())\n        }\n        None => {\n            // SPA fallback: let the client-side router handle unknown paths.\n            match UiAssets::get(\"index.html\") {\n                Some(index) => Response::builder()\n                    .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \"text/html; charset=utf-8\")\n                    .body(Body::from(index.data))\n                    .unwrap_or_else(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.into_response()),\n                None => StatusCode::NOT_FOUND.into_response(),\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n","numLines":66,"startLine":1,"totalLines":66}}}
