Unit tests for the JWT auth pipeline (ADR-018 §4).\n//!\n//! Covers:\n//! - Every row of the failure-mode table (14 variants)\n//! - JWT round-trip\n//! - ResolvedIdentity installation in request extensions\n//! - Revocation cache invalidation\n//! - Clock-skew boundary at ±30 s\n//! - HTTP-method × grant-ops matrix\n\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\n\nuse axum::body::Body;\nuse axum::extract::Extension;\nuse axum::http::StatusCode;\nuse axum::response::IntoResponse;\nuse axum::routing::get;\nuse axum::Router;\nuse http::Request;\nuse serde::Serialize;\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\nuse axon_core::auth::{\n    AuthError, GrantedDatabase, Grants, JwtClaims, Op, ResolvedIdentity, TenantId, TenantMember,\n    TenantRole, User, UserId,\n};\nuse axon_server::auth_pipeline::{\n    AuthErrorResponse, AuthPipelineState, InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer,\n    jwt_verify_layer,\n};\nuse axon_storage::MemoryStorageAdapter;\n\n// ── Test helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nconst ISSUER_ID: &str = \"test-issuer\";\nconst TENANT: &str = \"acme\";\nconst DATABASE: &str = \"orders\";\nconst USER_ID: &str = \"user-01\";\nconst SECRET: &[u8] = b\"super-secret-key-for-tests\";\nconst TEST_PATH: &str = \"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\";\n\nfn now_secs() -> u64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_secs()\n}\n\nfn valid_claims() -> JwtClaims {\n    let now = now_secs();\n    JwtClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER_ID.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants: Grants {\n            databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n                ops: vec![Op::Read, Op::Write],\n            }],\n        },\n    }\n}\n\nfn make_storage() -> MemoryStorageAdapter {\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\n    storage.insert_user(User {\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        display_name: \"Test User\".to_string(),\n        email: None,\n        created_at_ms: 0,\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\n    });\n    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\n    });\n    storage\n}\n\nfn make_state() -> Arc<AuthPipelineState> {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let storage: Box<dyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync> =\n        Box::new(make_storage());\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\n        issuer: Arc::new(issuer),\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(storage)),\n    })\n}\n\nfn make_app(state: Arc<AuthPipelineState>) -> Router {\n    Router::new()\n        .route(TEST_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            state,\n            jwt_verify_layer,\n        ))\n}\n\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request<Body>) -> (StatusCode, Value) {\n    let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\"service call should succeed\");\n    let status = resp.status();\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\n        .await\n        .expect(\"body read should succeed\");\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n    (status, json)\n}\n\nfn get_req(path: &str) -> Request<Body> {\n    Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(path)\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap()\n}\n\nfn bearer_req(path: &str, token: &str) -> Request<Body> {\n    Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(path)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {token}\"))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap()\n}\n\nfn error_code(json: &Value) -> &str {\n    json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\")\n}\n\n// ── JWT round-trip ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[test]\nfn jwt_roundtrip_succeeds() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let claims = valid_claims();\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).expect(\"issue should succeed\");\n    let decoded = issuer.verify(&token).expect(\"verify should succeed\");\n    assert_eq!(decoded.iss, claims.iss);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.sub, claims.sub);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.aud, claims.aud);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.jti, claims.jti);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.grants, claims.grants);\n}\n\n// ── ADR-018 §4 failure-mode table ─────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Row 1: No Authorization header → 401 unauthenticated\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_no_auth_header() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = get_req(TEST_PATH);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"unauthenticated\");\n}\n\n/// Row 2a: Header present but not `Bearer` → 401 credential_malformed\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_not_bearer_scheme() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .uri(TEST_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", \"Basic dXNlcjpwYXNz\")\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_malformed\");\n}\n\n/// Row 2b: JWT structurally invalid → 401 credential_malformed\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_jwt_malformed() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, \"not.a.valid.jwt\");\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_malformed\");\n}\n\n/// Row 2c: `aud` as JSON array → 401 credential_malformed\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_aud_is_array() {\n    #[derive(Serialize)]\n    struct ArrayAudClaims {\n        iss: String,\n        sub: String,\n        aud: Vec<String>,\n        jti: String,\n        iat: u64,\n        nbf: u64,\n        exp: u64,\n        grants: Grants,\n    }\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let bad_claims = ArrayAudClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER_ID.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: vec![TENANT.to_string()], // array, not string\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants: Grants {\n            databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n                ops: vec![Op::Read],\n            }],\n        },\n    };\n    let token = jsonwebtoken::encode(\n        &jsonwebtoken::Header::new(jsonwebtoken::Algorithm::HS256),\n        &bad_claims,\n        &jsonwebtoken::EncodingKey::from_secret(SECRET),\n    )\n    .unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_malformed\");\n}\n\n/// Row 3: Signature invalid → 401 credential_invalid\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_signature_invalid() {\n    let wrong_issuer = JwtIssuer::new(b\"wrong-secret\".to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let claims = valid_claims();\n    let token = wrong_issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app \"credential_invalid\");\n}\n\n/// Row 4: `exp` in past beyond skew → 401 credential_expired\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_exp_expired() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Base directory for this skill: /home/erik/.claude/skills/helix-commit\n\n# Commit: HELIX-Compliant Git Commit\n\nCreate a well-formatted commit with issue traceability, run the project's\nbuild gate, push, and optionally update the tracker.\n\n## When to Use\n\n- After implementing a tracked issue\n- When you need a properly formatted commit message\n- When codex or another agent needs commit support\n\n## What It Does\n\n1. **Inspect** staged changes (`git diff --cached --stat`)\n2. **Format** commit message:\n   - First line: `<issue-id> <concise summary>` (under 72 chars)\n   - Body: what changed and why, governing artifact refs\n   - Trailer: verification commands run\n3. **Pre-push gate**: run the project's build check before pushing\n   - `lefthook run pre-commit` if available\n   - `cargo check --workspace` for Rust projects\n   - `npm test` for Node.js projects\n4. **Commit and push**: `git commit`, `git pull --rebase`, `git push`\n5. **Tracker update** (if issue-id provided): close the issue\n\n## Commit Message Format\n\n```\nhx-abc123 Short summary of the change\n\nLonger description of what changed and why. Reference governing\nartifacts when relevant (SD-001, TP-002, etc.).\n\nVerification: cargo test -p changed-crate, cargo clippy\n```\n\n## Examples\n\n```bash\nhelix commit hx-abc123      # commit, gate, push, close issue\nhelix commit                 # commit and gate without tracker update\n```\n"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2","uuid":"389e7b5b-4f0d-4836-a072-b1bc977baa31","timestamp":"2026-04-15T01:49:25.805Z","isSynthetic":true}

.ddx/executions/20260415T012939-e548f96b/manifest.json:14:    "description": "Add a new DDx CLI subcommand `ddx agent check [--provider X]`. Semantic distinction from existing `ddx agent doctor`: doctor answers "is my config failed?" (config validation, malformed YAML, missing API keys); check answers "what agents can I use right now?" (runtime liveness, which providers respond, which models are currently available). Both commands coexist.

Implementation lives in cli/cmd/agent_check.go. Mirrors ddx-agent's `cmdCheck` — probes each configured provider's /v1/models endpoint, reports reachability and current model inventory, exits 0 if at least one provider is reachable and has at least one usable model, exits 1 otherwise. Uses the Go package, not shell-out.",

.ddx/executions/20260503T124553-282667f7/manifest.json:13:    "description": "Once REACH-PROTO + REACH-GO have landed and ddx self-installs the check, run ddx ac run --check production-reachability against the current cli/ tree and resolve every violation. This is the systematic application phase the user mandated: "go back through the codebase systematically applying it."

ABSORBS
- ddx-09d2990c (WIRE) — the 4 specific unwired functions become a subset of this sweep. Close WIRE as superseded by this bead at file time.
- Any clusters from /tmp/agent-surface-dead-code-audit.md not already covered.
- Codex audit additions: jsonlFallbackForCollection (cli/internal/bead/backend_jsonl.go:90), Store.breakStaleLock (lock.go:70), NewStoreWithBackend (store.go:119), WithSpokeHTTPClient (federation_spoke.go:103), Server.execStore (server.go:2028), GraphQL NewResolver (resolver.go:14).

EXPECTED WORKFLOW
1. Run ddx ac run --check production-reachability against the current worktree. Capture violation list.
2. For each violation, decide: WIRE (add to production code path) or DELETE (function is genuinely obsolete).
3. File one child bead per violation cluster (group by package or by originating story for traceability).
4. Drain the children. Each closes either via wiring PR or deletion PR.
5. Final pass: ddx ac run returns status=pass with no violations and no // wiring:pending annotations remaining (or all annotations cite still-open future beads).

DECISION RULE per violation
- If the symbol's originating bead's AC describes desired runtime behavior → WIRE.
- If the symbol was added speculatively or the design has moved on → DELETE; reopen the originating bead with obsolescence rationale, or close as obsolete.
- If neither is clear within 15 min of investigation → annotate // wiring:pending <follow-up-bead> and file the follow-up.

.ddx/executions/20260503T124415-1a86a675/prompt.md:102:+    "description": "Ship the Go-specific production-reachability check as a library resource conforming to the REACH-PROTO check protocol. NOT installed by default for arbitrary projects (Go-opinion would leak through ddx init). This project (ddx itself) opts in by adding .ddx/checks/production-reachability.yaml.

DESIGN
- Location: library/checks/go-production-reachability/ (alongside library/personas/, library/patterns/)
- Implementation: a small Go binary or shell script invoking golang.org/x/tools/cmd/deadcode rooted at configurable entry packages.
- Default entry roots for ddx self-use: cli/cmd/..., cli/internal/server/graphql/..., cli/internal/server/http/...
- Logic:
  1. Read DIFF_BASE..DIFF_HEAD from env. Compute set of new Go symbols (functions, methods, types) added by the diff via AST parse of changed files.
  2. Run deadcode (or callgraph) rooted at the configured entry packages, EXCLUDING _test.go from roots and from reachability.
  3. For each new symbol not reachable from any entry root → violation { file, line, symbol, kind: "unreachable", detail: "no path from entry roots: <list>" }

.ddx/executions/20260503T021724-8a33226e/manifest.json:13:    "description": "The current cooldown logic treats any ExecutionFailed status as evidence the model can't make progress, applying noProgressCooldown=6h when BaseRev == ResultRev (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1074-1086 via shouldSuppressNoProgress at line 1550). This silently freezes important work whenever a worker is disrupted (killed, server restart, network blip, OS issue) — disruption produces ExecutionFailed + no commit, indistinguishable from "model genuinely tried and produced nothing."

OBSERVED (today, 2026-05-02)
After the picker fix investigation found the four "starved" P0 beads (ddx-dc157075, ddx-aee651ec, ddx-29058e2a, ddx-9d55601f) all had execute-loop-retry-after timestamps ~3 hours in the future. Investigation per ddx-9d55601f confirmed these were NOT genuine model failures — they were prior worker disruptions during this development session (worker kills, server restarts) being treated as model-gave-up.

User directive: "Cooldown should be reserved for cases where the model is truly unable to make progress on a task."

CURRENT COOLDOWN POLICY (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go)
- LandConflictCooldown = 15min — short cooldown after merge/land conflicts (line 64)
- noProgressCooldown = 6h default — when shouldSuppressNoProgress is true (line 367)
- MaxLoopCooldown = 24h — push_failed, push_conflict, decomposition decline (line 57)

shouldSuppressNoProgress (line 1550): returns true if BaseRev != "" AND ResultRev != "" AND BaseRev == ResultRev. This catches:
- Model wrote nothing AND came back cleanly (true no_progress) ✓
- Worker killed mid-execution before commit (FALSE no_progress — disruption)
- Network/provider error before model started (FALSE no_progress — disruption)
- Routing preflight rejection (FALSE no_progress — config issue)

The mis-classification is the bug.

PROPOSED FIX

Add a Disposition::Disrupted variant (or equivalent flag on ExecuteBeadReport) that explicitly marks results that are NOT evidence of model inability:
- ctx.Err() != nil (cancellation, deadline, parent died)
- syscall.SIGTERM / SIGINT received during execution
- Provider-side 5xx, connection refused, timeout (transport disruption)
- Worker process killed (parent never received clean result)
- Server-restart-detected marker
- Routing preflight rejection (operator config issue, not model failure)

For Disrupted: skip cooldown entirely (or use a tiny defer like 30s) and unclaim the bead so the next worker pickup is immediate.
For genuine no-progress (model returned clean with no commit AND rationale present): keep current 6h cooldown.

ALTERNATIVE: exponential backoff per bead. First failure: 5min; second: 30min; third: 2h; cap at 24h. Resets on any successful attempt or operator clear. This is more forgiving than the current flat 6h on the first hiccup.

PROBABLY BOTH: Disrupted skips cooldown entirely; non-Disrupted failures use exponential backoff starting at 5min, not flat 6h.

DETECTION POINTERS

- ctx.Err() check at the call site that produces ExecutionFailed (line 513, 629, 637, 1011)
- Check for `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/no_changes_rationale.txt` presence — if missing, the model never wrote one (likely disruption)
- Check the executor's exit code: SIGKILL/SIGTERM = disruption; clean exit with empty result = no-progress
- Track parent process restart count or boot ID; if changed since the attempt started, mark Disrupted

NOT IN SCOPE
- Changing the cooldown duration constants themselves
- Reworking the per-bead retry policy framework (separate concern)
- Changing land-conflict or push-failed cooldowns (those are correct as-is)
- The exponential-backoff alternative is OPTIONAL; primary fix is Disrupted classification

INTERSECTION WITH OTHER BEADS
- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive): the stay-alive fix prevents some classes of disruption (worker no longer exits on empty poll), but does NOT fix the cooldown-on-disruption mis-classification
- ddx-076147ee (worker client-server architecture TD): if workers become long-lived clients, server-restart disruption goes away by construction. But this fix is needed BEFORE that TD is implemented because the disruption classification is needed regardless.
- ddx-9228a484 C9 (StopCondition enum): may absorb this — the StopCondition enum could include Disrupted as a state. Verify and align.",
.ddx/executions/20260503T025720-bb59ccca/manifest.json:12:    "title": "ddx try: pre-merge production-reachability check protocol + runner",
.ddx/executions/20260503T025720-bb59ccca/manifest.json:13:    "description": "Add a language-agnostic check protocol to ddx so projects can declare mechanical pre-merge gates that ddx try evaluates before any worktree is merged back. Solves the recurring "implemented but not wired" failure mode at the platform layer rather than per-project.

CONTEXT
- Audit at /tmp/agent-surface-dead-code-audit.md surfaced 4 functions with tests but no production caller; reviewer didn't catch it because the diff "looked right" and tests passed.
- ac_structural_check.go (cli/internal/agent/) is the existing seed: parses claims from AC prose for test_name/file_deleted/field_removed. It can't catch the unwired case because a unit test naming the function satisfies the claim vacuously.
- Prior plan considered embedding Go-specific call-graph analysis directly in cli/internal/agent/. Rejected as a boundary violation (CLAUDE.md: "DDx provides primitives; HELIX and others provide opinions"). Each language ecosystem has its own dead-code tooling (Go: deadcode/callgraph; Python: vulture; TS: ts-prune/knip; Rust: cargo-udeps).

DESIGN — protocol (declarative, plugin-based)
- Discovery: .ddx/checks/*.yaml each declares one check: { name, command, when: pre_merge, applies_to: { paths: [globs] | always, labels: [...] } }
- Inputs to check command (env vars): BEAD_ID, DIFF_BASE, DIFF_HEAD, PROJECT_ROOT, EVIDENCE_DIR, RUN_ID
- Output contract: check writes ${EVIDENCE_DIR}/${name}.json with schema { status: pass|block|error, message: string, violations: [{file, line, symbol, kind, detail}] }
- Exit code 0 = ran-to-completion (status field is authoritative); non-zero = check itself errored (treated as BLOCK with status=error)
- Hook point: pre_merge — runs in the parent (after worker's worktree work is done, BEFORE the merge-back into integration branch). BLOCK aborts the merge; the worktree stays preserved per existing conventions for human resolution.
- Bypass: bead-level annotation checks_bypass: [{name: <check>, reason: <text>, bead: <bead-id>}] recorded in events. No CLI flag — bypass must be tracked.
- Standalone: ddx ac run <bead-id> [--check <name>] runs the same checks locally so devs can iterate without spawning a full try attempt.

WHY pre_merge (NOT pre_close in the worker)
- User directive: "We can't afford to merge this kind of dead code." Worker-side checks could be bypassed by a worker that ignores BLOCK; parent-side gate cannot.
- Worker still gets feedback via ddx ac run for local iteration.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Multiple lifecycle hook points beyond pre_merge (add later if needed).
- Check dependencies / ordering (independent + parallel until proven insufficient).
- Plugin manifests, capability flags, version negotiation.
- Auto-discovery beyond .ddx/checks/*.yaml.
- Retry / timeout policy (project owns; checks should be fast).
- Any specific language check (separate beads).

OUT OF SCOPE: REACH-GO (the Go-specific deadcode-based check) and REACH-BACKFILL (the systematic application across cli/) are separate beads that depend on this one.

FILES (anticipated)
- cli/internal/checks/protocol.go — config schema + JSON I/O types
- cli/internal/checks/loader.go — read .ddx/checks/*.yaml
- cli/internal/checks/runner.go — subprocess invocation, env injection, output parsing, evidence dir mgmt
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go — wire pre_merge call before merge step; BLOCK aborts merge
- cli/cmd/ac.go — ddx ac run subcommand
- cli/internal/checks/*_test.go — table-driven tests for protocol contract",

.ddx/executions/20260503T025809-45a220f7/manifest.json:13:    "description": "The bug at ddx-29058e2a (5 CLI flags silently dropped on the server path) is a symptom of a deeper architectural issue: the ExecuteLoopSpec is defined as parallel structs that must be hand-maintained in lockstep. Adding any flag requires editing 5+ places, and forgetting one is silent.

CURRENT STATE (the smell)

Each flag traverses 6 boundaries:

1. cobra extract: cmd.Flags().GetX("flag") → local var (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1573-1611)
2. positional param: passed to executeLoopWithServer / runExecuteLoopLocal (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1627, ~1941 signature)
3. loose JSON map: workerSpec map[string]interface{} (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:~1948)
4. server request struct: hand-maintained in handleStartExecuteLoopWorker (cli/internal/server/server.go:2246-2260)
5. ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec literal: server.go:2289-2303 → cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49
6. runWorker per-field consumption: cli/internal/server/workers.go ~656

ALSO IN SCOPE — the GraphQL parallel path (per codex review):
- workerDispatchAdapter at cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:62-75, 123-137 has its own request struct AND its own StartExecuteLoop literal — same parallel-struct smell on a different transport. Unification must DELETE this too, otherwise the refactor fixes REST and leaves GraphQL re-broken.

Two of those (steps 4 and 5) are typed but maintained in parallel. The 5 dropped flags (opaque_passthrough, max_cost, request_timeout, min_power, max_power) fell out exactly there. PLUS FromRev is also dropped on the server path (cobra flag at agent_cmd.go:1560 + work.go:58, consumed locally at agent_cmd.go:1753 ExecuteBeadRuntime{FromRev: fromRev}, never reaches the server). The bug fix would be local — add fields in 5 places — but the ROOT CAUSE recurs the next time a flag is added.

DESIGN — single canonical ExecuteLoopSpec struct

Define one struct, used at every layer:

  cobra RunE → parseExecuteLoopSpec(cmd) (ExecuteLoopSpec, DispatchOptions)
                ↓
       (--local) runExecuteLoopInline(spec)
                ↓
       (default) httpSubmitExecuteLoop(spec)  ── JSON ──▶ server.handleStartExecuteLoopWorker
                                                   ↓ (decode INTO THE SAME struct, then Validate + ApplyDefaults)
                                                startExecuteLoop(spec)
                                                   ↓ (persist as spec.json — SAME struct, fully-resolved)
                                                runWorker(spec)

Properties:
- One struct definition. JSON tags drive both wire format and disk persistence.
- Cobra binding is the only per-flag code (irreducible — cobra needs to know each flag).
- --local and server paths consume the same struct; behavior parity is structural, not asserted by tests.
- Adding a flag = (a) one struct field + (b) one cobra binding. Drop is impossible by construction — the JSON tag round-trips through wire and persistence.
- DispatchOptions is a SEPARATE small struct holding control-plane flags (--local, --json) that are NOT execution-spec fields and must NOT be persisted on disk.

CRITICAL CONTRACTS (per codex review)

1. Wire format for time.Duration: Go's default time.Duration JSON marshaling produces nanoseconds, but current code sends poll_interval as a string (agent_cmd.go:1969-1971). The unified spec MUST commit to one format — either custom Duration type with string MarshalJSON, or canonical numeric. Pick string (operator-readable spec.json wins) and add UnmarshalJSON tolerating both.

2. Validate + ApplyDefaults lifecycle:
   - parseExecuteLoopSpec returns spec + DispatchOptions, with cobra defaults applied
   - Spec.Validate() is called BEFORE HTTP submit (client-side guard)
   - server.handleStartExecuteLoopWorker decodes → calls Spec.ApplyDefaults() → Spec.Validate() BEFORE StartExecuteLoop (server cannot trust the wire)
   - GraphQL workerDispatchAdapter goes through THE SAME StartExecuteLoop entry point so Validate + ApplyDefaults are guaranteed
   - spec.json on disk is fully-resolved (no implicit defaults at runWorker time)

3. Forward-compat stance: ddx is single-repo, single-version client/server today. Add SpecVersion int field; server returns 400 with capability list if SpecVersion > supported. Newer-server-older-client: existing fields unchanged, new fields ignored on both sides (the current default). Make this explicit in the AC; do not rely on json's silent unknown-field tolerance as the only contract.

4. Backward-compat for old persisted specs: existing spec.json files in .ddx/workers/ have legacy fields (e.g., min_tier/max_tier in older specs at .ddx/workers/worker-20260422T083934-f77c/spec.json:5-6). UnmarshalJSON must tolerate unknown fields when LOADING from disk (json.Decoder default behavior is fine here; do NOT enable DisallowUnknownFields for disk reads).

LOCATION

Likely cli/internal/agent/spec.go or cli/internal/agent/executeloop/spec.go (a small focused package). Importable from both cli/cmd/ and cli/internal/server/ without import cycles. NOTE: REACH-PROTO (ddx-a946c744) needs to know about this new package as an entry-root for the production-reachability checker.

NOT IN SCOPE

- Other commands (ddx agent run, ddx try, ddx run, MCP agent dispatch, ddx work passthrough variants) — same smell may exist; covered by the sister audit bead ddx-fb290074.
- Reworking client→server transport (HTTP/JSON stays as-is).
- Changing validation logic itself — only ensuring it lives on the spec so both paths apply it.
- Renaming flags or reshaping CLI surface.
- Reshaping LabelFilter from string to []string — that is a picker semantics change, owned by ddx-9d55601f. Keep LabelFilter as string to keep ddx-9d55601f landing in parallel.

USER-OBSERVED SYMPTOM (originally what filed this bead)
- ddx work --harness claude works under --local but fails when going through the server.
- Root cause: opaque_passthrough was dropped at the server's request struct. With OpaquePassthrough lost, the server-spawned worker validates the harness against its provider list (which rejects 'claude') instead of treating it as opaque-passthrough.
- The unification refactor solves this by construction — there's no second struct to omit OpaquePassthrough from. NOTE: OpaquePassthrough has TWO skip points to verify (cli/internal/server/workers.go:314-317 AND :752-773), not one.

ABSORBS THE NARROW FIX
The 5 originally-listed dropped flags + FromRev all become fields on the unified spec. They cannot be dropped after this refactor. The refactor IS the fix.

SEQUENCING WITH IN-FLIGHT BEADS

- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive): touches execute_bead_loop.go's Run loop. Should land BEFORE this OR be tightly coordinated. If unification lands first, dc157075 redoes defaulting + persistence. Recommend dc157075 first.
- ddx-9d55601f (picker priority bug): does NOT block on this. Keep LabelFilter type stable. Picker fix lands in parallel.
- ddx-a946c744 (REACH-PROTO): needs to know about new cli/internal/agent/executeloop/ package as entry root.",

.ddx/executions/20260502T204932-0e555908/manifest.json:12:    "title": "ddx try: pre-merge production-reachability check protocol + runner",
.ddx/executions/20260502T204932-0e555908/manifest.json:13:    "description": "Add a language-agnostic check protocol to ddx so projects can declare mechanical pre-merge gates that ddx try evaluates before any worktree is merged back. Solves the recurring "implemented but not wired" failure mode at the platform layer rather than per-project.

CONTEXT
- Audit at /tmp/agent-surface-dead-code-audit.md surfaced 4 functions with tests but no production caller; reviewer didn't catch it because the diff "looked right" and tests passed.
- ac_structural_check.go (cli/internal/agent/) is the existing seed: parses claims from AC prose for test_name/file_deleted/field_removed. It can't catch the unwired case because a unit test naming the function satisfies the claim vacuously.
- Prior plan considered embedding Go-specific call-graph analysis directly in cli/internal/agent/. Rejected as a boundary violation (CLAUDE.md: "DDx provides primitives; HELIX and others provide opinions"). Each language ecosystem has its own dead-code tooling (Go: deadcode/callgraph; Python: vulture; TS: ts-prune/knip; Rust: cargo-udeps).

DESIGN — protocol (declarative, plugin-based)
- Discovery: .ddx/checks/*.yaml each declares one check: { name, command, when: pre_merge, applies_to: { paths: [globs] | always, labels: [...] } }
- Inputs to check command (env vars): BEAD_ID, DIFF_BASE, DIFF_HEAD, PROJECT_ROOT, EVIDENCE_DIR, RUN_ID
- Output contract: check writes ${EVIDENCE_DIR}/${name}.json with schema { status: pass|block|error, message: string, violations: [{file, line, symbol, kind, detail}] }
- Exit code 0 = ran-to-completion (status field is authoritative); non-zero = check itself errored (treated as BLOCK with status=error)
- Hook point: pre_merge — runs in the parent (after worker's worktree work is done, BEFORE the merge-back into integration branch). BLOCK aborts the merge; the worktree stays preserved per existing conventions for human resolution.
- Bypass: bead-level annotation checks_bypass: [{name: <check>, reason: <text>, bead: <bead-id>}] recorded in events. No CLI flag — bypass must be tracked.
- Standalone: ddx ac run <bead-id> [--check <name>] runs the same checks locally so devs can iterate without spawning a full try attempt.

WHY pre_merge (NOT pre_close in the worker)
- User directive: "We can't afford to merge this kind of dead code." Worker-side checks could be bypassed by a worker that ignores BLOCK; parent-side gate cannot.
- Worker still gets feedback via ddx ac run for local iteration.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Multiple lifecycle hook points beyond pre_merge (add later if needed).
- Check dependencies / ordering (independent + parallel until proven insufficient).
- Plugin manifests, capability flags, version negotiation.
- Auto-discovery beyond .ddx/checks/*.yaml.
- Retry / timeout policy (project owns; checks should be fast).
- Any specific language check (separate beads).

OUT OF SCOPE: REACH-GO (the Go-specific deadcode-based check) and REACH-BACKFILL (the systematic application across cli/) are separate beads that depend on this one.

FILES (anticipated)
- cli/internal/checks/protocol.go — config schema + JSON I/O types
- cli/internal/checks/loader.go — read .ddx/checks/*.yaml
- cli/internal/checks/runner.go — subprocess invocation, env injection, output parsing, evidence dir mgmt
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go — wire pre_merge call before merge step; BLOCK aborts merge
- cli/cmd/ac.go — ddx ac run subcommand
- cli/internal/checks/*_test.go — table-driven tests for protocol contract",

.ddx/executions/20260503T123048-397071dc/prompt.md:3:    <title>checks: ship Go production-reachability check (library/checks/go-production-reachability)</title>
.ddx/executions/20260503T123048-397071dc/prompt.md:5:Ship the Go-specific production-reachability check as a library resource conforming to the REACH-PROTO check protocol. NOT installed by default for arbitrary projects (Go-opinion would leak through ddx init). This project (ddx itself) opts in by adding .ddx/checks/production-reachability.yaml.

DESIGN
- Location: library/checks/go-production-reachability/ (alongside library/personas/, library/patterns/)
- Implementation: a small Go binary or shell script invoking golang.org/x/tools/cmd/deadcode rooted at configurable entry packages.
- Default entry roots for ddx self-use: cli/cmd/..., cli/internal/server/graphql/..., cli/internal/server/http/...
- Logic:
  1. Read DIFF_BASE..DIFF_HEAD from env. Compute set of new Go symbols (functions, methods, types) added by the diff via AST parse of changed files.
  2. Run deadcode (or callgraph) rooted at the configured entry packages, EXCLUDING _test.go from roots and from reachability.
  3. For each new symbol not reachable from any entry root → violation { file, line, symbol, kind: "unreachable", detail: "no path from entry roots: <list>" }
  4. Recognize // wiring:pending <bead-id> annotation as escape hatch for individual symbols. The check verifies the named bead exists and is open via local beads.jsonl read; otherwise the annotation is rejected.
  5. Write ${EVIDENCE_DIR}/production-reachability.json with the protocol-defined schema.
- Config: library/checks/go-production-reachability/check.yaml template that operators copy to .ddx/checks/ and customize entry_roots for their project.

DOGFOOD
- This project installs it: write .ddx/checks/production-reachability.yaml referencing the library check + cli's entry roots.
- The next ddx try on any bead touching cli/**/*.go will exercise the check.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Auto-install during ddx init (would leak Go-opinion). Operators opt in manually or via ddx install check (future, separate bead).
- Coverage-based "exercised via CLI" check (entered_via flavor). Static reachability is sufficient for the immediate problem.
- Python/TS/Rust analogues. Future contributions; protocol supports them.

DEP: REACH-PROTO (above)
.ddx/executions/20260503T123048-397071dc/prompt.md:8:1. library/checks/go-production-reachability/ exists with: README.md (usage), check.yaml (template), implementation (binary or script).
2. Implementation reads DIFF_BASE/DIFF_HEAD/PROJECT_ROOT/EVIDENCE_DIR from env; computes new symbols from diff; runs deadcode-rooted reachability; writes result.json conforming to REACH-PROTO schema.
3. // wiring:pending <bead-id> annotation parsed; bead existence + open-status verified against .ddx/beads.jsonl; invalid annotation = violation.
4. _test.go files excluded from both root selection and reachability calculation (so a unit-test-only reference does NOT satisfy the check).
5. Self-test: synthetic project with one unwired exported function returns status=block with that symbol in violations.
6. Self-test: same project with the function wired into cmd handler returns status=pass.
7. Self-test: same project with // wiring:pending annotation citing an open bead returns status=pass; annotation citing a closed/missing bead returns status=block.
8. This project installs it: .ddx/checks/production-reachability.yaml committed; ddx ac run on any current bead succeeds (or BLOCKs with the known unwired functions — see REACH-BACKFILL for resolution).
9. cd cli && go test green; lefthook pre-commit passes.
10. Evidence file at .ddx/executions/<run-id>/production-reachability.json shows the check ran and its findings.

.ddx/executions/20260503T110552-7129c1c3/prompt.md:5:The bug at ddx-29058e2a (5 CLI flags silently dropped on the server path) is a symptom of a deeper architectural issue: the ExecuteLoopSpec is defined as parallel structs that must be hand-maintained in lockstep. Adding any flag requires editing 5+ places, and forgetting one is silent.

CURRENT STATE (the smell)

Each flag traverses 6 boundaries:

1. cobra extract: cmd.Flags().GetX("flag") → local var (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1573-1611)
2. positional param: passed to executeLoopWithServer / runExecuteLoopLocal (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1627, ~1941 signature)
3. loose JSON map: workerSpec map[string]interface{} (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:~1948)
4. server request struct: hand-maintained in handleStartExecuteLoopWorker (cli/internal/server/server.go:2246-2260)
5. ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec literal: server.go:2289-2303 → cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49
6. runWorker per-field consumption: cli/internal/server/workers.go ~656

ALSO IN SCOPE — the GraphQL parallel path (per codex review):
- workerDispatchAdapter at cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:62-75, 123-137 has its own request struct AND its own StartExecuteLoop literal — same parallel-struct smell on a different transport. Unification must DELETE this too, otherwise the refactor fixes REST and leaves GraphQL re-broken.

Two of those (steps 4 and 5) are typed but maintained in parallel. The 5 dropped flags (opaque_passthrough, max_cost, request_timeout, min_power, max_power) fell out exactly there. PLUS FromRev is also dropped on the server path (cobra flag at agent_cmd.go:1560 + work.go:58, consumed locally at agent_cmd.go:1753 ExecuteBeadRuntime{FromRev: fromRev}, never reaches the server). The bug fix would be local — add fields in 5 places — but the ROOT CAUSE recurs the next time a flag is added.

DESIGN — single canonical ExecuteLoopSpec struct

Define one struct, used at every layer:

  cobra RunE → parseExecuteLoopSpec(cmd) (ExecuteLoopSpec, DispatchOptions)
                ↓
       (--local) runExecuteLoopInline(spec)
                ↓
       (default) httpSubmitExecuteLoop(spec)  ── JSON ──▶ server.handleStartExecuteLoopWorker
                                                   ↓ (decode INTO THE SAME struct, then Validate + ApplyDefaults)
                                                startExecuteLoop(spec)
                                                   ↓ (persist as spec.json — SAME struct, fully-resolved)
                                                runWorker(spec)

Properties:
- One struct definition. JSON tags drive both wire format and disk persistence.
- Cobra binding is the only per-flag code (irreducible — cobra needs to know each flag).
- --local and server paths consume the same struct; behavior parity is structural, not asserted by tests.
- Adding a flag = (a) one struct field + (b) one cobra binding. Drop is impossible by construction — the JSON tag round-trips through wire and persistence.
- DispatchOptions is a SEPARATE small struct holding control-plane flags (--local, --json) that are NOT execution-spec fields and must NOT be persisted on disk.

CRITICAL CONTRACTS (per codex review)

1. Wire format for time.Duration: Go's default time.Duration JSON marshaling produces nanoseconds, but current code sends poll_interval as a string (agent_cmd.go:1969-1971). The unified spec MUST commit to one format — either custom Duration type with string MarshalJSON, or canonical numeric. Pick string (operator-readable spec.json wins) and add UnmarshalJSON tolerating both.

2. Validate + ApplyDefaults lifecycle:
   - parseExecuteLoopSpec returns spec + DispatchOptions, with cobra defaults applied
   - Spec.Validate() is called BEFORE HTTP submit (client-side guard)
   - server.handleStartExecuteLoopWorker decodes → calls Spec.ApplyDefaults() → Spec.Validate() BEFORE StartExecuteLoop (server cannot trust the wire)
   - GraphQL workerDispatchAdapter goes through THE SAME StartExecuteLoop entry point so Validate + ApplyDefaults are guaranteed
   - spec.json on disk is fully-resolved (no implicit defaults at runWorker time)

3. Forward-compat stance: ddx is single-repo, single-version client/server today. Add SpecVersion int field; server returns 400 with capability list if SpecVersion > supported. Newer-server-older-client: existing fields unchanged, new fields ignored on both sides (the current default). Make this explicit in the AC; do not rely on json's silent unknown-field tolerance as the only contract.

4. Backward-compat for old persisted specs: existing spec.json files in .ddx/workers/ have legacy fields (e.g., min_tier/max_tier in older specs at .ddx/workers/worker-20260422T083934-f77c/spec.json:5-6). UnmarshalJSON must tolerate unknown fields when LOADING from disk (json.Decoder default behavior is fine here; do NOT enable DisallowUnknownFields for disk reads).

LOCATION

Likely cli/internal/agent/spec.go or cli/internal/agent/executeloop/spec.go (a small focused package). Importable from both cli/cmd/ and cli/internal/server/ without import cycles. NOTE: REACH-PROTO (ddx-a946c744) needs to know about this new package as an entry-root for the production-reachability checker.

NOT IN SCOPE

- Other commands (ddx agent run, ddx try, ddx run, MCP agent dispatch, ddx work passthrough variants) — same smell may exist; covered by the sister audit bead ddx-fb290074.
- Reworking client→server transport (HTTP/JSON stays as-is).
- Changing validation logic itself — only ensuring it lives on the spec so both paths apply it.
- Renaming flags or reshaping CLI surface.
- Reshaping LabelFilter from string to []string — that is a picker semantics change, owned by ddx-9d55601f. Keep LabelFilter as string to keep ddx-9d55601f landing in parallel.

USER-OBSERVED SYMPTOM (originally what filed this bead)
- ddx work --harness claude works under --local but fails when going through the server.
- Root cause: opaque_passthrough was dropped at the server's request struct. With OpaquePassthrough lost, the server-spawned worker validates the harness against its provider list (which rejects 'claude') instead of treating it as opaque-passthrough.
- The unification refactor solves this by construction — there's no second struct to omit OpaquePassthrough from. NOTE: OpaquePassthrough has TWO skip points to verify (cli/internal/server/workers.go:314-317 AND :752-773), not one.

ABSORBS THE NARROW FIX
The 5 originally-listed dropped flags + FromRev all become fields on the unified spec. They cannot be dropped after this refactor. The refactor IS the fix.

SEQUENCING WITH IN-FLIGHT BEADS

- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive): touches execute_bead_loop.go's Run loop. Should land BEFORE this OR be tightly coordinated. If unification lands first, dc157075 redoes defaulting + persistence. Recommend dc157075 first.
- ddx-9d55601f (picker priority bug): does NOT block on this. Keep LabelFilter type stable. Picker fix lands in parallel.
- ddx-a946c744 (REACH-PROTO): needs to know about new cli/internal/agent/executeloop/ package as entry root.
.ddx/executions/20260415T012939-e548f96b/prompt.md:5:Add a new DDx CLI subcommand `ddx agent check [--provider X]`. Semantic distinction from existing `ddx agent doctor`: doctor answers "is my config failed?" (config validation, malformed YAML, missing API keys); check answers "what agents can I use right now?" (runtime liveness, which providers respond, which models are currently available). Both commands coexist.

Implementation lives in cli/cmd/agent_check.go. Mirrors ddx-agent's `cmdCheck` — probes each configured provider's /v1/models endpoint, reports reachability and current model inventory, exits 0 if at least one provider is reachable and has at least one usable model, exits 1 otherwise. Uses the Go package, not shell-out.

.ddx/executions/20260503T124553-282667f7/prompt.md:5:Once REACH-PROTO + REACH-GO have landed and ddx self-installs the check, run ddx ac run --check production-reachability against the current cli/ tree and resolve every violation. This is the systematic application phase the user mandated: "go back through the codebase systematically applying it."

ABSORBS
- ddx-09d2990c (WIRE) — the 4 specific unwired functions become a subset of this sweep. Close WIRE as superseded by this bead at file time.
- Any clusters from /tmp/agent-surface-dead-code-audit.md not already covered.
- Codex audit additions: jsonlFallbackForCollection (cli/internal/bead/backend_jsonl.go:90), Store.breakStaleLock (lock.go:70), NewStoreWithBackend (store.go:119), WithSpokeHTTPClient (federation_spoke.go:103), Server.execStore (server.go:2028), GraphQL NewResolver (resolver.go:14).

EXPECTED WORKFLOW
1. Run ddx ac run --check production-reachability against the current worktree. Capture violation list.
2. For each violation, decide: WIRE (add to production code path) or DELETE (function is genuinely obsolete).
3. File one child bead per violation cluster (group by package or by originating story for traceability).
4. Drain the children. Each closes either via wiring PR or deletion PR.
5. Final pass: ddx ac run returns status=pass with no violations and no // wiring:pending annotations remaining (or all annotations cite still-open future beads).

DECISION RULE per violation
- If the symbol's originating bead's AC describes desired runtime behavior → WIRE.
- If the symbol was added speculatively or the design has moved on → DELETE; reopen the originating bead with obsolescence rationale, or close as obsolete.
- If neither is clear within 15 min of investigation → annotate // wiring:pending <follow-up-bead> and file the follow-up.

DEP: REACH-PROTO (ddx-a946c744), REACH-GO (ddx-ea76e1b8)

NOT IN SCOPE
- The protocol or check implementation itself (separate beads).
- Other languages (this project is Go-only at the check layer).
- Auto-run as recurring sweep (could be a future /schedule offer; not built into this bead).
.ddx/executions/20260503T124553-282667f7/prompt.md:8:1. ddx ac run --check production-reachability run on the current cli/ tree; full violation list captured to .ddx/executions/<run-id>/initial-violations.json.
2. One child bead filed per violation cluster (grouped sensibly); each child bead has explicit decision (WIRE or DELETE) with rationale.
3. Each child bead landed via standard execute-bead flow; merge gated by REACH-PROTO check (each landing must pass production-reachability for the symbols it touches).
4. ddx-09d2990c (WIRE) closed as superseded by this bead at filing time; its 4 functions surface as initial violations.
5. Final ddx ac run --check production-reachability returns status=pass with zero violations across cli/.
6. Any remaining // wiring:pending annotations cite open follow-up beads; no orphans.
7. Sweep summary report at .ddx/executions/<final-run-id>/reach-backfill-summary.md: total violations found, wired count, deleted count, follow-ups filed, LOC delta.
8. cd cli && go test ./... green at every child landing.
.ddx/executions/20260429T160253-45091def/manifest.json:14:    "description": "## Problem

`bun run test:e2e` from `cli/internal/server/frontend` exits 1: 42 of 196 tests fail, 149 pass, 5 skipped. The failures are not regressions — they are accumulated drift between the test suite and the current UI. `playwright.config.ts` runs only `bun run preview` (port 4173, static SvelteKit output, no Go backend), and the suite outgrew that scope.

Bead ddx-955b9c41 (closed 2026-04-21) flagged the split between `playwright.config.ts` (4173) and `playwright.demo.config.ts` (18080) as "likely intentional but undocumented". This epic resolves it.

## Failure breakdown

**(A) TC-008 HTTP API — 6 tests.** `e2e/app.spec.ts:368-409`. Direct `request.get('/api/...')` to `/api/health`, `/api/documents`, `/api/beads`, `/api/beads/status`, `/api/personas`, `/api/docs/graph`. Static preview returns 404; the Go server returns 200 for all six (verified with curl against the running ddx-server on https://127.0.0.1:7743 during the session that filed this bead).

**(B) TC-001 Dashboard — 8 tests.** `e2e/app.spec.ts:9-57`. All visit `/` and expect `h1` "Dashboard". Per Stage 3.8 (commit 3bf655cf), `src/routes/+page.svelte` does `goto(`/nodes/${nodeStore.value.id}`, { replaceState: true })` whenever a node is selected — `/` is no longer a Dashboard route. Tests were not migrated.

**(C) Pages requiring backend data — ~28 tests.** Spread across `e2e/app.spec.ts` (TC-002, TC-003, TC-005, TC-007), `e2e/beads-smoke.spec.ts`, `e2e/executions.spec.ts`, `e2e/navigation.spec.ts` (TC-006, TC-007), `e2e/screenshots.spec.ts`, `e2e/demo-recording.spec.ts`, `e2e/workers.spec.ts`, `src/routes/layout.e2e.ts`. All fail because the static preview can't serve real document/bead/session data.

## In-scope files

Configuration:
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/playwright.config.ts` — switch `webServer` to spawn the Go ddx server (built binary or `go run`) on a deterministic port with a fixture working dir, instead of `bun run preview`. Set `use.baseURL` to that port. Keep `bun run preview` available as a separate project ("static-only") for any tests that genuinely don't need a backend.
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/playwright.demo.config.ts` — **leave alone**. Used for demo recording; do not modify.

Fixture:
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/fixtures/<name>/` — new directory containing a seeded `.ddx/beads.jsonl` (handful of beads in varied states: open, closed, ready, blocked), a tiny `docs/` library (a few documents the API can index), and any minimum config the server needs. Self-contained — must NOT depend on the developer's `~/.config/ddx` or the parent repo's `.ddx/`.

Tests (categorize and migrate):
- `e2e/app.spec.ts` (TC-001, TC-002, TC-003, TC-005, TC-007, TC-008) — rewrite navigation paths to use `/nodes/<fixture-node-id>/projects/<fixture-project-id>/...`, matching the pattern in `e2e/navigation.spec.ts:177`. Drop assumptions about `/` resolving to a Dashboard route.
- `e2e/beads-smoke.spec.ts`, `e2e/executions.spec.ts`, `e2e/navigation.spec.ts`, `e2e/screenshots.spec.ts`, `e2e/demo-recording.spec.ts`, `e2e/workers.spec.ts`, `src/routes/layout.e2e.ts` — verify each passes against the new harness; update navigation paths and any data-shape assumptions where needed.

CI / runbook:
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/README.md` (or whatever is canonical) — document that `bun run test:e2e` now requires the ddx Go binary built first, and how the fixture is structured.

## Out-of-scope (file separate beads if needed)

- Server source changes in `cli/internal/server/` — the API is correct; this bead is about test wiring.
- Frontend route additions or component changes — only test files and fixtures change.
- `playwright.demo.config.ts` and `demo-recording.spec.ts` semantics — leave untouched (recording is its own concern).
- Visual regression baselines in `e2e/screenshots.spec.ts` — re-baselining if pixel diffs appear is a follow-up bead, not this one.

## Why an epic, not a single task

Per beads.md, anything that touches more than ~3 files or crosses subsystems should decompose. This crosses test-infra (playwright config + fixture seeding) and four distinct test files at minimum. Codex should decompose during refinement: at minimum, "(1) harness + fixture", "(2) migrate TC-001/TC-008", "(3) migrate page-data tests", and "(4) update runbook".

## Rollback / cleanup

If a child bead fails partway:
- Do not leave the fixture dir half-populated under `e2e/fixtures/`. Either commit a complete fixture or revert.
- Do not commit a `playwright.config.ts` that runs but skips tests (no `.skip` to make CI green).
- The currently-passing 149 tests must continue to pass at every checkpoint.

## Verification snapshot at filing time

- Working tree clean before run: confirmed.
- 196 tests collected, 149 pass, 42 fail, 5 skipped.
- Recent commits on main: d0945dc3 (frontend-verify Makefile target — unrelated to e2e), 5569a727 (beads.jsonl conflict resolve — unrelated).
- API endpoints verified live on https://127.0.0.1:7743: /api/health, /api/personas, /api/docs/graph, /api/beads, /api/documents all return 200.",

.ddx/executions/20260415T055750-15712870/manifest.json:14:    "description": "The CLI should not require the user to enumerate harness/model pairs. The tool already knows which tiers exist (model-catalog.yaml: cheap/standard/smart) and which harnesses are configured. Given a live probe of each provider's health at attempt time, the loop can auto-escalate on its own.

## Principle

Discovery over configuration. The user shouldn't write a fallback chain — the tool should figure it out.

## Default policy (no flags)

- Start at **cheap** tier, execute the bead
- If merge succeeds AND post-review (gap #1) APPROVES → close
- If anything fails (execution_failed, no_changes after N retries, post_run_check_failed, REQUEST_CHANGES from review) → escalate one tier and retry
- Chain: cheap → standard → smart → exhausted (preserve, flag for human)
- Review agent runs at max(impl_tier+1, smart). Never review cheap with cheap.

## Resource discovery at attempt time

- Before picking a concrete harness/model for a tier, probe each candidate provider (local inference host, API key validity, network reachability)
- If provider returns 502 / 500 / timeout / auth-rejected within a short budget, mark it unhealthy with a cooldown and try the next provider in the tier
- If every provider in a tier is unhealthy, skip the tier and escalate
- Per-provider health state is process-local and visible via a new 'ddx agent providers' sub-command (ddx-09ea1884 Visibility 3)

## Rare overrides

- --min-tier standard (skip cheap — useful for hard beads known to need it)
- --max-tier standard (never reach smart — cost control)
- --harness <name> / --model <name> (debug override; disables escalation)

## Surface

Default invocation becomes:

  ddx agent execute-loop --once

Everything else is implicit. Review, escalation, and resource discovery are on-by-default.

## Events

Each attempt appends an execute-bead event: { harness, model, tier, provider, probe_result, attempt_status, review_status }. The event stream shows exactly why a tier/provider was picked or skipped.

## Context

Observed during axon ADR-018 loop:
- bragi was down (502) — I manually probed with curl and switched to vidar-coder. Tool should do this.
- qwen3-coder-next merged but opus review rejected — I manually re-invoked with sonnet. Tool should escalate automatically.
- Resulting CLI invocation in my notes was too complex (—-fallback-chain a/b,c/d,e/f --post-review g/h). User feedback: too many knobs. Reach for a default that just works.

## Acceptance criteria replaced with discovery-driven ones (see accept field)",

- `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/fixtures/<name>/` — new directory containing a seeded `.ddx/beads.jsonl` (handful of beads in varied states: open, closed, ready, blocked), a tiny `docs/` library (a few documents the API can index), and any minimum config the server needs. Self-contained — must NOT depend on the developer's `~/.config/ddx` or the parent repo's `.ddx/`.

Tests (categorize and migrate):
- `e2e/app.spec.ts` (TC-001, TC-002, TC-003, TC-005, TC-007, TC-008) — rewrite navigation paths to use `/nodes/<fixture-node-id>/projects/<fixture-project-id>/...`, matching the pattern in `e2e/navigation.spec.ts:177`. Drop assumptions about `/` resolving to a Dashboard route.
- `e2e/beads-smoke.spec.ts`, `e2e/executions.spec.ts`, `e2e/navigation.spec.ts`, `e2e/screenshots.spec.ts`, `e2e/demo-recording.spec.ts`, `e2e/workers.spec.ts`, `src/routes/layout.e2e.ts` — verify each passes against the new harness; update navigation paths and any data-shape assumptions where needed.

CI / runbook:
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/README.md` (or whatever is canonical) — document that `bun run test:e2e` now requires the ddx Go binary built first, and how the fixture is structured.

## Out-of-scope (file separate beads if needed)

- Server source changes in `cli/internal/server/` — the API is correct; this bead is about test wiring.
- Frontend route additions or component changes — only test files and fixtures change.
- `playwright.demo.config.ts` and `demo-recording.spec.ts` semantics — leave untouched (recording is its own concern).
- Visual regression baselines in `e2e/screenshots.spec.ts` — re-baselining if pixel diffs appear is a follow-up bead, not this one.

## Why an epic, not a single task

Per beads.md, anything that touches more than ~3 files or crosses subsystems should decompose. This crosses test-infra (playwright config + fixture seeding) and four distinct test files at minimum. Codex should decompose during refinement: at minimum, "(1) harness + fixture", "(2) migrate TC-001/TC-008", "(3) migrate page-data tests", and "(4) update runbook".

## Rollback / cleanup

If a child bead fails partway:
- Do not leave the fixture dir half-populated under `e2e/fixtures/`. Either commit a complete fixture or revert.
- Do not commit a `playwright.config.ts` that runs but skips tests (no `.skip` to make CI green).
- The currently-passing 149 tests must continue to pass at every checkpoint.

## Verification snapshot at filing time

- Working tree clean before run: confirmed.
- 196 tests collected, 149 pass, 42 fail, 5 skipped.
- Recent commits on main: d0945dc3 (frontend-verify Makefile target — unrelated to e2e), 5569a727 (beads.jsonl conflict resolve — unrelated).
- API endpoints verified live on https://127.0.0.1:7743: /api/health, /api/personas, /api/docs/graph, /api/beads, /api/documents all return 200.",
.ddx/executions/20260415T062435-4255f769/manifest.json:14:    "description": "The ddx-bead skill guides bead creation but underspecifies what makes a bead *execution-ready* for automated execute-bead runs. Observed during axon ADR-018 execute-loop driving: beads that technically met the current skill's requirements (title, AC, labels, description pointing at spec-id) still produced bad execute-bead outcomes because the description lacked the context the agent needed to ship correct code in one pass.

## Gaps in the current skill

1. **Description quality is underspecified.** The skill gives AC quality rules but the description field just says 'per SD-004 Section 2' is OK. In practice, agents need:
   - Exact file paths to touch (e.g., 'crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs')
   - Cross-bead context when dependencies are listed (e.g., 'A1 landed the credential_revocations table in crates/axon-storage/src/auth_schema.rs — query it via SELECT 1')
   - Inline spec excerpts, NOT just doc-id references. If the spec is ADR-018 §4's failure table, paste the table into the description. Don't say 'see ADR-018 §4'.
   - Files NOT to touch (scope negation)

2. **spec-id and the doc graph are unreliable for execution context.** The current skill implies spec-id linkage is sufficient. It is not — spec-id drives docgraph.BuildGraphWithConfig() which only resolves HELIX-shaped doc references. Generic beads on non-HELIX projects have no equivalent. AND even when resolution works, the agent only gets a file path, not the relevant section. The bead description is the only place that carries content the agent can rely on.

3. **No guidance on cross-bead context.** When bead B depends on bead A, B's description should describe what A landed (file paths, type names, key APIs) — not assume the agent will reopen A's history. Automated execute-bead does not have that context; each bead is read cold.

4. **No guidance on verification commands in AC.** AC items like 'unit tests green' are too vague. Should be 'cargo test -p axon-server green' or 'bun test passes in sdk/typescript'. Named commands.

5. **No guidance on bead size.** A bead should be one unit of testable work, sized so a single agent pass can complete it. Too small is overhead; too large defeats review. Heuristic: if the AC lists more than ~12 checkboxes, consider splitting.

6. **No guidance on incorporating review findings on reopen.** When a bead is reopened after a REQUEST_CHANGES review, the author should either (a) fold the review findings into the description for the next attempt to see, or (b) rely on the tool to thread review events into the prompt (a separate ddx bead — see ddx-a317208c). The skill is silent on this.

7. **No template for description.** A recommended description template (Scope / Files to touch / Cross-bead context / Verification / Quality bar) would make good beads cheaper to author.

## Scope

Update skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md to add:

- A 'What makes a bead execution-ready' section that codifies the points above
- A 'Description template' with recommended sections: Scope, Files to touch, Files NOT to touch, Cross-bead context, Verification commands, Spec excerpts inline
- A 'Bead sizing' section with the ~12-AC heuristic
- A 'Review-driven reopen' section that tells authors to update the description in place when a bead is reopened after a review rejection
- A 'Common anti-patterns' section enumerating the mistakes observed during the axon execute-loop:
  - Listing doc IDs without inlining the relevant text
  - Not naming file paths in the description
  - AC items that are not verifiable by a named command
  - Depending on unmerged work without explaining what it landed

Keep the existing required-fields and label-conventions sections — they are correct.

## Context

Observed during axon ADR-018 execute-loop:
- Bead A1 was well-sized but initially had scope creep in its description (audit_retention_policies, which belonged to F1). Agent added the scope-creep table faithfully.
- Bead A2's initial description said 'UserIdentity { provider, subject, user_id }' — a typo carried over from an earlier ADR revision. Agent implemented 'subject' faithfully even though ADR-018 §3 said 'external_id'. The description was the contract the agent followed.
- Bead B1 is being re-authored right now with a richer description (inline ADR-018 §4 failure table, exact file paths, cross-bead context about A1 schema and A2 types) — this is the experiment to validate the authoring changes proposed in this bead.

## Acceptance

- [ ] skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md gains 'What makes a bead execution-ready' section
- [ ] Description template added with the 6 recommended sections
- [ ] Bead sizing heuristic documented
- [ ] Review-driven reopen guidance added
- [ ] Common anti-patterns enumerated
- [ ] Skill still lints against any existing skill-format CI check",

.ddx/executions/20260428T024807-b88003fb/manifest.json:70:          "body": "no_changes
rationale: ddx-af187382 — agent: migrate internal helpers off RunOptions/CompareOptions/QuorumOptions

Second-attempt no_changes after re-analysis with the EXPANDED scope (option b
authorized 2026-04-26).

## What is done

- Confirmed prior worker's analysis (attempt 1, base 12a2b57f). The runtime-lint
  pre-commit gate (lefthook.yml:164–186) filters violations to *staged* files.
  Any staged Go file that mentions a RunOptions / CompareOptions / QuorumOptions
  composite literal or function-parameter signature fails the gate.
- Inventoried all ref sites under the expanded scope. 38 .go files touch the
  three legacy types (excluding tools/lint testdata). Of those:
    Production:
      cli/internal/agent/grade.go (1 site — trivial, AC item 1)
      cli/internal/agent/service_run.go (RunViaService, RunViaServiceWith,
        runFixtureHarnessViaRunner — public API surface)
      cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go (RunCompareViaService,
        RunCompareWithAgent, RunCompareWith, RunQuorumViaService,
        RunQuorumWithAgent, RunQuorumWith, RunBenchmarkViaService,
        RunBenchmarkWithAgent, RunBenchmarkWith, runCompareArmWith,
        defaultResolvePromptForCompare, RunFunc, BenchmarkArmsToCompare)
      cli/internal/agent/runner.go (Run, RunWithConfig, BuildArgs, resolveHarness,
        resolveModel, resolveTimeout, resolveWallClock, resolvePrompt,
        TestProviderConnectivity — RunOptions is the central type)
      cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go, agent_runner_service.go,
        claude_stream.go, virtual.go, script.go (all consume RunOptions)
      cli/internal/agent/session_index.go (SessionIndexEntryFromResult signature)
      cli/internal/agent/routing_metrics.go, models.go (lesser uses)
      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:647 dispatchAgentRun
      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:531 dispatchReviewRun
      cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1462 (RunViaService) and :1243 (RunBenchmarkViaService)
    Tests (must be migrated to keep build green per AC item 6/7):
      cli/internal/agent/agent_test.go (15+ BuildArgs sites)
      cli/internal/agent/virtual_test.go (3)
      cli/internal/agent/claude_stream_test.go (6)
      cli/internal/agent/prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go (3)
      cli/internal/agent/session_index_test.go (2)
      cli/internal/agent/models_test.go (1)
      cli/internal/agent/run_internal_test.go, agent_run_runtime_test.go,
        execute_bead_runtime_test.go, execute_bead_review_test.go,
        execute_bead_review_evidence_test.go, execute_bead_env_isolation_test.go,
        execute_bead_e2e_test.go, execute_bead_artifacts_test.go
      cli/cmd/agent_run_config_test.go, agent_quorum_config_test.go,
        agent_execute_bead_test.go, agent_compare_config_test.go,
        cli/internal/server/workers_stop_propagation_test.go
      cli/cmd/command_factory.go (production), cli/internal/config/testconfig.go,
        cli/internal/exec/types.go (mostly comments)

  Net surface: ~4,000 LoC of net change across the agent package, the cmd
  package, the server package, the exec package, the config package, plus
  ~10,000 LoC of test code that constructs the legacy types or accepts them
  as helper parameters. Every public-API surface change cascades to its
  callers, including BuildArgs (15+ test call sites) and
  SessionIndexEntryFromResult (consumed by session-index machinery and the
  oversize-prompt ingress test).

## What is blocking

The migration is too large for a single execute-bead worker window even with
the expanded scope. Three concrete reasons:

1. **Per-bead size budget.** SD-024 §Migration discipline (lines 234–256)
   explicitly mandates "<500 LoC of net change, <20 minutes of agent time,
   under $10 of model spend" per bead, with a footnote that "Anything that
   wants to 'do the whole loop migration in one shot' is sized wrong and
   must be split." This bead's expanded scope is ~4,000 production LoC plus
   ~10,000 test LoC — 8x to 28x the SD-mandated budget. The previous attempt
   spent 16.6 minutes and $6.75 producing no_changes; even doubling that
   budget cannot produce a green tree across this surface in one pass.

2. **The migration is not one atomic change but a sequence of seam swaps.**
   Each affected public API (RunViaService → RunWithConfigViaService,
   RunCompareViaService → RunCompareWithConfigViaService, BuildArgs's
   RunOptions parameter, SessionIndexEntryFromResult's RunOptions parameter,
   RunFunc closure type) has its own caller graph. SD-024's own discipline
   (lines 248–256) prescribes per-caller migration beads precisely so each
   step leaves the tree green and lint-clean. Bundling them into one bead
   re-creates the bug class the SD's migration discipline was written to
   avoid: a half-finished refactor with the lint gate firing on the
   intermediate state, where the worker either has to disable hooks (forbidden)
   or revert most of the work to commit anything.

3. **Test-file lint cascade.** The runtime-lint pre-commit gate (lefthook.yml
   pattern 2) flags any staged Go file containing a RunOptions{} / CompareOptions{}
   / QuorumOptions{} composite literal. Migrating BuildArgs's signature
   alone (to take a non-RunOptions parameter) re-stages every one of the
   15+ agent_test.go test files, and the gate fires on each. The same
   cascade fires when SessionIndexEntryFromResult's signature changes
   (re-staging session_index_test.go), when RunFunc's closure type changes
   (re-staging compare/quorum/benchmark tests), and so on for each public-API
   seam swap. Every signature change requires migrating its consumers in
   the *same commit* — but the consumers' test files already contain
   composite literals from sibling test cases that aren't in this bead's
   AC. Those literals stay valid (BuildArgs still works) but the gate fires
   because the file is now staged. Untangling this requires touching every
   composite-literal site in every staged test file — hence the ~10,000 LoC
   of test work above.

## What a follow-up attempt would need

Per SD-024 §Stage 2 (lines 281–286) and §Migration discipline (lines 222–256),
this bead should be split along the seam-swap boundaries the SD itself
prescribes. Concretely, the recommended split is:

a) **B22d-grade.go** (~10 LoC): switch the single `r.Run(RunOptions{...})`
   call in cli/internal/agent/grade.go to
   `r.runInternal(RunArgs{...})` via the existing dispatch seam from
   ddx-dd640c18. Satisfies AC items 1, 4, 5 (RunOptions/CompareOptions/
   QuorumOptions removed from grade.go — items 4 and 5 are vacuous as the
   types aren't present today). Build and tests green; runtime-lint green
   because the staged file contains no legacy literals after the swap.
   Independently buildable, lint-clean, AC-checkable.

b) **B22d-cmd-RunViaService** (~30 LoC): switch
   cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1462's RunViaService call to
   RunWithConfigViaService, threading a ResolvedConfig through the call.
   Independently buildable, lint-clean.

c) **B22d-cmd-RunBenchmarkViaService** (~30 LoC): same migration for
   :1243 RunBenchmarkViaService → a new RunBenchmarkWithConfigViaService
   that takes ResolvedConfig.

d) **B22d-execute_bead-dispatch** (~80 LoC + 2-3 test files):
   migrate execute_bead.go dispatchAgentRun (line 647 RunOptions
   composite, line 938 RunViaServiceWith) and execute_bead_review.go
   dispatchReviewRun (line 531 RunOptions composite, line 704
   RunViaServiceWith) onto a new internal seam that takes
   ResolvedConfig + AgentRunRuntime. Update the corresponding
   execute_bead_*_test.go files in this bead (they already import the
   functions and stage as siblings).

e) **B22d-BuildArgs-signature** (~150 LoC, mostly tests):
   change BuildArgs's parameter from RunOptions to a new internal
   buildArgsInput struct (or a slice of explicit args). Update
   agent_test.go's 15+ call sites in the same commit. Adjacent runner.go
   call sites stay on RunOptions internally — runner.go is not in the AC
   removal list.

f) **B22d-SessionIndexEntryFromResult-signature** (~80 LoC):
   change SessionIndexEntryFromResult's RunOptions parameter to take
   the small subset of fields it actually uses (Harness, Model, Provider,
   Correlation, PromptSource, etc.) as discrete arguments or a small
   internal struct. Update session_index_test.go and the four
   service-run callers in the same commit.

g) **B22d-RunFunc-and-compare-internals** (~250 LoC):
   change RunFunc's signature, runCompareArmWith, defaultResolvePromptForCompare,
   the public RunCompareWith/RunQuorumWith/RunBenchmarkWith signatures
   and BenchmarkArmsToCompare's return shape. Update prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go,
   models_test.go, claude_stream_test.go in the same commit. After this
   step, compare_adapter.go has no RunOptions/CompareOptions/QuorumOptions
   remaining (AC items 2, 3 satisfied).

h) **B22d-service_run-public-API** (~150 LoC):
   delete RunViaService, RunViaServiceWith, runFixtureHarnessViaRunner and
   inline their bodies into the new RunWithConfigViaService internal helper.
   At this point all callers have already moved (steps b, c, d) so the
   deletion is mechanical. service_run.go has no RunOptions remaining
   (AC item 2 satisfied).

After all eight steps, the legacy types are unreachable from any caller,
runner.go's internal RunOptions usage can be retired in B22 final, and
the type definitions in types.go:36/81/171 can be deleted as planned.

The grade.go change in isolation (step a) is genuinely a 10-LoC win and
satisfies 3 of the 7 AC items (1, 4, 5) — but the bead's stated rule
"Do not commit partial, exploratory, or red code hoping the reviewer will
accept it" forbids landing it without also satisfying items 2, 3, 6, 7.
Per attempt-1's findings, the only path that satisfies *all* AC items in
*one commit* is the ~14,000 LoC change above, which both exceeds the
SD-024 size budget and is unworkable in a single execute-bead worker
window.

The right shape for this work is the eight-bead split above. Each step
is independently <500 LoC, satisfies SD-024 §Migration discipline, and
ends with a green tree, a passing build, and no runtime-lint violations
on its staged files.

.ddx/executions/20260428T025336-72807bcd/manifest.json:47:          "body": "Bead ddx-ade5ebb3 — agent: retire RunOptions + CompareOptions + QuorumOptions + legacy Run

STATUS: not attempted; scope-vs-budget mismatch between bead description
and actual codebase state. Request re-scoping into sub-beads (see below)
before another execution attempt.

================================================================
What is done
================================================================

Stage 2 dispatch-site migrations (beads c0ff5d3e, 4c31e465, 21f9e321,
ee2cd434) landed RunWithConfig / RunCompareWithConfigViaService /
RunQuorumWithConfigViaService alongside the legacy Run /
RunCompareViaService / RunQuorumViaService entry points. The CLI
dispatch sites (`ddx agent run`, `ddx agent compare`, `ddx agent
quorum`) now construct rcfg + AgentRunRuntime / CompareRuntime /
QuorumRuntime and dispatch through the new `*WithConfig*` paths.
A subset of agent test files were migrated by bead ee2cd434.

Stage 1 cleanup (bead edbfa046) removed ExecuteBeadLoopOptions and
the legacy `ExecuteBeadWorker.Run(opts)` method, renamed
`RunWithConfig` → `Run`. That cleanup touched 18 files and ~123
net-changed lines.

================================================================
Why this bead cannot land in one pass as currently scoped
================================================================

The bead description in-scopes only two files:

  - cli/internal/agent/types.go (delete RunOptions / CompareOptions /
    QuorumOptions structs)
  - cli/internal/agent/runner.go (delete legacy Run, rename
    RunWithConfig → Run)

The bead acceptance criteria, however, require `cd cli && go build
./...` and `cd cli && go test ./...` to pass. After the structs and
the legacy method are deleted, the tree does not compile. A grep
over the post-Stage-2 tree shows the deleted symbols are still
referenced in:

  RunOptions: 137 occurrences across 42 files
  CompareOptions: ~15 occurrences across compare_adapter.go,
    models.go, agent_compare_config_test.go, plus 4 testdata files
  QuorumOptions: ~5 occurrences across compare_adapter.go,
    agent_quorum_config_test.go, plus testdata

Stage 2's dispatch-site migrations only retired *construction* of
these structs at the four CLI entry points and a portion of
agent-package tests. The structs are still load-bearing for:

  - Internal helpers in cli/internal/agent: BuildArgs, resolveHarness,
    resolvePrompt, resolveModel, resolveTimeout, resolveWallClock,
    runVirtualFn, RunAgent, runScriptFn, runClaudeWithFallbackFn,
    SessionIndexEntryFromResult, BenchmarkArmsToCompare, and the
    entire compare_adapter.go internal pipeline (CompareOptions /
    QuorumOptions are used as the working struct shape inside
    RunCompare / RunQuorum, not just at the top of the public API).
  - The `runner.Run(RunOptions{...})` self-call inside
    runner.go::TestProviderConnectivity (line 976).
  - The `r.Run(RunOptions{...})` call inside grade.go::169.
  - The `agent.RunViaService` exported function and its internal
    callers (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1462, cli/internal/server/server.go
    at lines 1913 and 3786, cli/internal/exec/store.go:201).
  - The `Run(opts agent.RunOptions)` interface contract on
    cli/internal/exec/types.go:11 plus its three implementers
    (cmd/exec.go, internal/exec/store.go, exec_acceptance_test.go,
    workers_stop_propagation_test.go).
  - cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead_test.go's two test runners
    (fakeAgentRunner, modelPassthroughCapture).
  - cli/internal/agent/prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go's
    resolvePrompt / defaultResolvePromptForCompare /
    RunViaServiceWith calls (the bead 21 commit explicitly noted
    these were left out of scope).
  - cli/tools/lint/evidencelint/* — the testdata uses a *separate*
    stub `agent.RunOptions` in testdata/src/agent/agent.go that is
    independent of the production type, so testdata compiles fine,
    but analyzer.go's package-level doc comments and
    runOptionsTypeNames list reference the production type's name.

================================================================
Estimated true cost
================================================================

Stage 1's analogous cleanup (ExecuteBeadLoopOptions) was 18 files /
~123 net lines. Stage 2's RunOptions/CompareOptions/QuorumOptions
cleanup is ~3-4× larger by raw reference count, and ~2× larger
structurally because (a) RunOptions is used as the central internal
data carrier inside compare_adapter.go's full Run / RunCompare /
RunQuorum pipelines (not just at the dispatch entry), and (b)
external callers in three different packages (cmd/, internal/exec/,
internal/server/) each need their own rcfg + AgentRunRuntime
construction wired in, which means the `agent.RunViaService` /
`agent.RunCompareViaService` / `agent.RunQuorumViaService`
back-compat shims must stay or be replaced.

Conservative estimate: ~40 files / ~1500–2500 net lines / 3-5×
the per-bead size budget defined in SD-024 §"Per-bead size budget"
(target <500 LoC, <20 minutes, <$10 spend).

A no-change here is preferable to a half-finished commit that
leaves the tree red or smuggles a partial migration past review.

================================================================
What a follow-up attempt would need
================================================================

Recommend splitting into a fresh stage-2-cleanup sub-stage with
beads sized to the SD-024 budget. Suggested decomposition:

## What is done
